(dir. of guaranteed railways, India Office).—Joined the service, Dec., 1866, as asst. engr.; railway branch, Bengal, 1870; asst. consulting engr., Bengal, July, 1870; supt., Darjiling railway survey, Oct., 1870; Holkar state railway, Nov., 1870; exec. engr., March, 1871; dep. consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Calcutta, April, 1872; manager, Holkar state railway, Dec., 1875; on special duty, Madras famine relief works, 1877; served in the Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal); manager, Rajputana and Sindhia railway, Sept., 1879; agent, Bombay, Baroda, and Central India railway co., Jan., 1885; C.I.E., June, 1888; offg. dir.-gen. of railways, and dep. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., Dec., 1893; sec., public works dept., July, 1894; K.C.I.E., June, 1897; apptd. govt. dir. of Indian railway companies, India Office, Nov., 1897; ret'd. from the Army, Nov., 1898.

BLACK, HENRY SOMERS, commander, Royal Indian Marine.—Entered the Indian Marine, July, 1877; commander, May, 1886; port officer and marine transport officer, Rangoon, Nov., 1895 to Nov., 1900.

BLACKWOOD, JOHN ROSS, M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. chairman, Calcutta Corporation).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 21st Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. chairman, Calcutta Corporation, April, 1900.

BLAIR, HARRISON FALKNER, B.A., B.L. (judge, High Court, N.W. Prov.).—Educ. at Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; called to the bar (Inner Temple), June, 1864; apptd. puisne judge, high court, N.W. Prov., 1st April, 1892.

BLAKESLEY, EDMUND HOLMES, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Chatrherhouse and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 30th Oct., 1889, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; dep. commr. May, 1897.

BLANC, HENRI JULES, M.D., F.R.C.P., M.R.C.S. (Lon.). Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the dept., 10th Feb., 1859; arrived, 7th Aug., 1859, and served in the mil. dept. and also as acting civil surgn. in various stations, and as supt. of vaccn.; served in the China war, 1860-62; was a member of the Abyssinian mission, 1864, and was confined in chains during 21 months by the Emperor Theodore; subsequently joined the expedy. force, and was present at the storming of Magdala (medal); supt. of Yerrowda central jail, April, 1875; senr. surgn., Jamsetji Fijibhoj hosp., Bombay, and profir. Grant med. coll., May, 1876; physician, European gen. hosp., Aug., 1882; retd., April, 1887.

BLATHWAYT, CHARLES GEORGE, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 2nd Jan., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. collr. and mag., Kanara, Oct., 1882; senr. collr., March, 1886; retd., July, 1890.

BLENKINSOPE, EDWARD ROBERT KAYE, Indian C.S. (settl. offr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Christ Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 19th Oct., 1892, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; settl. officer, Dec., 1897.

BLENKINSOPE, FREDERICK HENRY, Lieut., Col., late Indian Med. Service, Madras Med. Dept.—Arrived, 7th May, 1869, and served with various regts. to Oct., 1888, including service in the Burmese expdn.; med. officer, Coonoor, May, 1889; civil surgn. and supt. of jail, Cuddapah, July, 1890; Trichinopoly, May, 1891; services lent to mil. dept., April, 1893; offg. principal med. officer, Madras dist., April, 1897; retd., Dec., 1898.

BLENNERHASSETT, ARTHUR CHARLES FRANCIS HENRY MARIA BERNARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 6th Dec., 1895, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

BLENNERHASSETT, WILLIAM, late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Wimborne Gr. Sch. and Eton; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 1st Nov., 1865, and served in Oudh as asst. commr.; from Feb., 1882, served in the jud. dept.; district and sessions judge, May, 1885; offg. judge, High Court, March, 1896; addl. judl. commr., Oudh, Jan., 1898; retd. Nov., 1900.

BLIGH, WILLIAM GEORGE, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., July, 1869, and employed on irrign. works in N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec.

engr., May, 1883; from Nov. 1889; served in Burma (medal with clasp) on the Mu valley railway and on irrign. works; retd. Aug., 1898.

BLISS, SIR HENRY WILLIAM, K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 15th Dec., 1863, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; on famine duty, 1877-78; from Feb., 1878, served as commr. of salt rev., and of abkari rev., and was apptd. customs collr. under the sea customs act, Aug., 1882; on duty to Bombay, 1879, and to Calcutta and N.W. Prov. and Oudh, 1880, to examine the working of the salt dept.; fellow of Madras univ., 1882; on duty to Orissa to report on the system of salt administration, Jan., 1884; addl. member of the board of rev. in addn. to his duties as commr. of salt rev., Feb., 1886; member of fin. committee under govt. of India, April, 1886; member of board of rev. and commr. of salt and abkari rev., April, 1887; C.I.E., May, 1889; member of legisl. council, 1890-91 and 1891-92; commr. of land rev., Madras, April, 1891; member of council, Madras, Nov., 1893 to Nov., 1898; acted as Govr. of Madras, March, 1896; K.C.I.E., June, 1897; retd., Nov., 1898.

BLISSETT, T., late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 1st March, 1859; asst. supt., 1st grade, Jan., 1866; supt., Arakan divn., Aug., 1869; personal asst. to dir.-gen., 1873-75; served subsequently in the Dacca and Sind divns., and in the store dept., and from 1882 in Bombay; 1st grade supt., Aug., 1883; retd., Jan., 1889.

BLOOD, BAGOT WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 7th Aug., 1866, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Sept., 1872; supt. of way and works, Rajputana state railway, Nov., 1879; suptg. engr., Dec., 1890; retd., May, 1893.

BLOOD, SIR BINDON, K.C.B., Maj.-Genl., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—Apptd. to Bombay public works dept., 13th March, 1871; transf. to Bengal sappers and miners, Sept., 1871; on famine duty, 1874; exec. engr., March, 1874; took part in Jowaki expdn., 1877-78 (medal with clasp); on special duty, Calcutta torpedo defence, April, 1878; served in S. African war (medal with clasp), 2nd Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); rejoined Indian establt., Feb., 1880, as offg. commandant, Bengal sappers and miners; took part in Egyptian expdn., 1882 (medal, star, and 4th class Osmanieh); supernumerary exec. engr., mil. works dept., Simla, April, 1885; second in command, Bengal sappers and miners, Aug., 1885; commandant, 1886-93; apptd. to mil. works dept. as suptg. engr., Sept., 1893; col. on staff, Rawal Pindi, Oct., 1894; chief staff officer, Chitral relief force, 1895; K.C.B., Jan., 1896; comdg. Bandelkhand dist., Jan., 1896; commanded the Malakand Field Force, July, 1897; comdg. Meerut divn., Sept., 1898.

BLOOMFIELD, ALFRED, Col., I.S.C., late Central Prov. Commn.—First commn., March, 1856; served in the Central Prov. from July, 1864, as asst. commr., asst. sec. to the chief commr., and dep. commr.; inspr.-gen. of registrn., supt. of stamps, and commr. of excise, Oct., 1876; reverted to mil. dept., March, 1892; author of 'Notes on the Baigas of the Central Prov.', 1885.

BLYTH, WILLIAM DUNBAR, M.A., LL.D., Indian C.S. (opium agent, Bihar).—Educ. at

Liverpool Coll. Instrn., and Queen's Coll., Cork; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 16th Nov., 1874; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and junr. sec., board of rev.; under sec., govt. of Bengal, finl. dept., April, 1882; inspr. of registrn. offices, Oct., 1886; inspr.-gen. of registrn., April, 1887; mag. and collr., Nov., 1890; opium agent, Bihar, March, 1900.

BOAS, HENRY JULIUS, Indian C.S. (joint mag. N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 19th Nov., 1891, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; joint mag., Oct. 1896.

BODDAM, HUNGERFORD TUDOR, B.L. (judge, High Court, Madras).—Apptd. judge of the High Court, Madras, July, 1896.

BODDAM, WELBY WROUGHTON, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Punjab.—First commn., 12th Dec., 1851; arrived, 2nd July, 1852; served with the mil. police, 1857-61; district supt. of Lahore police, Feb., 1861; dep. inspr.-gen., March, 1878; offg. inspr.-gen., Punjab, 1883 and 1885; reverted to mil. dept., March, 1888.

BODDY, OWEN VIDAL, Col., R.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Arrived, 11th Feb., 1873; asst. engr., April, 1873; exec. engr., March, 1883; employed in the mil. works branch, govt. of India, Aug., 1887, to Feb., 1888; dep. chief engr. and under sec. in the public works dept., Feb., 1891, to April, 1894; suptg. engr., April, 1896.

BODY, CHARLES STUART (2nd class clerk, India Audit Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 29th Jan., 1895.

BOILEAU, LESTOCK FRANCIS, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., 24th March, 1865, and served at Aden, and in Rajputana; exec. engr., March, 1871; transf. to N.W. Prov., and Oudh, May, 1873; returned to mil. dept., 1880; re-apptd. to public works dept., 1885, and served in N.W. Prov. and Oudh, and Rajputana; retired, 1st Oct., 1895.

BOLSTER, JOHN ABRAHAM (second class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd Feb., 1872.

BOLTON, CHARLES WALTER, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (member, board of revenue, Bengal).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., Royal Coll., Mauritius, and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 7th Oct., 1872; asst. mag. and collr. in Bengal, Oct., 1872; offg. pol. agent, Hill Tippera, Apr., 1877; under sec., govt. of Bengal, Mar. 1879; offg. under sec., govt. of India, home, rev., and agricl. dept., Feb., 1880; under sec. in the jud. &c., depts., govt. of Bengal, Feb., 1883; senr. marriage registrar, Calcutta, Nov., 1884; joint mag. and dep. collr., 1st grade, May, 1885; mag. and coll., Sept., 1888; sec. to the board of rev., Dec., 1891; acted in 1894 and 1895 as addl. commr., Patna, and opium agent, Bihar; sec. to govt., genl. rev. and statistics dept., April, 1896; chief sec., govt. of Bengal, Nov., 1896; C.S.I., June, 1897; member, board of rev., Nov., 1900.

BOLTON, HORATIO NORMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Rossall and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 9th Dec., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

BOMFORD, GERALD, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (princ., Med. Coll., Calcutta).

—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1874, and served in the mil. dept., and as profir. and rest. surgn. in the med. coll., Calcutta; supt., Calcutta lunatic asylum, April, 1890; sec. to the surgn.-gen. and sanitary commr., Aug., 1891; principal, med. coll., Calcutta, Nov., 1893.

BOMPAS, CECIL HENRY, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Westminster, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 5th Nov., 1889, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offd. as under sec. in 1893; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1896; mag. and collr., Aug., 1900.

BONAVIA, EMANUEL, M.D., Brigade-Surgn., late Med. Dept., N.W. Prov.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., 4th Aug., 1857; served under the mil. dept. up to Dec., 1861, and was present at the capture of Lucknow, March, 1858, and in the trans-Gogra campaign; asst. civil surgn., Lucknow, Dec., 1861; health officer, Lucknow, and supt. of museum, &c., April, 1867; principal of med. sch. at Balrampur, in addn. to other duties, Jan., 1873; offg. sanitary commr., Oudh, 1874 and 1877, also offg. as inspr.-gen. of prisons, Oudh; civil surgn., Etawah, Dec., 1881; ret'd., April, 1888; has publ. works on date culture in India, 'The Villager's Domestic Medicine,' 1885, and 'Studies in the Evolution of Animals,' 1895.

BONHAM-CARTER, NORMAN, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Rugby and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 28th Nov., 1888, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., fin. and municipal depts., Jan., 1894; transf. to gen., &c., depts., April, 1895; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1896; offg. under sec., May, 1898; mag. and collr., Oct., 1899.

BONUS, ARTHUR RIVERS, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 17th Jan., 1887, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; transf. to Burma as asst. commr., Aug., 1889; retransfd., March, 1892: 1st asst., and forest settlement officer, Feb., 1897.

BONUS, JOSEPH, Major-Gen., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Bombay engrs., June, 1855; arrived, March, 1857; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasp); joined the dept., June, 1859, and was employed on the Bombay harbour defences, Oct., 1860; from Sept., 1861, served as dep. consulting engr. for railways in Sind; offg. dep. consulting engr., N. W. Prov., March, 1870; from Nov., 1871, served as suptg. engr. on Indus valley state railway; engr.-in-chief, Sindia state railway, Sept., 1876; transf. to Punjab N. state railway, May, 1877; offg. consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Lahore, July-Oct., 1878; engr.-in-chief, Pindi-Peshawar section, Oct., 1879; medal for services in the Afghan war, 1879-80; acting consulting engr. for railways, and joint sec. to govt., Bombay, 1882 and 1885-86; senr. dep. consulting engr. and under sec. to govt., railway branch, from Nov., 1883; chief engr., Dec., 1885; ret'd., Nov., 1888.

BOOTH, WILLIAM, M.A. (dir. of pub. instrn., Assam).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; served from 27th Nov., 1876, as profir. in the presidency coll.; principal, Dacca coll., June, 1884; profir. of physics, presidency coll., Nov.,

1890; principal, Hugli coll., Dec., 1892; offg. dir. of public instrn., Assam, April, 1897; confd. March, 1899.

BOOTY, PERCY ABBEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and Trin. Coll. Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 16th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BORRAH, BOLI NARAYAN, Indian Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Jan., 1878, and posted to Assam; exec. engr., March, 1890.

BOSANQUET, OSWALD VIVIAN, Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept.).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 17th Sept., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. to rest. at Haidarabad, April, 1892; first asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Rajputana, Oct., 1893; acting under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., May, 1895, and Jan., 1898; confd., April, 1899.

BOSE, BHOOBUN MOHUN, RAI BAHADUR, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. from Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll., Sept., 1867, as apprentice engineer, and posted to Oudh; asst. engr., Sept., 1868; transfld. to State Rlwys., Dec., 1869; exec. engr., Oct., 1886; ret'd., 1898.

BOSWORTH-SMITH, BERTRAND NIGEL, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Harrow, Magdalen Coll., Oxford, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

BOTHAM, ARTHUR WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Newcastle - under - Lymne and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 21st Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

BOUGHEY, GEORGE FLETCHER OTTLEY, C.S.I., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., June, 1862; arrived, 5th Jan., 1865; served in the Bhotan expcdn., 1865-66 (medal and clasp); joined the dept., Nov., 1869, as asst. engr., railway branch, N.W. Prov.; exec. engr., Rajputana railway, Sept., 1870; dep. consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Lucknow, May, 1877; asst. sec., railway branch, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, 1879; served in the Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal); manager, Indus valley and Kandahar railway, Jan., 1882; manager, E. Bengal state railway, Nov., 1885; manager, N.W. railway, Oct., 1892; C.S.I., Jan., 1896; ret'd., Oct., 1897.

BOULNOIS, CHARLES, B.A., LL.B., B.L., late Punjab Chief Court.—Educ. at King's Coll., London, and London Univ.; called to the bar (Middle Temple), 30th April, 1855, and in the same year was admitted advocate of the Calcutta supreme court; prof. of law in the presidency coll., Calcutta, 1857-61 and 1882-83; judge of the court of small causes, Bengal, 1860; offg. sec. to govt. of Bengal, in the legisl. dept., 1862-64; offg. dep. sec. to the govt. of India, legisl. dept., 1864; apptd. judge of the chief court, Punjab, in Feb., 1866, and was sent. judge from 1867 until 1877; from 1881 has acted as reporter of appeal cases; fellow of the Punjab univ., 1882; editor of law reports in the supreme court, 1856-60; joint author of 'Notes on Customary Law,' by Boulnois and Rattigan, 1876; ret'd., 1877.

BOURDILLON, JAMES AUSTIN, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (chief sec. to govt., Bengal).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1870; asst. mag. and collr. in Bengal, Dec., 1870; offg. under sec., govt. of India, finl. dept., Sept., 1874, and in the home dept., March, 1876; inspr. of registrn. offices, Aug., 1876; dep. supt. of census, Dec., 1880; inspr.-gen. of registrn., Dec., 1881; m. gr. and collr., April, 1887; offg. sec., finl. and municipal depts., 1892 and 1893; member of Bengal legisl. council, 1893 and 1895; commr., Bardwan, Sept., 1895; C.S.I., Jan., 1898; chief sec. to govt., Nov., 1900.

BOURNE, ALFRED GIBBS, D.Sc., F.R.S., F.L.S. (prof'r., Presy. Coll., Madras).—Apptd. Prof. of Biology, Presy. Coll., Madras, Jan., 1886.

BOUSTEAD, ROBINSON, F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Major, late Bombay Med. Dept.—Civil surgn., Thana, 21st Jan., 1876; acted as supt. of vaccn., 1876; supt. of Matheran, Feb., 1882; acted as surgn., Gokuldas Tejpal hosp., 1883; civil surgn., Ahmadnagar, July, 1887; ret'd., Feb., 1889.

BOUTFLOWER, WILLIAM NOLAN, B.A. (inspr. of schools, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the dept., 9th Aug., 1869, as prof'r. in the Agra coll.; prof'r. in Muir coll., Allahabad, Oct., 1874; offg. meteorological reporter to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, April-June, 1881; inspr. of schools, Feb., 1892; offd. as dir. of public instrn. from April, 1896; author of 'Elementary Statics and Dynamics,' and 'The Indian Arithmetic.'

BOVILL, EDWARD, M.B., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (civil surgeon, Howrah).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1872, and served on gen., jail, and hosp. duty and as civil surgn. at various stations, from Dec., 1885.

BOWDEN, WILLIAM JAMES (Finl. Dept., India Office).—Apptd. supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893; clerk, 2nd class, 28th Aug., 1895; asst. to the India Office member of the Committee for valuation of military equipment, Nov., 1897.

BOWEN, CHARLES, Col., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—First commn., Dec., 1855; arrived, April, 1863; joined the dept., Nov., 1863, and, from June, 1864, served under the Mysore govt.; exec. engr., March, 1867; asst. to chief engr., and sec. to chief commr., Mysore, June, 1869; offg. engr.-in-chief and joint sec. to govt., 1880; served in Madras, April, 1883, to Sept., 1885, as dep. chief engr. and under sec. to govt.; served again under the Mysore govt. from Sept., 1885, to July, 1892; suptg. engr., April, 1886; ret'd., Feb., 1894.

BOWER, GEORGE, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Caistor Gr. Sch., Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 10th Dec., 1884, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and offg. under sec. to govt.; joint mag., March, 1890; dep. supt., family domains of the Maharaja of Benares, April, 1890, to April, 1893; dep. commr., April, 1896; mag. and collr., March, 1897.

BOWIE, MATHEW MORTON, Col., I.S.C., Arrived, 20th June, 1888; from June, 1863, served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and dep. commr., and was placed on special duty in connection with the Delhi assemblage, Nov., 1876; commr. of excise, &c., June, 1880; dep. commr., Nagpur, Sept., 1880; inspr.-gen. of police and prisons, Central Prov., April,

1882; commr., Nerbudda divn., April, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., June, 1896.

BOWRING, LEWIN BENTHAM, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Mount Radford Sch., Exeter, and at Leipzig; apptd. from Haileybury, 1843; asst. mag. and collr., Bengal, 1845; asst. to treasurer, Calcutta, 1846; asst. to rest. at Lahore, 1847; dep. commr., Punjab, 1849-54; private sec. to Lord Canning, 1858; chief commr. of Mysore and Coorg, 1862; ret'd., 1870; has publ'd. 'Eastern Experiences,' 'Haidar Ali and Tipu Sultan,' &c.

BOYCE, HENRY GEORGE, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; appointed Oct., 1877, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1889.

BOYD, CHARLES CLIFFORD, Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Feb., 1897.

BOYD, FREDERICK KENNEDY, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Haileybury, and at King's and Univ. Colls., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1896, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

BOYD, HENRY WALKER BUTLER, F.R.C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (physician, St. George's Hosp., Bombay).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1876; served as civil surgeon at various stations; supt. of lunatic asylum, Colaba, June, 1890; physician, St. George's Hosp., Bombay, April, 1898.

BOYD, JOHN EWART EDWARDS AGINCOURT, late Madras Police.—Joined the service, Nov., 1869, and served in the police dept. as inspr., asst. supt., and dist. supt.; ret'd., 1900.

BOYS, HENRY SCOTT, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Harrow, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 30th Oct., 1863; from Dec., 1864, served in Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. settl. officer, and was apptd. supt. of the encumbered estates of Sitapur and Kheri, June, 1872; dep. commr., Dec., 1881; mag. and collr., Nov., 1884; acted as commr. in 1883, 1837, and 1888; ret'd., 1889.

BRACKENBURY, SIR HENRY, K.C.B., K.C.S.I., Lieut.-Gen., R.A. (dir.-gen. of ordnance, War Office).—First commn., 7th April, 1856; served in the Mutiny, 1858, the Ashanti expedn., 1873, and the S. African war, 1879-80; asst. adjutant and quarter-master-gen., Cyprus, July, 1878; mil. sec., S. Africa, May, 1879; private sec. to viceroy of India, March to June, 1880; mil. attaché, Paris, Jan., 1881; dep. adjutant and quarter-master-gen., Egypt, Sept., 1884; brigadier-gen., Egypt, Jan., 1885; dep. quarter-master-gen. at head-quarters (intelligence branch), Jan., 1886; dep. adjutant-gen., and director of mil. intelligence, June, 1887, to March, 1891; mil. member of viceroy's council, April, 1891, to April, 1896; prest. ordnance commn., War Office, May, 1896; dir.-genl. of ordnance, War Office, Feb., 1899; K.C.B., May, 1894; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1896.

BRACKENBURY, MAULE CAMPBELL, C.S.I., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., June, 1865; arrived and joined the dept., June, 1875; exec. engr., March, 1877; served in the Perak expedn., 1875-76 (medal and clasp), and the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal

and two clasps and bronze star); offg. dep. consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Lahore, 1881; asst. manager, Punjab N. state railway, Feb., 1883; offg. manager and traffic supt., Mysore railway, April, 1883; acting dep. consulting engr., Bombay, Oct., 1884; dep. manager, N.W. state railway, Sind section, Dec., 1885; manager, Sind section, March, 1887; under sec. to govt. of India, railway branch, Nov., 1887, and dep. dir.-gen. of railways, April, 1888, to July, 1890; manager, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, May, 1892; manager, N.W. railway, April, 1896; C.S.I., May, 1898; ret'd., Sept., 1899.

BRADDON, JOHN BLOOMFIELD, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 12th Sept., 1871, and was employed on various public works and railway accts.; examr., Feb., 1884; offg. dep. acctnt.-gen. for inspn. duty, March, 1895; govt. examr. of accts., Bengal and N.W. railway, Nov., 1895; ret'd., Oct., 1899.

BRADFORD, SIR EDWARD RIDLEY COLBORNE, K.C.B., K.C.S.I., Col., I.S.C. (chief commr., Metrop. Police).—Cornet, 6th light cavalry, Madras, Feb., 1854; proceeded to Persia under Gen. Jacob in 1856; pol. asst. in W. Malwa, and in command of 1st regt. Cealntr India horse, Nov., 1860; pol. agent, Bakhelkhand, 1871; pol. agent, Bhartpur, Jan., 1873; gen. supt. of operations for suppression of thagi and dakaiti, May, 1874; agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, Dec., 1878; also chief commr. of Ajmir; sec., pol. and secret dept., India office, Feb., 1887; accompanied H.R.H. Prince Albert Victor on his visit to India in 1889-90; K.C.B., May, 1890; resigned on his appt. as chief commr., metropolitan police, London, June, 1890.

BRADLEY, HERBERT, B.L., Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Winchester Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1878, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sec. to the commr. of salt, abkari and separate rev., April, 1888; collr. and mag., July, 1894.

BRADLEY, RICHARD, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept., 1st Nov., 1868, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; ret'd., 1896.

BRADLEY-BIRT, FRANCIS BRADLEY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 28th Nov., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

BRAIDWOOD, HAROLD LITHGOW, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BRANDIS, SIR DIETRICH, K.C.I.E., Ph.D..—Educ. at Univs. of Copenhagen, Gottingen, and Bonn; lecturer on botany at Univ. of Bonn, 1849; served in the India forest dept. from 16th Jan., 1856, and was apptd. inspr.-gen. of forests, April, 1864; C.I.E., Jan., 1878; ret'd., 1883; apptd. a member of the board of visitors of Cooper's Hill Coll., 1886; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887; author of 'Forest Flora of N.W. and Central India,' 1874, of the article on 'Teak' in the Encyclopædia Britannica, &c., &c.; is dir. of the

practical course of forestry on the Continent in connection with Cooper's Hill Coll.

BRANDRETH, ASHTON MILNES, Col., R.E., late Public Works Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—First commn., Dec., 1855; arrived, 30th Dec., 1857; served in the public works dept. from 8th Jan., 1858, as asst. engr., asst. principal, Thomason coll., and exec. engr.; transfld. to the Punjab, Sept., 1861, and served on the W. Jumna canal; asst. sec., govt. of India, May to Dec., 1863; asst. sec., public works dept., Punjab, May, 1867; principal, Thomason coll., 1879-91; ret'd., March, 1892.

BRANDT, FRANCIS, M.A., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., under sec. to govt., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; acting collr. of sea customs, Madras, April, 1874; collr. and mag., S. Kanara, Oct., 1877, and in the Nilgiris, 1883; district and sessions judge, Trichinopoli, June, 1878; judge of the high court, Madras, Sept., 1884; fellow of Madras univ., 1886; ret'd., Dec., 1887.

BRANFOOT, ARTHUR MUDGE, C.I.E., M.B., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Col., Indian Med. Service (principal med. officer, Rangoon dist.).—Asst. surgn., 30th March, 1872; arrived, 28th Dec., 1872, and served under the mil. dept. till March, 1873, and subsequently as civil surgn. at Coconada and Madras, rest. surgn. at the gen. hosp., Madras, and profir. in the med. coll.; asst. physician, gen. hosp., and profir. of hygiene, med. coll., July, 1878; supt. of the govt. maternity hosp., and profir. of midwifery, med. coll., July, 1881; fellow of Madras univ. 1886; principal med. officer, Rangoon district, March, 1898; C.I.E., May, 1898.

BRANSON, JAMES CHARLES EVERTON, Indian C.S. (acctnt.-gen., Bengal).—Educ. at Harrow, and Balliol College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 16th Jan., 1882, and served in Oudh as asst. commr.; transfld. to Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., Dec., 1885; offg. asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, March, 1886; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, April, 1889; acting acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, April, 1892; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1892; returned to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1894; dep. commr., Oct. 1894; mag. and collr., Feb., 1896; retrnsf. to Bombay as acctnt.-gen., Aug., 1898; acct.-genl., Bengal, Nov., 1899.

BRASHER, ALFRED HERBERT, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Uppingham and Tonbridge Schs., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 7th Dec., 1899; and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

BRAY, DENYS DE SAUMAREZ, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 21st Nov., 1899, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

BRENDON, BENJAMIN ADAMS, Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. on the Continent and at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 6th Nov., 1892, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., July, 1898.

BRERETON, ALFRED, India Public Works Dept. (dir. of railway traffic).—Joined the dept. as asst. engr., March, 1873, and served in the

railway branch; exec. engr., April, 1880; asst. dir. N.W. railway, Nov., 1887; district traffic supt., Nov., 1888; dep. manager, July, 1891; offg. manager, E. Bengal railway, March, 1893; manager and engr.-in-chief, E. Coast railway, Nov., 1893; dir. of railway traffic and dep. sec. to govt. of India, Nov., 1897.

BRERETON, HERBERT JAMES, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (dep. acct. gen., Bengal).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch.; joined the service, 7th April, 1873; from Feb., 1874, served in the office of the acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov.; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, July, 1877; N.W. Prov., Nov., 1877; Burma, April, 1878; Punjab, Aug., 1879; N.W. Prov., March, 1884; Central Prov., March, 1888; N.W. Prov., July, 1888; comptr., Assam, June, 1890; asst. comptr.-gen., India (in charge of paper currency), July, 1893; dept. acctnt.-gen., March, 1896; comptr., Cent. Prov., April, 1896; dep. acct. gen., Bengal, Dec., 1898.

BRERETON, REGINALD HUGH, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 14th Dec., 1882, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1889; asst. commr., Oudh, Nov., 1891; joint mag., 1st grade, June, 1892; dep. commr., Nov., 1895; mag. and collr., April, 1896.

BRETT, CECIL MICHAEL WILFORD, B.L., Indian C.S. (judge, high court, Calcutta).—Educ. at Guildford Gr. Sch. and Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 30th Nov., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1885; registrar, high court (appellate side), April, 1886; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1890; offg. jud. commr., Chota Nagpur, April-Sept., 1891; judge, Calcutta high court, June, 1900.

BRIDGES, ALBERT, Indian C.S. (dist. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 29th Nov., 1887, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dist. judge, Jan., 1899.

BRIDGES, JAMES EMILE, B.L., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma).—Educ. at Royal Coll., Tournai, and Univ. of Bonn; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 16th Nov., 1876, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settl. officer; offg. sec. to chief commr., land rev. and agricl. dept., and offg. dir. of agric., Nov., 1884; dep. commr., Aug., 1886, and Dec., 1888; offg. chief sec., March-July, 1890; commr., March, 1891.

BRIGGS, HERBERT SAMUEL, Store Dept., India Office (second class clerk).—Apptd. clerk, second class, 25th June, 1900.

BRIGGS, WILLIAM HAMILTON, Lieut.-Col., late Army Med. Staff.—First commn., 30th Sept., 1875; served in the Jowaki campaign, 1877-78 (medal and clasp), the Afghan campaigns, 1878-80 (medal), and the Nile campaign, 1884-85 (medal and clasps and Khedive's star); med. officer on personal staff of govt. of Madras, Dec., 1886 to March, 1889; ret'd. from the army, Dec., 1893.

BRIGHT, WILLIAM ROBERT, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (chairman, Calcutta corporation).—Educ. at Winchester, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 13th Dec., 1880, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1890; dep. commr., Palamau, March, 1892; mag. of Midna-

pur, Dec., 1895; chairman, Calcutta corporation, Dec., 1897; C.S.I., Jan., 1901.

BRIGSTOCKE, ARTHUR MONTAGUE, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Heidelberg Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 15th Dec., 1892, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

BRIND, MICHAEL JOSEPH (chief supt., India Telegraph Dept.).—Apptd. asst. supt., 9th Nov., 1870; served in the construction of several lines in the Punjab, Assam, &c.; supt., April, 1891; chief supt., March, 1900.

BROADBENT, JOHN EDWARD, Colonel, R.E., India Mil. Works Services (chief engr.).—First commn., 17th July, 1866; arrived, Dec., 1868, and served as asst. engr.; exec. eng., June, 1879; asst. sec., mil. dept., Dec., 1882; second dep. sec., March, 1890; dep. dir.-gen., mil. works, for fortifications, April, 1892; transfed. to Bombay command as chief engr., April, 1894; transfed. to Punjab, March, 1895.

BROADFOOT, RAMSAY DOUGLAS, B.L., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Kensington Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 27th Dec., 1880, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Nov., 1888; sub-collr. and joint mag., Feb., 1893; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1896.

BROADWAY, PHILIP NEVILLE, late Punjab Police.—Joined the service, 27th Feb., 1861, as asst. district supt. of police; district supt., April, 1873; retired, 1898.

BRODIE, NORMAN SOMERVILLE, B.A., Indian C.S. (sec. to commrs. of salt rev., Madras).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 12th Dec., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and acting asst. sec. to govt.; head asst., Malabar, Feb., 1893; dep. commr. of salt, &c., rev., April, 1896; sec. to commrs. of salt, &c., rev., Nov., 1897.

BRODIE, VERNOR ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 6th Jan., 1878, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag. and forest settlt. officer; senr. asst., Vizagapatam, Feb., 1885; sub-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1891; district and sessions judge, March, 1894; collr. and mag., March, 1895.

BRODIE, WILLIAM PATRICK, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (supg. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1872, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., July, 1881; suptg. engr., April, 1899.

BROJENDRA COOMAR SEAL, B.L., late Statutory C.S.—From 23rd Oct., 1865, served in the subord. jud. dept., Bengal, and was appointed subord. judge, 4th grade, May, 1873, and district judge, Bankura, and asst. sessions judge, Bardwan, April, 1881; district and sessions judge, June, 1887; retired, Jan. 1900.

BROJENDRA NATH DE, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Hare Sch., Calcutta, Canning Coll., Lucknow, Calcutta Univ., and St. Mary's Hall, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 6th Sept., 1875, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1886; mag. and collr., Aug., 1891.

BROOK-FOX, FREDERICK GEORGE, M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., India Public Works Dept. (supt. of

works).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept., Sept., 1873, and employed on mil. works in the Punjab; transfed. temporarily to Madras on famine relief work, 1877-78; employed in Burma, 1880-85; exec. engr., May, 1883; transfed. temporarily to state railways, April, 1885; and permanently, Oct., 1892; supt. of works, April, 1899.

BROOKE, THOMAS HENRY BARSTOW, Col., I.S.C., late Central Prov. Commn.—First commn., April, 1855; arrived, 13th May, 1855, and was employed under the mil. dept. up to 1864, including service in the China expdn., 1857-59; from Feb., 1864, served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; cantonmt. mag., Saugor, Nov., 1874; dep. commr., March, 1883; reverted to mil. duty, 1889.

BROOKE, SIR WILLIAM ROBERT, K.C.I.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 13th Sept., 1857; supt., Bombay, Jan., 1866; served in the Punjab, 1869-71; 1st grade supt., Aug., 1877, offg. as dir. of traffic; dir., construction branch, June, 1883; dep. dir.-gen., July, 1889; dir.-gen. of telegraphs, April, 1890; C.I.E., Jan., 1894; retd., April, 1895; K.C.I.E., May, 1895.

BROOKE, WILLIAM SAURIN, Col., I.S.C., late Central Prov. Commn.—First commn., Dec., 1858; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1859; served in the Central Prov., from Jan., 1863, as asst. commr., &c.; cantonmt. mag., Jabalpur, May, 1869; dep. commr., Feb., 1873; acting inspr.-gen. of police and jails, Aug.-Nov., 1883; reverted to mil. dept., May, 1893.

BROUGHTON, LEWIS PRICE DELVES, B.L. (Administrator-General).—Apptd. administrator-general to the govt. of India, April, 1870.

BROUN, JOHN ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at foreign schs., Univ. Coll., London, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1879, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr. and cantonmt. mag.; joint mag., April, 1888; dep. commr., April, 1894; mag. and collr., Feb., 1896.

BROWN, ALAN DANIEL, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Highgate and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 26th Nov., 1898, and served in Bombay as asst. coll. and mag.

BROWN, CHARLES (dep. inspr.-gen., Punjab Police).—Joined the service, 8th Feb., 1868; asst. district supt. of police, Nov., 1868; district supt., April, 1876; served in Afghanistan, 1879; in charge of Gen. Daud Shah in 1880; rejoined the dept., Feb., 1882, and served as personal asst. and as asst. to the inspr.-gen.; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1894.

BROWN, CHARLES DALLAS, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1887, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; retd., June, 1895.

BROWN, FREDERICK LATHAM, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service as asst. engr., 15th July, 1869; transfed. to superior accts. establt., April, 1878; examr., state railway accts., Lower Burma, Nov., 1885; offg. engr.-in-chief and manager, Burma state railway, 1885 and 1886; examr., telegraph accts., March, 1889; dep. acctnt.-gen. for inspn. duty, Nov., 1891; retd., April, 1893.

BROWN, JAMES SAMUEL, M.I.C.E., India

Public Works Dept. (suplg. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Feb., 1885; offg. dep. manager, N.W. railway, 1889-90; offg. engr.-in-chief, Sept., 1895; suptg. engr., April, 1890.

BROWN, RICHARD CECIL, Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at the Clergy Orphan Sch., Canterbury, and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 1st Dec., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settl. officer; 2nd asst., Sept., 1896.

BROWN, THOMAS EDWIN BURTON, C.I.E., M.D., Brigade-Surgn., late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the dept. in 1858; from Nov., 1860, served as profir. and principal, med. sch., Lahore; also offd. as civil surgn. of Lahore on several occasions, and as meteorological reporter, 1879; ret'd., Oct., 1889; C.I.E., Jan., 1891; author of a work on the symptoms and treatment of the poisons used in the Punjab.

BROWN, WILLIAM BARCLAY, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Ayr Acad., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 8th Dec., 1886, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Assam as asst. commr., June, 1889; returned to Bengal as offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1894; mag. and collr., Oct., 1896; district and sessions judge, June, 1897; author of a grammar of the Diori-Chutiya language.

BROWNE, LORD HENRY ULICK, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, Dec., 1850; arrived, 1st Sept., 1851; served in rev. and jud. dept., and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1st grade, March, 1866; also served as private sec. to 'the prest. of the govr.-gen.'s council, Feb., 1858, under sec. to the govt. of Bengal, July, 1859, offg. under sec. to the govt. of India, home and finl. dept., April, 1860, registrar of the Sadar Diwani and Nizamat Adalat, April, 1861, supt. of stamps and stationery, Oct., 1865, and offg. addl. judge, Jessur and Nuddea, Feb., 1867; commr., Dec., 1869; chairman of the justices of the peace, Calcutta, Feb., 1872, and offg. member, board of rev., June, 1878; ret'd., Oct., 1886.

BROWNE, VEN. JAMES FRANCIS, B.D., late Ecclesl. Dept., Madras.—Joined the service as junr. chaplain, Madras, 20th July, 1870; senr. chaplain, July, 1880; arch-deacon of the diocese of Madras and commissary to the lord bishop, Feb., 1884; senr. joint chaplain of St. George's Cathedral, Feb., 1884; fellow of Madras univ., 1886; ret'd., April, 1893.

BROWNE, SAMUEL HASLETT, C.I.E., M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (principal, Med. Coll., Lahore).—Joined the service, March, 1874, and arrived, 14th Nov., 1874; served on hosp. duty and on mil. duty in Afghanistan in 1878; from Aug., 1879, served in the Central Prov. on gen. and jail duty; served under the govt. of India from Dec., 1886, in joint med. charge, Simla; principal of med. coll., Lahore, Oct., 1889; offg. surgn. supt., presidency gen. hosp., Calcutta, April-Nov., 1893; hon. surgn. to the viceroy, Jan., 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1896.

BROWNE, WILLIS, F.I.A. (actuary, India Office).—Writer, 15th Oct., 1867; clerk, 2nd class, Aug., 1878; actuarial clerk, Feb., 1882; fellow of inst. of actuaries, 1887; actuary, funds dept., Dec., 1889; actuary to India office, Oct., 1892.

BROWNE, WILLIAM RICHARD, M.D., Ch.M., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (principal, Madras Med. Coll.).—Joined the service, April, 1873; arrived, 1st Nov., 1873, and served on mil. and civil and jail duty in Madras; rest. surgn. in gen. hosp., and profir. of pathology, med. coll., Nov., 1883; surgn. in gen. hosp., and profir. of surgery, med. coll., May, 1890; fellow of Madras univ., 1892; field service in Rumpi hill tracts and Jaipur territory in 1879-80; principl. and profir. of medicine, med. coll. April, 1896.

BROWNING, COLIN ARROTT ROBERTSON, C.I.E., M.A., late Educl. Dept., Central Prov.—Senr. inspr. of schools, N.W. Prov., 26th June, 1862; acted as dir. of public instrn., Central Prov., 1864-65, and as inspr.-gen. of educn., Central Prov., 1868, and 1869-70; dir. of public instrn. in Oudh, July, 1870; inspr.-gen. of educn., Central Prov., Oct., 1873; C.I.E., Jan., 1886; ret'd., Jan., 1889; author of 'The Indian Official Thesaurus,' 'The Annals of the Indian Empire,' 'Mahomedan Education in Oudh,' 'A List of Sanscrit Manuscripts existing in Oudh,' &c.; has edited several school and text books, and was joint editor of the 1st edition of the Central Prov. Gazetteer.

BROWNING, HERBERT ARROTT, Capt., I.S.C. (dep. commr., Burma).—First commn., 21st Jan., 1881; employed in Burma as asst. commr., Sept., 1886; dep. commr., April, 1897.

BROWNING, WINTHROPP BENJAMIN, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (surgn., 2nd dist., Madras).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1880; arrived, 18th Sept., 1880, and served in Madras under the mil. dept. and as acting civil surgn.; district surgn. and jail supt., Madura, Sept., 1890; surgn. to the govr., from May, 1892; surgn., and district, Madras, May, 1895; C.I.E., Dec., 1898.

BROWNLOW, HENRY ALEXANDER, Lieut.-Gen., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., Bengal engrs., Dec., 1849; dep. supt., Ganges canal, Feb., 1852; and afterwards on the E. and W. Jumna canals; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasp); suptg. engr., N.W. Prov., irrign., March, 1865; offg. chief engr. and joint sec., irrign., N.W. Prov., Feb., 1872; suptg. engr., Bari Doab, Punjab, April, 1874; acted as chief engr. and joint sec., Punjab, Feb.-May, 1876, and as chief engr. and sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., irrign., June, 1876; offg. inspr.-gen. of irrign. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, April, 1877; chief engr. and sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, July, 1878; inspr.-gen. of irrign. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, Feb., 1882; ret'd., Nov., 1886.

BROWNRIGG, FRANCIS WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (sec. rev. board, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Rossall, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 31st Dec., 1881, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. settlt. officer, Nov., 1888; joint mag., April, 1890; settlt. officer, Sultanpur, Nov., 1893; dep. commr., Jan., 1895; mag. and collr., March, 1896; sec. rev. board, March, 1899.

BRUCE, ALEXANDER BANNERMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Glasgow Acad., Chanowry Sch., Aberdeen, Milne's Instn., Fochabers, and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 11th Nov., 1889, and served in the

N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1893; dep. commr., Oct., 1899.

BRUCE, RICHARD ISAAC, C.I.E., late Punjab Commn.—Served in the Punjab as extra asst. commr. from May, 1863; on special duty in connection with Major Sandeman's 2nd Kalat mission, 1876; 1st asst. to agent to govr.—gen., Baluchistan, Feb., 1877; acted as agent, in 1878, 1882 and 1885; pol. agent at Thal-Chotiali from 1882; rejoined Punjab commn. and was apptd. dep. commr., May, 1888; commr. and supt., Derajat, Feb., 1893; on special duty as British joint commr. for demarcation of Afghan-Waziri boundary, Oct., 1894; ret'd., April, 1896; author of a gazetteer of Dera Ghazi Khan district, a manual of the Baluch language, and a history of the Marri-Baluchi tribe.

BRUERE, ROBERT SALE ELPHINSTONE RICHARD, late Punjab Police.—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., June, 1862; dist. supt., June, 1873; ret'd., 1900.

BRUNYATE, JAMES BENNETT, Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt. of India, Fin. Dept.).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 9th Dec., 1891, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settl. officer, and asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., June, 1896; under sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., Nov., 1897.

BRYANT, JOHN FORBES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Weymouth Coll., and Clare Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 16th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BUCK, SIR EDWARD CHARLES, K.C.S.I., B.L., LL.D., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived 2nd Feb., 1862; from May, 1863, served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., settl. officer, and offg. sec., board of rev.; dir. of agric. and commerce, N.W. Prov., Feb., 1875; commr. for the govt. of India at the Melbourne exhibn. of 1880; sec. to the govt. of India in the rev. and agric. dept., April, 1882; on special duty from Sept., 1895, to June, 1896, regarding proposals for agricultural improvements; ret'd., Dec., 1896; Kt., Aug., 1886; C.S.I., May, 1892; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1897.

BUCKLAND, CHARLES EDWARD, C.I.E., B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal).—Educ. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 29th Nov., 1870; asst. mag. and collr. in Bengal, Dec., 1870; attaché, foreign dept., Nov., 1871; on famine duty, March, 1874; private sec. to lieut.-govr., 1874-77; asst. sec., govt. of Bengal, Aug., 1876; private sec. to Sir R. Temple during his mission to Madras and Bombay, 1877; private sec. to govr. of Bombay, May, 1877; offg. under sec., govt. of India, home dept., May, 1878; private sec. to prest. of govr.-gen.'s council, Nov., 1878; offg. press commr. with govt. of India, Feb., 1879; supt. of stamps and stationery, Bengal, May, 1880; junr. sec., board of rev., May, 1887; sec., May, 1890; sec. to govt. of Bengal, gen., rev. and statistical depts., Dec., 1891; member of Bengal legisl. council, Dec., 1893; C.I.E., May, 1895; commr. Burdwan divn., April, 1896; edited 'Excise Manual,' &c.

BUCKLAND, HENRY HARVEY, Indian Public Works Dept. (dist. locom. supt.).—Apptd. to state railways, locomotive dept., July, 1887; dist. locom. supt., Nov., 1888.

BUCKLE, HENRY (addl. sess. judge, Burma).—Served in the Burma commn. from 7th Aug., 1867, as asst. commr., and was in charge of the town mag.'s office, Akyab, 1871-74, and of the hill tracts of N. Arakan, 1874-76; in command of an expdn. against the Shandus, Nov., 1876; from June, 1878, served as dep. commr. in various districts; prest., Henzada municipal committee Dec., 1884; offg. commr., 1888, 1890, 1892; addl. sessions judge, Pegu and Irawadi, Jan., 1891; ditto, Pegu Divn., Nov., 1896.

BUCKLEY, LLEWELLYN EDDISON, Indian C.S. (head asst. Madras).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and at New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 19th Sept., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; acted as rev. under sec., 1894; head asst., Dec., 1894; senr. asst. dist. mag., and agent to govr., Nov., 1895; sub. collr. and joint mag., May, 1899; head asst., Nov., 1899.

BUCKLEY, ROBERT BURTON, M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Joined the dept. as asst. engr., July, 1869; exec. engr., March, 1876; services lent to Calcutta corporation, June, 1881; offg. under sec., public works dept., Bengal, July, 1886, and again, Feb., 1887; under sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., April, 1887, to Dec., 1889; suptg. engr., March, 1892; chief engr., March, 1898; member of council of lieut.-govr. of Bengal; author of 'Irrigation Works in India,' 1893.

BULL, CHARLES ALBERT, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept. as exec. engr., Nov., 1868, and served in the railway branch; engr.-in-chief, Madras railway surveys, Nov., 1884, and Cuddapah-Nellore railway, Sept., 1886; also in charge of Madras state railway surveys, July, 1887; supt. of way and works, Cuddapah-Nellore railway, Sept., 1887; supt. of works, Burma state railway, Sept., 1891; rank of suptg. engr., June, 1892; ret'd., Nov., 1896.

BULLOCK, FREDERICK D'OLBERT, LL.B., B.L., late Indian C.S., Punjab.—Educ. at Kingstown Gr. Sch., St. Aubin's Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 11th Jan., 1865, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause court, Delhi, Jan., 1873; jud. asst., Dec., 1877; district judge, Nov., 1884; divl. judge, Oct., 1885; offg. judge, chief court, Punjab, in 1892 and 1893; ret'd., June, 1894.

BULLOCK, FREDERICK SHORE, C.I.E., late Indian C.S., Berar.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 7th Nov., 1870, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as mag. and collr., joint mag., asst. supt. of Dehra Dun, and small cause court judge; mag. and collr., April, 1889; jud. commr., Berar, April, 1892; commr., Hyderabad assigned dists., Oct., 1895; C.I.E., June, 1897; ret'd., April, 1899.

BULLOCK, ROBERT, Col., I.S.C., late Berar Commn.—First commn., Madras infantry, 11th Dec., 1858; from July, 1862, served in the Berar commn., as asst. commr., offg. asst. rest., and dep. commr. for demarcation of the Dhanga frontier, 1886; jud. commr., Aug., 1888; civil and sessions judge, July, 1889; ret'd., April, 1891.

BULMAN, ALBERT ROBINSON, late Indian C.S., Punjab.—Educ. at Richmond Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and

served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and offg. sec. to finl. commr.; jud. asst., March, 1878; dep. commr., Dec., 1882; offg. commr. and supt., Lahore, Aug., 1884; and Ambala, Jan., 1887; ret'd., April, 1889.

BUNBURY, CECIL EDWARD FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 21st Nov., 1885, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr., July, 1896.

BURBRIDGE, DAVID JOHN, late Finl. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service, Feb., 1868; served in Fin. Dept., Burma, as asst. comptr., Feb., 1890; asst. acct.-gen., Bombay, Jan., 1893; ret'd., Jan., 1899; author of "Guide for investors in and holders of government securities."

BURGESS, JAMES, C.I.E., LL.D., Hon. A.R.I.B.A., M. Soc. As. Paris, late Archaeological Survey of India.—Educ. at Dumfries, Glasgow, and Edinburgh; apptd. archaeological surveyor and reporter for W. India, 15th Jan., 1874; also archaeological surveyor for S. India, Nov., 1881; dir.-gen. of the archaeological survey of India, March, 1886; fellow of the univ. of Bombay; ret'd., 1889; in 1894, attended the Geneva oriental congress as representative of India; editor of 'The Indian Antiquary,' 1872-84; author of 'Views of Architecture and Scenery in Gujarat and Rajputana,' 'The Temples of Satrunjaya,' 'Somanath, Girnar, &c.,' 'Reports of the Archaeological Survey of W. and S. India,' 'Notes on the Rock Temples of Ajanta, their Paintings and Sculptures,' 'Elephanta,' 'Epigraphia Indica,' &c., and, conjointly with the late J. Ferguson, 'The Rock Temples of India.'

BURKE, CHARLES TOLER, B.E., M.I.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1868, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., March, 1876; under sec., May, 1892; chairman, Bombay Port Trust, May, 1898; addl. member of govrs. council; suptg. engr., July, 1898; author of articles on various waterworks schemes; ret'd. Sept., 1900.

BURKE, JOHN, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the dept., 2nd June, 1864; supt., Aug., 1883; offg. dir. of traffic, Feb.-June, 1886; has held charge of various divns.; ret'd., 1898.

BURKITT, WILLIAM JOHN DWYER, Indian C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Marlborough and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 1st Nov., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. and mag. collr., and asst. settlt. officer; joint mag. and settlt. officer, March, 1900.

BURKITT, WILLIAM ROBERT, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (judge, High Court, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to the bar at the Middle Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 11th Oct., 1861; from Oct., 1862, served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and mag. and collr.; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1887; addl. jud. commr. in Oudh, March, 1891; jud. commr. in Oudh, July, 1891; acting puisne judge, high court, N.W. Prov., Nov., 1892; confd., June, 1895.

BURLS EDWIN GRANT (dir.-gen. of stores, India Office).—Temporary clerk, India office, 20th Oct., 1863; junr. clerk, store dept., Dec.,

1865; senr. clerk, Jan., 1875; asst. dir.-gen. of stores, June, 1891; designation changed to dep. dir.-gen. of stores, June, 1895; dir.-gen. of stores, August, 1896.

BURLTON, CHARLES HENRY BEAUMONT, Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1875; exec. engr., March, 1887; dep. chief engr. and under sec. to govt., public works dept., April, 1894.

BURLTON, HENRY MONTGOMERY BARTON, Col., I. S. C.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, Aug., 1855; served in the Mutiny (medal and clasp); served subsequently in the Bhutan campaign, 1862, and the Abyssinian expdn., 1867-68 (medal); asst. cantonmt. mag., Morar, 1870; cantonmt. mag., Mhow, 1881, Morar, Dec., 1884, and Ambala, Nov., 1886; reverted to mil. duty, 1891.

BURN, JAMES MONTAGUE, Capt., R.E., Survey of India (dep. supt.).—First commn., 17th Feb., 1886; asst. engr., mily. works dept., April, 1888; apptd. to survey of India as asst. supt., May, 1891; dep. supt., Dec., 1896.

BURN, JOHN GUNN, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Inverness Coll. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 24th Dec., 1894, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BURN, RICHARD, Indian C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Liverpool Inst., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1891, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., June, 1896; under sec. to govt., Nov., 1897; supt. of census operations, April, 1900.

BURN, ROBERT NATHANIEL, India Public Works Dept. (under sec. to govt. of India).—Apptd. to the dept., Aug., 1870; dep. examr., Jan., 1880; examr., Jan., 1882; auditor, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, April, 1886; govt. examr. of accts., E. Indian railway, Feb., 1889; examr. of telegraph accts., April, 1890; dep. acctnt.-gen. for inspn. duty, Jan., 1895; offg. under sec., public works dept., March, 1895; confd. Dec., 1897.

BURN-MURDOCH, JOHN, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 14th Feb., 1876, and employed on state railways; served in the Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal with two clasps); employed in mil. works dept., 1880; offg. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, Dec., 1881; on service with the Egyptian expdn., 1882 (medal with two clasps, star, and 5th class Medjidie); dep. consulting engr. for railways, Lahore, Nov., 1882; in same capacity at Bombay, March, 1890; exec. engr., Jan., 1887; in 1890-92 offd. as under sec. to govt. of Bombay, railway branch; services lent to Assam-Bengal railway co., Sept., 1892; and to S. Mahratta railway co., Dec., 1894; suptg. engr., May, 1899; ret'd. May, 1900.

BURNE, HENRY KNIGHTLEY, C.B., Gen., I.S.C.—Ensign, Dec., 1843; arrived, 25th May, 1844, and served throughout the Sutlej campaign, 1845-46 (medal and clasp), and during the Burmese war of 1852-53 (medal and clasp); acted as cantonmt. joint mag., Ambala, Nov., 1849; also as brigade major, Ambala, 1849-51, and as dep. asst. adjutant-gen., Sirhind divn., 1851-52; dep. judge advocate-gen., April, 1852; 2nd asst. sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., July, 1856; 1st asst.

sec., Jan., 1858; dep. sec., March, 1862; sec., June, 1870, to Dec., 1881; C.B., May, 1875.

BURNE, KNIGHTLEY GREY (dep. commr., Burma).—Served in the Bengal police as asst. supt., from 26th Oct., 1865 to May, 1872; from the latter date served in the Burma commn. as asst. commr., and acted as town mag. and supt. of jail at Maulmain, 1872-75; offg. dir. of public instrn., 1882; dep. commr., July, 1886; acting addl. sessions judge, March, 1895, and January, 1896; offg. judge, Moumein, Jan., 1898, and offg. commr., Feb., 1898.

BURNE, SIR OWEN TUDOR, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., Major-Gen., late of the Council of India.—Ensign, 20th foot, 15th April, 1855; served in the Crimea; also in the Mutiny of 1856-58, and was present in fifteen actions and received two steps of rank for gallantry in the field; apptd. mil. sec. to the commander-in-chief in India (Lord Strathnairn), 1861-62, and afterwards A.D.C., and at times offg. mil. sec., to that gen. officer when commander of the forces in Ireland, 1865-68; private sec. to viceroy of India (Earl of Mayo), 1869-72; pol. A.D.C. to sec. of state for India, 1872; asst. sec. in pol. and secret dept., India office, 1873; sec. in that dept., 1874; on special duty in India as private sec. to the viceroy (Lord Lytton), 1876-78; member of the council of India, Jan., 1887 to Dec., 1896; G.C.I.E., Jan. 1897; was a royal commr. of the Health and the Colonial and Indian exhibns. of 1882 and 1886; vice-prest. of the society of arts, 1888-94; apptd. by H. M. the Queen a govr. of the Imperial Institute, 1891; and a royal commr. for the World's Exposition in Chicago, 1891-93; vice-prest. of the council of India, 1891-92; ret'd. from the army, Jan., 1894; chairman of the society of arts, 1896-97; a knight of grace of the order of St. John of Jerusalem; author of 'Clyde and Strathnairn,' &c.

BURRARD, SIDNEY GERALD, Major, R.E., Survey of India (supt. trigon. survey).—First commn., 6th April, 1879; attached to mil. works dept., Baluchistan, March, 1883; asst. supt., trigon. survey, Sept., 1884; dep. supt., June, 1889; supt., Oct., 1890.

BURRIDGE, CHARLES EDWARD (2nd class clerk, Store Dept., India Office).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 16th March, 1888.

BURROWS, LEONARD ROBERT, M.A., late Madras C.S..—Educ. at Lincoln Diocesan Sch., St. Nicholas Coll., Lancing, and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1863; asst. collr. and mag., Dec., 1865; prest. of municipal commn., Madras, 1875-80; sub-collr. and joint mag., March, 1882; acting collr. of sea customs, Madras, and protector of emigrants, Sept., 1883; collr. and mag. and addl. sessions judge, Nilgiris, Sept., 1884; ret'd., Aug., 1889.

BURT, HENRY PARSELL, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (dep. traffic supt.).—Apptd. from Royal Indian Engrg. Coll. as asst. engr., Sept., 1878; employed at Liverpool on works of L. and N. W. railway; posted to state railways, July, 1879; exec. engr., Nov., 1892; offd. as under sec. to govt. of India, March, 1895; dep. traffic supt., N.W. Rly., Nov., 1896.

BURTON, FRANCIS HELY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 21st Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

BURTON, WILLIAM HENRY, Col., R.E., late

Madras Public Works Dept..—Arrived, 26th July, 1857; was apptd. to the dept., May, 1858, and served in Oudh and Hyderabad; exec. engr., July, 1865; exchanged for temporary service out of India, and apptd. to Portsmouth district, and charge of Needles divn., Aug., 1867; proceeded to China, Nov., 1875, where he acted in 1876-77, as commanding royal engrs.; returned to India, Nov., 1877; member of defence committee on coast defences, Dec., 1877; exec. engr., Madras, Jan., 1878; suptg. engr., April, 1889; ret'd., Dec., 1890.

BUSTEED, HENRY ELMSLEY, C.I.E., M.D., Brigade-Surgn., late Mint Dept., Calcutta..—First commn., Indian med. service, Aug., 1855; arrived, March, 1856, and served through the Mutiny campaign (medal with clasp); civil surgn., Cuddalore, Jan., 1861; asst. assay master, Madras mint, Feb., 1865; acted as assay master for various periods, and also as mint master and commr. of paper currency; dep. assay master, Bombay, April, 1868; dep. assay master, Calcutta mint, Nov., 1870; assay master, Sept., 1872; also acted as mint master in 1873 and 1875; ret'd., June, 1886; C.I.E., Jan., 1887; author of 'Echoes from Old Calcutta.'

BUTLER, JAMES, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C. (addl. sess. judge, Burma).—Ensign, 19th foot, Feb., 1866; arrived in Madras, 24th May, 1866; served in the Burma commn. from Oct., 1868, as asst. commr. and town mag.; dep. commr., July, 1881; addl. sess. judge, Bassein; author of the 'Mergui Gazetteer.'

BUTLER, MONTAGU SHERARD DAWES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Haileybury and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 26th Nov., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

BUTLER, SPENCER HARCOURT, Indian C.S. (settlt. offr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Harrow and Balliol College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 29th Oct., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. sec., board of rev., March, 1892; asst. settlt. officer, Dec., 1893; settlt. officer, Dec., 1894.

BUTLER, THEOBALD, B.A., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1879; exec. engr., Sept., 1891; inspr. of works, Behar divn., Feb., 1898.

BUTLER, WILLIAM GEORGE (dep.-dir.-gen., Store Dept., India Office).—Educ. at Bradfield and Eton; junr. clerk, store dept., India office, 31st Jan., 1867; asst. private sec. and précis writer to sec. of state, 1874-78; senr. clerk, Dec., 1889; dep. dir.-gen. of stores, June, 1900.

BUTTERWORTH, ALAN, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and joint mag., Madras).—Educ. at Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 15th Dec., 1885, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec., rev. dept., Aug., 1893; judge, and sessions judge, March, 1897; divl. judge, Feb., 1898; sub-collr. and joint mag. Aug., 1898.

BUTTS, HENRY HILL, B.L., late Oudh Commn..—Extra asst. commr., 3rd June, 1863; from Nov., 1865, served as asst. settlt. officer and asst. commr.; subord. judge, Faizabad, Aug., 1879; district judge, Faizabad, Jan., 1882; dep. commr., Jan., 1882; district and sessions

judge, Sept., 1892; ret'd., Aug., 1893; author of the Lucknow settl. report.

BUYERS, JOHN WALKER, C.I.E., M.A., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. exec. engr., N.W. Prov., 18th Nov., 1869; engr.-in-chief, Garo hills survey, Dec., 1881; suptg. engr., March, 1887; C.I.E., May, 1889; engr.-in-chief, Mu valley railway, July, 1889; ret'd., 1891.

BYATT, JOHN (supervisor, Store Dept., India Office).—Asst. clerk, educn. dept., May, 1869; clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, May, 1873; supervisor, 2nd grade, Nov., 1880; 1st grade, July, 1888.

BYRNE, JOSEPH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at the Royal Univ. of Ireland and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Nov., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

BYTHELL, WILLIAM JOHN, Major, R.E., Survey of India (dep. supt.).—First commn., 25th July, 1882; served as survey officer in Bechuanaland in 1884-85; apptd. asst. engr. Sind-Pishin railway, July, 1887; joined survey of India as asst. supt., May, 1889; dep. supt., April, 1894; served as survey officer in the Chin-Lushai expedition, 1894-95, and with the Chitral Relief Force, 1895.

CABELL, WILLIAM HENRY LAWSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Eton and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 30th Dec., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

CADELL, ALAN, C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., Guetersloh Gymnasium, Westphalia, and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 10th Oct., 1862, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; from April, 1867, to March, 1881, served in the settl. dept.; mag. and collr., May, 1880; offg. commr. of excise and stamps and inspr.-gen. of registrn., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, March, 1883; offg. commr., May, 1889; junr. member, board of rev., April, 1893; senr. member, March, 1894; also member of provl. legisl. council, Nov., 1893; lieut.-govr. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Jan. to Nov., 1895; C.S.I., May, 1895; member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, Oct., 1895; member of govr.-gen.'s council, Feb.-May, 1896; member board of rev., May, 1896; addl. member of legisl. council, Dec., 1896-April, 1897; ret'd., Oct., 1897.

CADELL, ALEXANDER, Lieut.-Gen., late R.E.—2nd lieut., Bengal engrs., 13th June, 1845; joined the public works dept., April, 1848; exec. engr., Rawal Pindi, 1850; Nagpur, 1856; Oudh road dept., Dec., 1858; suptg. engr., Punjab, 1864; from 1867 to 1878 served in Indore, as suptg. engr., and chief engr., and sec. to the govr.-gen.'s agent for Central India; also offd. as chief engr. and sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., 1874-75; chief engr. and sec. to govt. of Bengal, 1879-80; dep. inspr.-gen. of mil. works, 1881; inspr.-gen., mil. works, 1881-82; ret'd., March, 1882.

CADELL, PATRICK ROBERT, Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 7th Dec., 1891, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and administrator of Sachin state; 2nd asst., May, 1897; chairman, Karachi plague commn., July, 1898.

CADELL, THOMAS, V.C., Col., I.S.C.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, April, 1854; arrived, July, 1854; served during the Mutiny (medal, clasp, and Victoria cross); second in command, Malwa Bhill corps, dep. Bhill agent, and pol. asst., April, 1861; pol. agent, E. states, Rajputana, April, 1870; pol. agent, Alwar, Nov., 1870; pol. agent, Marwar, Nov., 1877, and in Mewar, April, 1878; commandant, Erinpura force, and pol. agent, W. Rajputana, Oct., 1879; chief commr. and supt., Port Blair and the Nicobars, Dec., 1879; reverted to mil. dept., April, 1892.

CALCRAFT-KENNEDY, BENNETT CHRISTIAN HUNTINGDON, Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., Queen's Coll., Oxford, and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1891, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., July, 1897.

CALLAGHAN, THOMAS (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Dept., India Office).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 25th March, 1885.

CALNAN, DENIS, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at French Coll., Blackrock, Ireland, and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 13th Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. mag.; joint mag., Oct., 1891; dep. commr., March, 1897; mag. and collr., Nov., 1899.

CALVERT, HUBERT, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 28th Nov., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

CAMA, ARDESHIR KAIKHOSHRO, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. in India and at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1897, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

CAMERON, CHARLES, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., 27th July, 1859; arrived, 28th Jan., 1860; was employed under the mil. dept., 1860-66, and served in the Bhutan campaign (medal); from April, 1866, served in Oudh as civil surgn. and supt. of jail; ret'd., Sept., 1892.

CAMERON, DUNCAN, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 30th Nov., 1876, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1887; in 1889 served in the Punjab as offg. divl. judge; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1894.

CAMERON, LEWIS, M.D., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1866; from Feb., 1873, served as civil asst. surgn. and surgn. in Bengal; ret'd., Aug., 1891.

CAMERON, WILLIAM LOCHIEL, A.M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Educ. at Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; entered the service as asst. engr., Sept., 1877; exec. engr., Sept., 1889.

CAMMIADE, PAUL EUGENE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at the Madras Univ., Caius Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served mag. and in Bengal as asst. collr.

CAMPBELL, ALEXANDER WILLIAM DENNIS-

TOWN, Lt.-Col., I.S.C., late Jud. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1871; served under the mil. dept. to April, 1882, when he was apptd. cantonmt. mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh; ret'd., Feb., 1900.

CAMPBELL, ARCHIBALD EDWARDES, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Assam Commn.—First commn., Jan., 1851; served in the Sonthal campaign, 1855-56, and in Central India, 1857-58 (medal and clasp); from May, 1862, served in the Assam commn.; dep. commr., Dec., 1866; inspr. of labourers, Sibsagar, Feb., 1882; reverted to mil. dept., 1890.

CAMPBELL, ARCHIBALD YOUNG GIPPS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

CAMPBELL, ARTHUR FREDERICK TRAVERS (2nd class clerk, India Audit Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 18th March, 1893.

CAMPBELL, CHARLES STEWART, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 25th Nov., 1899 and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

CAMPBELL, COLIN GEORGE (asst. sec., Pol. Dept., India Office).—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; junr. clerk, mil. dept., 13th Jan., 1876; private sec. to permanent under sec. of state, 1877-92; senr. clerk, Feb., 1889; reading clerk to council, Aug., 1890, to Aug., 1897; private sec. to sec. of state (Right Hon. H. H. Fowler), March, 1894, to July, 1895; sec. to royal commn. on Indian expenditure, Aug., 1895; asst. sec., pol. dept., Aug., 1897.

CAMPBELL, DUNCAN JOHN ALFRED, Indian C.S. (div. judge, Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 24th Nov., 1877, and served in Bengal as asst. mag.; transfed. to Burma, Aug., 1878, and served as asst. commr. and boundary officer; cantonmt. mag., and small cause court judge, Thayetmyo, 1883; on special duty in Upper Burma, Feb., 1886; dep. commr., May, 1888; addl. sessions judge, June-Oct., 1894; transfed. to Cent. Prov. as dep. commr., Nov., 1895; div. judge, May, 1896.

CAMPBELL, DUNCAN McNEIL, Col., R.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 8th April, 1869, and served as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; under sec. to govt., public works dept., 1885-86; suptg. engr., July, 1890; chief engr. and sec. to govt., public works dept., Mysore, July, 1892, to July, 1894; joint sec. to govt. of Madras, gen. buildings and roads branch, Jan., 1896; chief engr., Jan., 1896; ret'd., April, 1898.

CAMPBELL, FRANCIS JOHNSTONE GRAHAM, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 20th Jan., 1864, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and district and sessions judge; ret'd., Oct., 1890.

CAMPBELL, GEORGE ALLEN, India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Joined the service, 5th Aug., 1873, as asst. engr., and was employed in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh on provl. railways and other duties; exec. engr., May, 1883; services lent to Indian Midland railway co., 1887-88; transfed. to state railways, March, 1891;

supt. of works, Cawnpore-Gogra connection, Dec., 1894; suptg. engr., July, 1898.

CAMPBELL, GERALD EDWARD LYON, late Indian C.S., Madras.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 11th Dec., 1883, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and head asst.; sub. collr. and joint mag., Mar., 1896; ret'd., May, 1898.

CAMPBELL, HENRY WILLIAM, Captain Indian Staff Corps (late Burma Police).—First commn., 9th Sept., 1882; served with the Zoh valley expdn., 1890; apptd. asst. comdt. Burma mil. police battn., Oct., 1893; comdt., May, 1894; dep. inspr.-gen. of police for supply and clothing, Dec., 1896; reverted to mil. duty with 18th B. Lancers, Nov., 1898.

CAMPBELL, SIR JAMES McNABB, K.C.I.E., M.A., LL.D., late Indian C.S., Bombay.—Educ. at Glasgow Acad., and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 13th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine duty, April-Aug., 1877; acting municipal commr., Bombay, April, 1880; acting under sec. to govt. in the rev., finl., and gen. depts., Oct., 1880; acted as collr. of Bombay and supt. of stamps and stationery in 1881; C.I.E., Jan., 1885; junr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1890; collr. of land rev., customs and opium, and supt. of stamps and stationery, Nov., 1891; senr. collr., Aug., 1893; K.C.I.E., June, 1897; compiler of 'Bombay Gazetteer.'; ret'd., April, 1900.

CAMPBELL, JOHN, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

CAMPBELL, JOHN STRATHEDEN, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 14th Dec., 1883, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1890; dep. commr., Feb., 1895; dist. and sessions judge, July, 1895; mag. and collr., April, 1898.

CAMPBELL, RICHARD HAMILTON, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and joint mag., Madras).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 18th Dec., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and head asst.; acting under sec., rev. dept., in 1892 and 1893; sub-collr. and joint mag., Aug., 1897.

CAMPBELL, WILLIAM EDWARD MARMADUKE, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

CAMPBELL-WALKER, INCHEs, Col., I.S.C., late Forest Dept., Madras.—Arrived, 27th Dec., 1859; from May, 1865, served in the Madras forest dept. as asst. and dep. conservator; apptd. a commr. for the Vienna exhibn., 1873; knight commander of the order of Francis Joseph of Austria for services there; offg. inspr. of forests, Feb., 1874, to Sept., 1875; on duty at the forest conference, Simla, Sept., 1875; on special duty to New Zealand, 1876-77, and in the Nilgiris, S. Coimbatore, and Tinnevelly, Dec.,

1877; conservator of forests, 1st grade, Dec., 1884; retd., Jan., 1892; author of reports on forest management in Germany, Austria, and Great Britain (1872), and on the forests of New Zealand.

CAMPION, JOHN MONTRIOU, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Apptd. to the dept., 1st July, 1869, as asst. engr., and employed on state railways; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; transf. to Punjab provl. establ., Aug., 1881; served temporarily in mil. works dept., 1881, and on state railways, 1884-85; suptg. engr., July, 1896; offg. sec. to govt., Punjab, July-Oct., 1899.

CANDY, CHARLES HARRISON, B.A., LL.M., late Indian Educ. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st March, 1875, as acting vice-principal, Elphinstone high sch.; head master, Maharaja's coll., Mysore, June, 1876; principal and prof. of English literature, Rajaram coll., Kolhapur, June, 1880; principal, Jan., 1886; retd., April, 1899.

CANDY, EDWARD TOWNSEND, B.L., Indian C.S. (judge, High Court, Bombay).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge and sessions judge; jud. asst. to the pol. agent, Kathiawar, Feb., 1878; acting jud. commr. and judge of the sadar court in Sind, May, 1883; judge and sessions judge, Aug., 1883; acting remembrancer of legal affairs, July-Nov., 1885; judge of high court, Bombay, April, 1892.

CANDY, RICHARD EDWARD, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; acting dir. of public instrn., Berar, June, 1872, to Dec., 1873; first asst. collr. and mag., Oct., 1877; junr. collr. and mag., May, 1885; senr. collr., Sept., 1888; retd., 1899.

CANTOPHER, BERNARD WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Apptd. to the dept., 29th Aug., 1873, and posted to state railways; transf. to Bengal, Aug., 1881; again employed on railways, from Nov., 1883; exec. engr., May, 1884; services lent to Mahrattra railway co., 1888-89; offg. engr. in-chief, Burma state railway, Sept.-Dec., 1893.

CAPEL, SIR ALBERT JAMES LEPPOC, K.C.I.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 10th Feb., 1857; 1st grade supt. and personal asst. to dir.-gen., Dec., 1866; dir. of traffic, May, 1869; dep. dir.-gen., Feb., 1879; dir.-gen. of telegraphs, June, 1883; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887; retd., 1889.

CAPEL, EDWARD LOUIS, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (senr. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Merchant Tailors' Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 28th Oct., 1878, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; from May, 1882, served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; dir. of land records and agric., June, 1891, to April, 1893; junr. collr. and mag., June, 1899; senr. collr., July, 1897; C.I.E., Jan., 1900.
