Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 21st Oct., 1879, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; forest settlt. officer, Khandesh, March, 1885; served in Burma as dep. commr., 1887-90 (medal with clasp); 1st asst. collr. and mag., Aug., 1892; joint collr. and collr. and mag., Sept., 1896; acting chairman, Poona plague committee, October, 1897; senr. collr., April, 1899.

LAMBE, WILLIAM, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, Oct., 1868, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., dep. commr., and mag. and collr.; ret'd., Nov., 1895.

LAMBERT, GEORGE BANCROFT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Provs. and Oudh).—Educ. at Magdalen Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 6th Dec., 1897, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.

LAMBERT, GEORGE BUCHANAN, A.M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.)—Entered the service, Oct., 1887, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1891.

LAMBERT, SIR JOHN, K.C.I.E., late Police Dept., Bengal.—Served from 23rd Nov., 1863, as asst. and district supt.; visitor of the presidency jail, and of the lunatic asylums, July, 1872; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, Feb., 1874; served under the govt. of India, foreign dept., from March, 1882, to March, 1884, as offg. gen. supt. of the operations for the suppression of thagi and dakaiti; acted as commr., Calcutta, and prest. of the boiler commn., 1885-86; commr. of police, Calcutta, April, 1889; member of provl. legisl. council, June, 1892; K.C.I.E., June, 1893; ret'd., May, 1897.

LANE, CLAYTON TURNER, C.I.E., Col., I.S.C., late Berar Commn.—Ensign, Bengal army, 11th Dec., 1858; from March, 1863, served in the Punjab as asst. supt. and district supt. of police; transf. to Berar police, Sept., 1865; inspr.-gen. of police, &c., Haidarabad assigned districts, April, 1877; C.I.E., May, 1895; rank of dep. commr., June, 1895; placed on unemployed supy. list, Feb., 1899.

LANE, JAMES HORSBURGH, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd., 1st Feb., 1857 supt., 1st grade, Bombay, Aug., 1870; dir. of traffic, Feb., 1879; dep. dir.-gen., April, 1890; offg. dir.-gen., 1892; ret'd., June, 1894; compiler of 'Word Code for Foreign Telegrams.'

LANE, JOHN MACDONALD, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd., 22nd Sept., 1862; supt., Bengal, Oct., 1869; served subsequently in the Malabar Coast, Nagpur, British Burma, &c., divns.; ret'd., April, 1891.

LANE, WILMOT, B.L., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1854; arrived, 13th Jan., 1855, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr., and in Oudh as offg. dep. commr., Lucknow; mag. and collr., Jan., 1872; civil and sessions judge, Aug., 1877; commr., Sept., 1880; offd. as member, board of rev., N.W. Prov., 1883; ret'd., April, 1889.

LANG, ARTHUR MOFFATT, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., June, 1852; arrived, July, 1854, and served in the Punjab; exec. engr., March, 1857; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasps); asst. to chief engr., Oudh, April, 1858; asst. sec., public works dept., Oudh, July, 1865; principal, Thomason coll., 1871-77; suptg. engr., dep. inspr.-gen., mil. works, and sec., defence committee, Feb., 1879; sec. to agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan, public works dept., Feb., 1881; chief engr., and sec. to chief commr., British Burma, public works dept., Jan., 1883; joint sec., buildings and roads and railway branches, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, July, 1885; ret'd., Dec., 1888.

LANG, BASIL (advocate-gen., Bombay high court).—Offd. as advocate-gen., May-Nov., 1891, and from April, 1892; confd., 23rd Oct., 1893.

LANG, JOHN, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 28th Nov., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and offg. under sec. to govt.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1896.

LANG, RICHARD TICKELL MONTGOMERY, Col., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 20th Nov., 1859; from May, 1862, served in the Punjab as asst. and dep. commr.; offg. commr., 1881–82; retd., March, 1895.

LANG, ROBERT DACRES MENZIES, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1879; exec. engr., Jan., 1894.

LANGLEY, ALEXANDER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 10th Dec., 1895, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

LANGLEY, EDWARD HARTLEY RAYNSFORD, B.A. (Dub.), M.R.C.S. (Lon.), Brigade-Surgn., late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Bombay med. service, 23rd July, 1858, and was employed under the mil. dept., and as acting civil surgn. in various stations; served in China in 1860–62; presidency surgn. and marine surgn., Bombay, Oct., 1876; retd., Nov., 1889.

LANSDOWNE (MARQUESS OF), RIGHT HON. HENRY CHARLES KEITH FITZMAURICE, K.G., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., D.C.L. (sec. of state for foreign affairs).—Born, 1845; educ. at Eton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; succeeded to the marquise in 1866; a lord of the treasury, 1868–72; under sec. of state for war, 1872–74; under sec. of state for India, April to July, 1880; govr.-gen. of Canada, May, 1883; apptd. govr.-gen. of India, and assumed office, 10th Dec., 1888; returned to England, 27th Jan., 1894; hon. D.C.L., Oxford, 1888; K.G., 1894; sec. of state for war in Lord Salisbury's third administration, July, 1895, Nov., 1900; sec. of state for foreign affairs, Nov., 1900.

LARGE, PHILIP TOWNSEND SOMERVILLE, B.C.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 21st Dec., 1868, and posted to Bombay; transf. to state railways, Punjab, Jan., 1870; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; employed on Bengal-Nagpur railway, Oct., 1884; services lent to Gwalior state, Oct., 1892; suptg. engr., Feb., 1893; retd., April, 1897.

LARKINS, ARTHUR BROOKS (dir., construction branch, Indian Telegraph Dept.).—Apptd. asst. supt., 30th Oct., 1869, and employed on various lines, including Tavoy-Siam line; supt., March, 1886; dir., construction branch, April, 1895.

LARMINIE, JOHN CHARLES, A.M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1868, as proby. asst. engr.; exec. engr., Sept., 1877; suptg. engr., Aug., 1894; retd., Feb., 1901.

LARYMORE, ALEXANDER DOUGLAS, late Jail Dept., Bengal.—Served in the Behar cavalry as trooper, 1857–58; joined the Bengal police as asst. supt., 31st July, 1862; district supt., June, 1872; on famine relief duty, 1874; jail supt., Jan., 1877; has offd. as inspr.-gen. of jails; retd., Nov., 1898.

LAST, WALTER ARTHUR WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Westminster, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 19th Nov., 1891, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; asst. settlt. officer, Sept., 1894; settlt. officer, Oct., 1895; joint mag., Dec., 1896.

LATHAM, FRANCIS LAW, M.A., B.L., late

Jud. Service, Bombay.—Offd. as remembrancer of legal affairs, 1872–73, and as advocate-gen., and judge of the high court, Bombay, for various periods between 1880 and 1884; advocate-gen. Bombay, March, 1884, to Oct., 1893.

LA TOUCHE, JAMES JOHN DIGGES, C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (member, rev. board, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867; served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. officer, till Dec., 1871, when he was transf. as settlt. officer to Ajmir; offg. dep. commr., Ajmir; returned to the N.W. Prov., 1875, and served as district supt. of police, settlt. officer, &c.; from Aug., 1886, served in Upper Burma as commr.; rejoined the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as mag. and collr., April, 1890; member of provl. legisl. council, Dec., 1891; commr. and agent, Benares, April, 1893; chief sec. to govt., Dec., 1893; C.S.I., Jan., 1896; member, board of rev., Dec., 1896; member of viceroy's legisl. council, Oct., 1897, and again Jan., 1899; offg. lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov., May, 1898; author of 'Gazetteer of Ajmir-Mairwara.'

LA TOUCHE, JAMES NORMAN DIGGES, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to Cent. Provs.; exec. engr., Aug., 1893; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Calcutta, April, 1895; ditto, Bombay, Feb., 1896.

LA TOUCHE, THOMAS HENRY DIGGES, B.A., Geological Survey of India (supt.).—Apptd. asst. supt., geol. survey, Nov., 1881; dep. supt., April, 1887; supt., July, 1894; has written several publications of the geol. survey.

LA TOUCHE, WILLIAM PAGET, Lieut.-Gen., I.S.C., late Salt Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Bombay army, Sept., 1854; served in the Persian war, 1856, and the Mutiny campaign; apptd. asst. mag., Dec., 1859, and asst. district supt. of police, Jan., 1865; acting pol. supt., Palanpur, 1867; from Dec., 1867, served as asst. and district supt. of police; and from Feb., 1883, as dep. collr. of salt rev.; returned to Europe, 1887.

LAUDERDALE (EARL OF), RT. HON. FREDERICK HENRY MAITLAND, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C.—First commn., 4th Hussars, Feb., 1861; served in Central India in civil employ from 1869 as boundary officer, and pol. asst.; 2nd asst. agent, Indore, 1877; 1st asst., 1878; addl. pol. agent and supt., Charkhari, 1880; retd., Jan., 1886.

LAUGHLIN, ROBERT CAMPBELL, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the dept. as asst. supt., Oct., 1866; held charge of various divns., chiefly in Madras and Bengal, and of the telegraph stores, Calcutta; retd., May, 1891.

LAUGHTON, DANIEL WILSON, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Berar Commn.—First commn., Jan., 1854; arrived, April, 1854; from Jan., 1865, served in the Hyderabad assigned districts as asst. commr., small cause court judge, and cantonmt. mag.; acted as inspr.-gen. of registrn., 1873; dep. commr., Feb., 1885; retd., May, 1886.

LAURIE, HENRY MONTAGUE, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—

Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1887, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., and offg. under sec.; acted as commr. of excise in 1892; dep. commr., June, 1895; 2nd sec. to chief commr., April, 1900.

LAURIE, LEWIS KOSUTH, B.L., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 27th Oct., 1873, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., and in various acting appts.; under sec., Oct., 1885; judge, small cause court, Jabalpur, Jan., 1890; dep. commr., Dec., 1890; commr. of excise, Jan., 1891; inspr.-gen. of police and jails, April, 1892; sec. to chief commr., April, 1895; commr., Sept., 1895; author of a digest of rulings of jud. commrs., Central Prov., in civil cases (1881), and some manuals relating to the Central Prov.; ret'd., Jan., 1900.

LAURIE, MAXWELL, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, Edinburgh Univ., and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 9th Dec., 1889, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settlt. officer.

LAW, SIR EDWARD FITZGERALD, K.C.M.G., Govt. of India.—Entered the Royal Artillery as lieutenant, 1868; served in the Suakin campaign of 1885; entered the diplomatic service in 1887 as commercial attaché at St. Petersburg; minister resident at Athens, 1898; K.C.M.G., May, 1898; member of the council of the Ottoman debt, at Constantinople, Jan., 1899; financial member of the govr.-gen.'s council, March, 1900.

LAW, VICTOR EDWARD, Maj.-Gen., Madras cavalry, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Madras cavalry, 20th April, 1859; attaché, foreign dept., July, 1872; served in Rajputana as supt. of railway police, 1872-77; pol. agent, Jaipur, 1877, and afterwards in Alwar, 1877-83; asst. at Sujanagar to govr.-gen.'s agent in Rajputana, Feb., 1884; agent with king of Oudh, and supt. of pol. pensions, March, 1884; in charge of ex-Amir Muhammad Yakub Khan of Kabul, Aug., 1884; rest., Jaipur, April, 1895; returned to Europe, April, 1897.

LAWRENCE, ALEXANDER JOHN, C.I.E., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1856; arrived, 10th Nov., 1856; served from March, 1858, in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and settlt. officer, and in the Central Prov. as settlt. officer of Bhandara; mag. and collr., Oct., 1874; commr., Allahabad, April, 1885, and Meerut, April, 1889; C.I.E., May, 1888; ret'd., Nov., 1891.

LAWRENCE, EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (senr. collr. and mag., and pol. agent, Jhalawar).—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 12th Nov., 1878, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; also acted as asst. to the pol. rest. at Aden, March, 1882, and as under sec. to govt.; under sec. to the govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., April, 1889; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, April, 1890; acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Aug., 1894; senr. collr. and mag., and pol. agent, Jhalawar, Nov. 1899.

LAWRENCE, HENRY JOHN, Lieut.-Col., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 14th Feb., 1862; from Aug., 1866, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and jud. asst.; district judge, Nov.,

1884; divl. judge, Jan., 1885; ret'd., March, 1895.

LAWRENCE, HENRY STAVELEY, Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt., Bombay).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Sept., 1896; under sec. to govt. rev. and finl. depts., May, 1898.

LAWRENCE, WALTER ROPER, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (private sec. to Viceroy).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 5th Dec., 1879, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and in Ajmir and Merwara as pol. asst. and mag.; under sec. to govt., Punjab, 1885 and 1886; under sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agricl. dept., Sept., 1886; offd. as sec. in 1888; settlt. officer, Kashmir, April, 1889; C.I.E., Jan., 1891; ret'd., Mar., 1897; author of 'The Valley of Kashmir,' 1895; apptd. private sec. to the Viceroy (Lord Curzon), Jan., 1899.

LAWRIE, EDWARD, M.B., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (residency surgn., Haidarabad).—First commn., Bengal med. service, 15th Nov., 1872; rest. surgn., Calcutta med. coll. hosp., April, 1874; prof'r., med. coll., June, 1875; civil surgn., Monghyr, March, 1879; prof'r. of surgery, Lahore med. sch., and meteorological reporter, Oct., 1879; residency surgn., Haidarabad, Oct., 1885.

LEA, JAMES HERBERT, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 30th Oct., 1888, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1895; mag. and collr., March, 1900.

LEACH, EDMUND ARTHUR (supt. Indian Telegraph Dept.).—Apptd. to Indo-European Telegraph Dept., Feb., 1873; transfld. to Indian Tel. Dept., Sept., 1878, as asst. supt.; supt., Oct., 1887; served in Mu Valley Railway, 1890, and received Burma medal and clasp.

LEAPINGWELL, ARTHUR HENRY, Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (dist. surgn., Vizagapatam).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1873, and was employed under the mil. dept.; from Nov., 1879, served as supt., lunatic asylum, Madras, zillah surgn. and civil surgn.; district surgn., Calicut, March, 1892; Vizagapatam, March, 1893.

LEATHER, JOHN WALTER, Ph.D., F.I.C., F.C.S. (asst. agricl. chemist to the govt. of India).—Apptd. agricl. chemist to the govt. of India, 17th Nov., 1892; asst. agricl. chemist to the govt. of India, Aug., 1893.

LE BRETON, WILLIAM INGLIS, Major, I.S.C., late Public Works Dept., Bombay.—First commn., royal artillery, Dec., 1864; arrived, 1st Nov., 1865; joined the dept., Nov., 1866, and served in Bombay till Nov., 1869, and subsequently as asst. comptr., dep. comptr., and comptr. of accts. in the Punjab, N.W. Prov., Bombay, and Calcutta; from Dec., 1870, served as examr. of guaranteed railway accts., Bombay; examr., public works accts., Bombay, Feb., 1887; ret'd., June, 1888.

LEE, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (dist. med. officer, Madra).—First commn., Sept., 1876; on mil. duty till Oct., 1884; civil surgn., Mangalore, Oct., 1884;

dist. med. and sany. officer, Tanjore, July, 1897; surgn. 1st dist. Madras, and inspr. of emigrants, May, 1898; dist. med. officer, Madura, March, 1900.

LEE, WILLIAM HERBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (district and sessions judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 19th Dec., 1887, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfld. to Assam as asst. commr., June, 1890; retransfd. to Bengal as joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1895; mag. and collr., April, 1897; district and sessions judge, June, 1898; author of a history of Puri, &c.

LEEDS, ROBERT JAMES, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 25th Sept., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; from Nov., 1879, served in the jud. dept. as civil and sessions judge; retd., April, 1887.

LEEDS, WILLIAM HENRY ARTHUR ST. JOHN, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 9th Dec., 1885, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfld. to Burma as asst. commr., Nov., 1888; offd. as dir. of land records and agric., 1890-91; dep. commr., June, 1893; offd. as commr., Arakau, March, 1899; presdtt., Rangoon municipality, Aug., 1899.

LEES, DONALD HECTOR, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam).—Educ. at N. London Coll. Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 1st Dec., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfld. to Assam as asst. commr., Dec., 1892; dep. commr., Feb., 1899.

LEES, OSWALD CAMPBELL, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (under sec. to govt. of India).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. as asst. engr., 24th Sept., 1878, and posted to Bengal irrgn. branch; exec. engr., May, 1890; under sec. to govt., March, 1895; under sec. to govt. of India, Nov., 1897.

LEES, REGINALD OSWELL, India Telegraph Dept. (supt.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. to the telegraph dept. as asst. supt., Sept., 1879; supt., Nov., 1894.

LEE-WARNER, JOHN, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1863, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on special duty, inquiring into condition of poorer classes, N. Arcot and Salem, 1868; special asst. collr., Madura, for the management of the Ramnad estate, 1871-78; sub-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1882; acting collr. of sea customs, Madras, May, 1884; district and sessions judge, Kistna, Oct., 1884; collr. and mag., Dec., 1884; retd., Jan., 1890.

LEE-WARNER, SIR WILLIAM, K.C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (pol. sec., India Office).—Educ. at Rugby Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. to the Bombay civil service after exam. of 1867; arrived, 8th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., private sec. to govr., and acting under sec.; from Dec., 1876, to Aug., 1878, served in Sind as asst. commr., &c.; member of the educn. commn., Calcutta, 1882; in 1884 acted as junr. under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., and as press commr.; acting dir. of public instrn.,

Bombay, March, 1885; on special duty in the Haidarabad state, Nov., 1885; acting pol. agent, Kolhapur, Jan., 1886; also prest. of the regency council, May, 1886; sec. to govt. of Bombay, pol., &c., depts., Aug., 1887; C.S.I., Jan., 1892; addl. member of govr.-gen.'s council, 1893-94, and in 1895; rest. in Mysore, and chief commr. of Coorg, Feb., 1895; retd. from the civil service, Sept., 1895; sec., pol. and secret dept., India office, Sept., 1895; K.C.S.I., May, 1898; author of 'The Protected Princes of India'; 'The Citizen of India,' &c.

LE FANU, WILLIAM JOSEPH HENRY, B.A., B.L., late Indian C.S., Madras.—Educ. at Kingstown Sch., Tipperary Gr. Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 28th Oct., 1865, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; district and sessions judge, Kistna, Dec., 1885; collr. and mag., Jan., 1886; acted as member, board of rev., 1893; acting collr. of sea customs, &c., April, 1895; member of rev. board and commr. of land rev., Dec., 1898; compiler of 'Salem District Manual'; offd. as famine commr., Madras, May, 1897; addl. member of Madras legisl. council, March, 1900; retd., Oct., 1900.

LEFTWICH, CHARLES GERRANS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Christ's Hospl., and at St. John's Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 11th Dec., 1896, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

LEGEYT, PHILIP HARRISON, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 27th March, 1851; was employed under the mil. dept., including service with the Persian expedy. force (medal and clasp); served subsequently with the Gujarat irregular horse, and also acted as asst. pol. agent, Mahi Kantha; from May, 1863, served in the pol. dept. as asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar and Cutch, and mag. of Bhaunagar; pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, 1872-80; pol. supt., Palanpur, Sept., 1880; returned to Europe, 1889.

LEGGATT, EDWARD OWEN EVERY, B.A., Indian C.S. (district and sessions judge, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. supt. and judge of the small cause court, Dehra Dun; joint mag., May, 1890; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1895.

LEGGATT, ERNEST HUGH EVERY, Indian C.S. (joint judge and sess. judge, Bombay).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 15th Dec., 1891, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., June, 1897; joint judge and sess. judge, July, 1898.

LEGH, EDMUND WILLOUGHBY, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Malvern Sch., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 6th Dec., 1897, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

LEIGH, HENRY PERCY POINGDESTRE, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C. (dep. commr., Punjab).—In mil. employ from 11th Nov., 1872; joined the Punjab commn., 23rd April, 1879, and served as asst. commr.; C.I.E., Jan., 1892; dep. commr., April, 1894; author of Barak Tappa settlt. report.

LELY, FREDERIC STYLES PHILPIN, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (commr., Bombay).—Educ. at Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 8th Dec., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; in charge of the Sachin state, Oct., 1881; first asst., Surat, Dec., 1884; administrator of the Porbandar state in Kathiawar, June, 1886; junr. collr., June, 1890; member of Deccan agriculturists relief act commn., 1891-92; senr. collr. and mag., March, 1893; commr., July, 1897; addl. member of govr's. council, April, 1899; C.S.I., Jan., 1901.

LEMAN, GEORGE DOWNTON, late Madras C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; 1857, and served in the rev. and jud. depts., Madras; principal asst., Ganjam, Dec., 1866; collr. and mag., Feb., 1872; ret'd., March, 1888.

LEMARCHAND, WALTER JOHN (dist. supt. of police, Punjab).—Entered the service as asst. dist. supt., Feb., 1869; dist. supt., July, 1876.

LE MARCHANT, FRANCIS CHARLES, B.A. (member of the council of India).—Educ. at Eton and at Balliol Coll., Oxford (first class in classics, 1865); apptd. member of the council of the sec. of state, 27th Feb., 1896.

LE MESSURIER, AUGUSTUS, C.I.E., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., Bombay engrs., Dec., 1856; served in the Mutiny; joined the Bombay public works dept., May, 1860; exec. engr., March, 1863; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, Aug., 1864; served in the Abyssinian expedn., 1868 (medal); dep. consulting engr. for railways in Sind, Aug., 1871; sec. to the railway transport committees, 1875-76 and 1876-77; suptg. engr., May, 1877; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, railway branch, 1877-78; served in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); engr.-in-chief, Mysore state railway, Nov., 1879; joint sec. to chief commr., Mysore, 1880; manager, Mysore state railway, Feb., 1881; consulting engr. to Mysore govt., and sec. to chief commr., Coorg, March, 1882; engr.-in-chief, Bangalore-Harihar railway; offg. consulting engr., railways, Calcutta, Feb., 1888; chief engr. and sec. to govt. of Punjab, May, 1889; reverted to mil. duty, June, 1892; ret'd. from the army, May, 1893.

LE MESURIER, HAVILLAND, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 9th Nov., 1886, and served in Assam as asst. commr. and asst. sec. and personal asst. to chief commr.; transf. to Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., April, 1892; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1893; mag. and collr., June, 1897.

LE MESURIER, JOHN, Lieut.-Gen., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Bombay.—Joined Addiscombe, Feb., 1852; arrived, 26th Nov., 1855, and served in Sind as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; served with the Persian expedn., 1856-57 (medal and clasp); supt. of works, W. Indus, Karachi, Dec., 1873; suptg. engr., Jan., 1879; chief engr. for irrign., and joint sec. to govt. of Bombay, public works dept. (irrign.), March, 1884; suptg. engr. in Sind, Jan., 1886; ret'd., March, 1887.

LENOX - CONYNGHAM, GERALD PONSONBY, Captain, R.E., Survey of India (dep. supt.).—First commn., 16th Sept., 1885; apptd. to survey of India as asst.-supt., July, 1889; dep. supt., May, 1894.

LE QUESNE, WILLIAM HENRY, late Bom-

bay Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1876; exec. engr., April, 1884; asst. chief engr. for irrign., 1886-87; under sec. to govt. public works dept., in 1891; ret'd., Sept., 1899.

LE ROSSIGNOL, WALTER AUBIN, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1893, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

LESLIE, JOHN TASMAN WADDELL, M.B., Major, Indian Med. Service (sec. to dir.-gen., I.M.S.).—First commn., 1st Oct., 1884; in mil. employ to 19th April, 1889, when he was apptd. sec. to inspr.-gen. of jails, Burma; junr. civil surgn., Rangoon, May-Nov., 1890; acting chemical examr. and prof'r., Calcutta med. coll., 1892; sec. to dir.-gen., Indian med. service, Oct., 1893.

LESLIE, MORICE, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to Haidarabad; exec. engr., May, 1884; transf. to Central Prov., Feb., 1886; asst. sec. to chief commr., June-Sept., 1887; transf. to Coorg, Jan., 1893; retransf. to Central Prov. with independent exec. charge, Saugor, Feb., 1897; ret'd., Nov., 1899.

LESLIE-JONES, LEYCESTER HUDSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Hereford Cathedral School and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 6th Dec., 1895, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

LETHBRIDGE, SIR ALFRED SWAINE, K.C.S.I., M.D., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1867; served from March, 1872, as jail supt., offg. civil surgn., and offg. sanitary commr., British Burma, and afterwards of Bengal; inspr.-gen. of jails, Bengal, Jan., 1878; C.S.I., May, 1890; prest. of the factory commn., 1890; and of the native passengers ship committee, 1890-91; gen. supt. of operations for the suppression of thagi and dakaiti, April, 1892; member of the viceroy's legisl. council, Feb., 1895, to Jan., 1897; ret'd. April, 1898.

LETHEREN, SAMUEL KEMP (2nd class clerk, store dept., India Office).—Educ. at King's Coll., London; clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 6th July, 1877.

LEUPOLT, JOHN CUNNINGHAM, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 10th Oct., 1862, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; civil and sessions judge, Aug., 1882; ret'd., April, 1889.

LEVENTHORPE, ALGERNON, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1879; exec. engr., June, 1890; asst. to chief engr., and under sec. to govt., Sept., 1899.

LEVENTHORPE, JOHN BONFOY, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Oct., 1877, and served in the Cent. Provs.; exec. engr., Nov., 1887.

LEVESON, HENRY GEORGE ASHWORTH, Indian C.S. (asst. supt., S. Shan states, Burma).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 14th Dec., 1889, and served in

Burma as asst. commr.; asst. supt., S. Shan states, July, 1892.

LEVINGE, EDWARD VERE, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 28th Nov., 1888, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., jud., &c., depts, Nov., 1893; joint mag. and dep.-collr., April, 1896; mag. and collr., July, 1898.

LEWIS, EDWIN MORRIS, late supt. of printing, Bengal.—Supt., Bengal secretariat press, April, 1869; supt. of govt. printing, Bengal, Sept., 1877, to June, 1895; ret'd., July, 1895.

LEWIS, THOMAS CROMPTON, M.A. (dir. of public instrn., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service as profir., govt. coll., Lahore, 18th Oct., 1881; principal, May, 1886; inspr. of schs., Nov., 1891; dir. of public instrn., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Oct., 1894; author of 'Arithmetic for Indian Schools,' and various articles on mathematical subjects.

LEWIS, WILLIAM CUTHBERT, M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Entered the service Oct., 1876, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Sept., 1880, ret'd., April, 1899.

LEWTAS, JOHN, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (civil surg., 24-Parganas).—Joined the service 30th Sept., 1875; in mil. employ to July, 1878, and again from Oct., 1878 to Nov., 1881, and from Feb., 1883 to Nov., 1890; joint med. offir., Simla, Nov., 1890; civ. surg., Chittagong, June, 1894; civ. surg., Darjeeling, 1898; civil surg., 24-Parganas, Jan., 1900.

LEY, WILLIAM EDMUND, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Provs.).—Educ. at Bromsgrove Sch., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 17th Nov., 1899, and served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr. and registrar, judl. commr.'s court.

LIARDET, CHARLES AYLMER, Col., I.S.C., late Rev. Survey Dept., Madras.—Ensign, Madras infantry, Oct., 1855; arrived, 14th Nov., 1855; served in the Madras rev. survey dept. from Oct., 1868; on famine relief works, 1877-78; dep. supt., Sept., 1884; ret'd., April, 1888.

LIDDERDALE, ROBERT, M.D., Brigade-Surgn., late Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1858; supt. of vaccn., 8th Nov., 1869; sanitary commr., Bengal, April, 1880; ret'd., Jan., 1889.

LIGHTFOOT, JABEZ, India Public Works Dept. (manager E. Coast railway).—Apptd. from the Sind, Punjab, and Delhi railway as dep. manager, N.W. state railway, Jan., 1886; manager, Punjab section, March, 1887; Sind section, May, 1887; auditor, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, Jan., 1889; transf. to superior accts. establt., Sept., 1889; acted as manager, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, in 1890 and 1891; examr. of guaranteed railway accts., Madras, Oct., 1891; examr. of accts., N.W. railway, Oct., 1896; manager, East Coast railway, Sept., 1899.

LILLEY, ABRAHAM ROBERT, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 14th Sept., 1870, and employed in the Central Prov. and on state railways; transf. to Mysore, Oct., 1875; and to Assam, Oct., 1880; exec. engr., Jan., 1885; employed on railways in Burma from May, 1887.

LILLINGSTON, EDWARD GORDON, Col.,

I.S.C.—First commn., 30th Aug., 1859; from Feb., 1864, served in Bengal as asst. commr., offg. pol. agent, Hill Tipperah, supt. of the central jail and the European penitentiary at Hazaribagh, and dep. commr.; reverted to mil. dept., March, 1892.

LIMJI NAVROJI BANAJI, B.L. (prothonotary, Bombay high court).—Joined the service April, 1871; head assist. to prothonotary, Nov., 1893; prothonotary, eccl. and admy. regr., June, 1898.

LINDSAY, BENJAMIN, Indian C.S. (registrar, high court N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Methodist Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 4th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and in Oudh as asst. commr.; asst. supt., and small cause court judge, Dehra Dun, April, 1894; joint mag., May, 1894; registrar, high court, March, 1898.

LINDSAY, JAMES GAVIN, Col., R.E., late Indian Public Works Dept.—Lieut., Madras engrs., Aug., 1855; joined the dept., Dec., 1857; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasp) and subsequently as exec. engr. in Banda and Orissa; dep. consulting engr. for railways, 1863; exec. engr., railway survey, Mysore, April, 1870; offg. engr.-in-chief, N. Bengal railway, 1872; acted as dir. of state railways, N.E. system, and joint sec. to Bengal govt., 1878-79; engr.-in-chief, Harnai and Gulistan-Karez sections of Kandahar railway, Feb., 1880; served in the Afghan campaign, 1879-80 (medal); suptg. engr. and sec. to rest., Hyderabad, March, 1881; engr.-in-chief, S. Mahratta railway, Nov., 1881; services lent to S. Mahratta railway co., May, 1884; engr.-in-chief, Bolan railway, April-June, 1885; ret'd., April, 1891.

LIST, GEORGE HENRY, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., 12th April, 1873; exec. engr., July, 1875; asst. sec., railway branch, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Oct., 1879; served subsequently on various railways; supt., way and works, N.W. railway, Nov., 1891; suptg. engr., March, 1892; chief engr., Aug., 1895; ret'd., Dec., 1899.

LISTER, ALLEN LINDSAY, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Rossall Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1862; served in Madras as asst. and sub-collr. and on special duty in connection with the relief of the poorer classes, S. Arcot, 1868; on famine duty, 1877; collr. and mag., S. Kanara, July, 1882; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1882; ret'd., March, 1890.

LISTER, EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt. of Bengal).—Educ. at Sheffield Gr. Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 26th Dec., 1894, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., genl. depts., Dec., 1899.

LISTER, WASTEL JAMESON, Major, R.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Educ. at Wellington Coll.; entered the service, 6th Jan., 1872; joined the dept., 26th April, 1875; served with the Kandahar field force in 1881; exec. engr., Sept., 1884; ret'd., March, 1897.

LISTON, JOHN, Col., I.S.C.—Arrived as ensign, 29th May, 1856; served with the Bundelkhand field force in the campaign of 1859; from Aug., 1863, served in Jhansi, Kumaon, and Ajmir as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Lalitpur, Dec., 1879; offg. commr., Jhansi, Aug. to Nov., 1882; ret'd., Nov., 1889.

LITTLE, CHARLES, M.D., M.C., L.R.C.S., L.M., Col., Indian Med. Service (insp. gen., civil hospls., Burma).—Arrived, 1st Nov., 1872, and served under the mil. dept.; civil surg., Akola, Jan., 1877; sanitary commr., insp.-gen. of dispensaries, and supt. of vaccn. in the Haidarabad assigned districts, July, 1881; admn. med. officer, Haidarabad contingent, June, 1897; staff surg., Secunderabad, June, 1898; insp. gen. of civil hospls. and sany. commr., Burma, Feb., 1900.

LITTLE, CHARLES, M.A. (professor, Presidency coll., Calcutta).—Educ. at the Univs. of Aberdeen and Cambridge; entered the service, Oct., 1885, as professor, Presidency coll., also meteorological reporter to the govt. of Bengal, March, 1896.

LITTLE, FREDERICK ARTHUR, late Jud. Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at Uppingham Sch.; admitted a solicitor, 1874; acted as govt. solicitor and public prosecutor, Bombay, 1870 and 1883, and was confd. in this appt., May, 1884.

LITTLE, JOHN ALBERT, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., 9th March, 1867, and served in various divns. in Central India; transf. to state railways, Feb., 1871; exec. engr., Sept., 1872; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Calcutta, April, 1889; consulting engr., Aug., 1892, with rank of suptg. engr.; reverted to mil. dept., Feb., 1895.

LITTLE, STEPHEN, M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgeon, Rawal Pindi).—First commn., 31st March, 1879; on milv. duty till Feb., 1884, since when he has acted as civil surgeon at various stations; apptd. dep. sany. commr., Punjab, Feb., 1890; civil surgeon, Rawal Pindi, Aug., 1898.

LIVESAY, JOHN JAMES, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 14th Nov., 1861; from Jan., 1863, served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., dep. commr., and mag. and collr.; on famine duty in 1874; ret'd., March, 1888.

LIVINGSTONE, WILLIAM BOYD, late Bengal Educ. Service.—Entered the service, June, 1865; headmaster, Dacca Coll. Sch., Jan., 1867; asst. profr., Dacca Coll., July, 1871; profr., Rajshahi Coll., Sept., 1887; principal, July, 1893.

LLOYD, ERNEST SAMPSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 21st Dec., 1894, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

LLOYD, ERNEST THOMAS, B.A., B.L., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Charterhouse, Shrewsbury, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 2nd Feb., 1882, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Nov., 1885, as asst. commr. and offg. city mag., Lucknow; joint mag., March, 1889; ret'd., Sept., 1894.

LLOYD, FRANCIS, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at the Coll. Sch., Gloucester; apptd. from Haileybury Coll., in 1844; served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge and sessions judge; jud. asst. to mag., Satara, 1860; judge and sessions judge, 1861; judge of the high court, Bombay, 1869; ret'd. in 1873.

LLOYD, HENRY JESSE, late Bengal Opium Dept.—Entered the service, Sept., 1869, and

served as asst. and sub-dep. opium agent; ret'd., April, 1900.

LOCH, JAMES HENRY, M.D., Dep. Surgn.-Gen.—Joined the Bengal med. service, Dec., 1854; served with the Oudh field force in 1855, and at the relief of Azamgarh, 1858 (medal); from Aug., 1859, served in the N.W. Prov. as civil asst. surgn. and civil surgn. in various stations, and supt. of jail; dep. surgn.-gen., Central Prov., Jan., 1884; ret'd., Jan., 1889.

LOCH, WILLIAM, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Pol. Dept. Govt. of India (resdt., Nepal).—First commn., March, 1866; asst. pol. supt., Sawantwari, May, 1877, asst. to gen. supt. of operations for suppression of thagi and dakaiti, 1878; 2nd asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Indore, 1879; asst. at Banswara and Partabgarh, 1880; during the Afghan war was on pol. duty with Major-Gen. Sir R. Phayre's force, marching to the relief of Kandahar (medal); 2nd asst. agent, Baluchistan, in charge of Pishin valley, Nov., 1880; asst. rest., W. Rajputana, and boundary settl. officer in Marwar, April, 1889; acting pol. agent, Bikanir, April, 1894; pol. agent, E. states of Rajputana, July, 1895; resdt., Baghdad, and pol. resdt. in Turkish Arabia, Sept., 1897; resdt., Nepal, April, 1900.

LOCH, WILLIAM, Lieut.-Col. Bengal Infantry (princl., Mayo Coll., Ajmer).—First commn., 4th Jan., 1862; aide-de-camp to the viceroy, Oct., 1876; principal, Mayo Coll., Ajmer, Aug., 1879.

LOCH, WILLIE WALKER, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Glenalmond, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 14th Nov., 1868, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. collr. and mag., Sept., 1888; senr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1890; acting commr. of customs, &c., Bombay, May, 1891; also British delegate for the Portuguese-Indian treaty; acting commr., N. divn., April-July, 1892; acting sec. to govt., rev., &c., depts., Oct., 1894; offd. again as commr. of customs, &c., Bombay, in 1894, and from March, 1895; member of legisl. council, July, 1895; ret'd. Sept., 1896.

LOFTUS - TOTTENHAM, ALEXANDER ROBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Clifton and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 6th Dec., 1897, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

LOGAN, ALEXANDER COCHRANE, Indian C.S. (junr. collr. and collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1881, and served as asst. collr. and mag., forest settl. officer, and offg. under sec. to govt.; 1st asst., March, 1892; junr. collr. and collr. and mag., March, 1897; acting sec. to govt. of Bombay, gen. educ., &c., depts., May, 1896, and again, Nov., 1897.

LOGAN, ROBERT, late Indian C.S.—Educ. at the Royal Acad., Inverness, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 28th Oct., 1869, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; from April, 1881, served in the finl. dept.; asst. compt.-gen., Sept., 1881; under sec., govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., July, 1883; dep. acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Dec., 1885; acctnt.-gen., Punjab, Oct., 1889; acctnt.-gen., Bombay, and commr. of state paper currency, March, 1890; on special

duty in Kashmir, 1891; offg. comptr. and auditor-gen., April, 1892; ret'd., Aug., 1894.

LOGAN, THOMAS TWEDDLE, B.A. (inspr. of schools, Madras educt. dept.).—Apptd. headmaster, Bellary Coll., Nov., 1880; inspr. of schs., Sept., 1885.

LOGAN, WILLIAM, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Musselburgh Gr. Sch., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1861, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., sub-collr., and collr. and mag.; fellow of univ. of Madras, March, 1881; acting member, board of rev., Nov., 1882; acting rest. of Travancore and Cochin, May, 1883 to Feb., 1884; publ'd. a collection of treaties, &c., relating to British affairs in Malabar, 1879; and edited Mr. Græme's glossary of Malayalam words and phrases, 1882; ret'd., 1888.

LOKENDRA NATH PALIT, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 13th Dec., 1886, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1893; mag. and collr., Aug., 1897; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1899.

LONGE, FRANCIS BACON, Major, R.E. (asst. surv. genl., Survey of India).—First commn., 2nd Feb., 1876; asst. supt., survey dept., March, 1880; dep. supt., April, 1888; supt., Mar., 1896; asst. surv. gen., Nov., 1899; served in Afghan war, 1878-81, and with the Suakim expedy. force, 1885.

LORD, ROBERT JOHN CORNWALLIS, Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt., pol. dept., Bombay).—Educ. at St. Peter's Sch., Weston-super-Mare, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 2nd Nov., 1892, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst. July, 1897; joint judge and sess. judge, Aug., 1897; asst. judge, May, 1898; under sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts., Bombay, Jan., 1900.

LORD, WALTER FREWEN, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., London, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1881, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; ret'd., Feb., 1889.

LORIMER, JOHN GORDON, Indian C.S. (asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept.).—Educ. at the Dundee Instn., High Sch., Dundee, Edinburgh Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1891, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and acting under sec. to govt.; asst. settlt. collr., Jan., 1896; asst. pol. officer, Tochi field force, July, 1897; pol. officer, Tochi, Jan., 1898; pol. officer, Khyber, June, 1899; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Sept., 1899.

LOVE, HENRY DAVISON, Lieut.-Col., R.E. (principal, civil engrg. coll., Madras).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., Bath, and Royal Mil. Acad., Woolwich; lieut., R.E., 12th Sept., 1872; acted as inspr. of schools for science and art dept., 1875-6; admitted to Madras establt., Oct., 1876; first asst. master, civil engrg. coll., July, 1877; served subsequently in the public works dept., and acted as dep. consulting engr. for railways, and as dep. chief engr. and under sec. to govt.; asst. examr. of accts., Nov., 1879; principal, civil engrg. coll., Jan., 1880; fellow of Madras univ., March, 1881; compiled a 'Manual of Hydraulics,' 1886.

LOVETT, BERESFORD, C.B., C.S.I., Major-

Gen., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., June, 1858; arrived, Sept., 1860; joined the public works dept., April, 1861, and served in the Punjab till Sept., 1866, when he was transf'd. to the Persian telegraph dept.; asst. commr., Seistan boundary commn., Sept., 1870; rejoined public works dept. as exec. engr., Sept., 1872; C.S.I., Feb., 1873; attached to Bori valley field force, 1877-78; served in the Afghan war, 1878-79; transf'd. to Bengal, Feb., 1881; British consul at Astrabad, 1880-83; suptg. engr., Bengal, 1885; transf'd. to mil. works dept., April, 1886; commanding engr., Hazara field force, Sept., 1888; C.B., 1889; ret'd., Nov., 1894.

LOVETT, HARRINGTON VERNEY, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Sherborne, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 20th Nov., 1884, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr.; joint mag., Dec., 1893; dep. commr., Feb., 1896; mag. and collr., Dec., 1896.

LOVETT-THOMAS, LEWIS HENRY (dep. inspr.-gen. of police, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Joined the dept., 5th Dec., 1870; asst. district supt., Feb., 1874; district supt., July, 1877; dep. inspr.-gen., Sept., 1894.

LOW, CHARLES ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (settlt. offr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Rugby, and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 19th Dec., 1893, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., and offg. under sec.; settlt. offr., Oct., 1898.

LOWIS, CECIL CHAMPAIN, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Newton Coll., Devonshire, Göttingen Gymnasium, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 8th Jan., 1888, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and registrar to jud. commr.; under sec. to chief commr., April, 1891; offd. as sec. to chief commr., from Feb., 1895; dep. commr., Oct., 1898.

LOWIS, EDMOND ELLIOT, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 1st Feb. 1857; asst. mag. and collr., Aug., 1858; joint mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1861; dep. commr., W. Duars, Oct., 1867; dep. collr. of customs, Calcutta, Oct., 1867; mag. and collr., Maldah, Dec., 1867; commr., March, 1875; ret'd., Dec., 1891.

LOWIS, JOHN, B.L. (govt. advocate, Rangoon).—Apptd. asst. govt. advocate, April, 1884; govt. advocate, April, 1900.

LOWNDES, THOMAS, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Burma.—Joined the Madras army, Dec., 1854; served in the dept. from March, 1861, as supt.; on special mission to Chiengme, 1871; pol. agent, Bhamo, May, 1872, to Oct., 1873; inspr.-gen. of police, British Burma, Jan., 1876; apptd. to special duty in Upper Burma, Dec., 1885; ret'd., Aug., 1887.

LOWNDS, EDWIN ALBERT (2nd class clerk, registry and record dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 26th June, 1899.

LOWRY, WILLIAM EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Leys Sch., Cambridge, London Univ., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 30th Dec., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settlt. offr.

LOXTON, CHARLES WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Dorset County Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Jan., 1897, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

LUARD, CHARLES HENRY, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., June, 1855; joined the dept., Nov., 1857; asst. principal, Thomason coll., 1863; asst. sec., govt. of India, public works dept., March, 1865; dep. supt., Ganges canal, 1865; dep. consulting engr., Bengal, Oct., 1867; offg. under sec., railway branch, 1873-75; suptg. engr., June, 1874; acted as mint master, Calcutta, 1876; and as consulting engr., Lucknow, 1877, Lahore, 1884, and Calcutta, 1885; chief engr., May, 1886; ret'd., Jan., 1890.

LUCAS, ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1883, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., forest settlt. officer, and W. Bhil agent; first asst., May, 1894; dep. commr., March, 1896; acting judl. commr., June, 1898.

LUCAS, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 19th Nov., 1888, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; employed in Sind from Nov., 1891; on special plague duty in Karachi, March, 1897; 1st asst., July, 1897.

LUCKSTEDT, HENRY, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 10th Oct., 1871, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., May, 1884.

LUDLOW, EDMOND SAMUEL, C.I.E., Col., I.S.C., late Haidarabad Administration.—Served with the Madras native infantry from 1858 to 1864, and with the Saugor field divn. in 1859; from Oct., 1864, served in Mysore and Coorg as asst. supt., small cause court judge, asst. commr., and cantonmt. mag.; services lent to the Nizam's govt. and apptd. judge of the suburban court, Haidarabad, Feb., 1884; insp.-gen. of police to the Nizam's govt., April, 1884; rank of dep. commr., May, 1887; returned to Europe, Feb., 1896.

LUGARD, HENRY IBBETSON, Col., I.S.C., late Central Prov. Commn.—First commn., Dec., 1853; employed under the mil. dept. till 1864, also serving in the Madras police in 1857 and 1860-61; from Jan., 1864, served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., cantonmt. mag., and dep. commr.; reverted to mil. duty, Jan., 1893.

LUKE, STEPHEN PAGET WALTER VYVYAN, C.I.E., M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the dept., 16th Jan., 1868; had charge of field telegraph with Peshawar valley force, 1878, and with Afghanistan force, 1879; C.I.E., Jan., 1880; supt., April, 1880; dir., construction branch, April, 1890; dep. dir.-gen. of telegraphs, April, 1895; ret'd., March, 1897.

LUKER, FRANK (supt., govt. press, N.W.P.).—Apptd. supt., govt. press, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Sept., 1893.

LUMSDEN, JOHN JAMES FOOTE, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 16th Nov., 1856, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settlt. officer, and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., Gorakhpur, July, 1874; commr., Dec., 1879; senr. member of board of rev., Nov., 1889; member of provl. legisl. council, 1891; ret'd., Nov., 1891.

LUMSDEN, OSWALD FARQUHAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh University and Christ Church,

Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 30th Nov., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

LUMSDEN, SIR PETER STARK, G.C.B., C.S.I., Gen., late Council of India.—Ensign, Bengal army, Dec., 1847; asst. exec. engr., 1849; served in various expeds. against the frontier tribes in 1852-56; on a special mission to Afghanistan, 1857-58 (medal and clasp); asst. quartermaster-gen., Sept., 1858; served with China expdn. in 1860 (medal and 2 clasps); dep. quartermaster-gen., April, 1863; with Bhutan field force in 1865 (clasp); quartermaster-gen., May, 1868; offg. rest., Haidarabad, 1872; adjutant-gen., Sept., 1874; chief of the staff, 1879; member of council of India, Dec., 1883, to Dec., 1893; Afghan boundary commr., 1884-85.

LUPTON, WALTER JAMES EDWIN, Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the City of London Sch., London Univ., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 17th Jan., 1895, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., June, 1899.

LUSHINGTON, EDWARD HARBORD, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury in 1841; arrived, 22nd June, 1841; served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag., joint mag., and mag.; dep. collr. of govt. sea customs, Calcutta, 1855; collr., Nuddea, 1856; junr. sec., board of rev., Lower Provinces, 1857; acting commr. of Bardwan, Manbhum, and Singbhum, 1857-58 (medal); commr. of rev. and circuit, Nuddea, 1861; sec. to govt. of Bengal, 1861; sec. to govt. of India in the finl. dept., govt. dir. of Bank of Bengal, and member of mint committee, 1863; ret'd., 1871.

LUSON, HEWLING, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. sec., to govt. of India, home dept.).—Educ. at St. Olave's, Southwark, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1832; arrived, 30th Dec., 1834, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; under sec. to govt., jud., &c., depts., Oct., 1891; under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., March, 1893; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1895; mag. and collr., Dec., 1895; dep. commr., Nov., 1896; dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Nov., 1897.

LUTTMAN-JOHNSON, HENRY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Winchester, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., offg. private sec. to lieut.-govr., and acting under sec. to govt.; transfd. to Assam, Feb., 1874, as dep. commr., and sec. to chief commr.; judge and commr., Assam valley districts, May, 1886; retransfd. to Bengal as commr., Nov., 1892; ret'd., Jan., 1898.

LYALL, SIR ALFRED COMYNS, G.C.I.E., K.C.B., D.C.L., late Indian C.S. (member of the Council of India).—Apptd. to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury, 1855; arrived, 2nd Jan., 1856, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; transfd. to Central Prov., April, 1864, and served as dep. commr., offg. sec. to chief commr., and offg. commr. of the Nagpur divn.; transfd. to Haidarabad assigned districts, July, 1867, and apptd. commr., W. Berar, Aug., 1867; sec. to the govt. of India, home dept., March, 1873; from Nov., 1874, served in the foreign dept., and was apptd. agent to the

govr.-gen. in Rajputana, and chief commr. of Ajmir; on special duty in England, Aug., 1876; sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., April, 1878; on special pol. duty in Kabul, March, 1880, and again in Sept., 1880; K.C.B., May, 1881; lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov., and chief commr. of Oudh, April, 1882; C.I.E., May, 1882; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887; retd., Dec., 1887; apptd. member of the council of India, 17th Jan., 1888; vice-prest., 1895-96; D.C.L. (Oxon.), 1889; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1896; author of 'Rajputana Gazetteer,' 'Berar Gazetteer,' 'Asiatic Studies,' 'Manual of the Law of the Hyderabad Assigned Districts,' 'Life of Warren Hastings,' 'Verses written in India,' 'Natural Religion in India' (Rede Lecture for 1891), and 'The Rise of the British Dominion in India.'

LYALL, SIR CHARLES JAMES, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., LL.D., late Indian C.S. (sec., jud. and public dept., India Office).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., King's Coll., London, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and offg. under sec.; asst. under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., April-June, 1872; under sec. in the dept. of rev., agric., and commerce, Sept., 1873; C.I.E., Jan., 1880; offg. sec. to chief commr., Assam, April, 1880; offg. judge and commr., Assam valley districts, 1883 and 1888; sec. to chief commr., Oct., 1883; offg. sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agricl. dept., Feb.-Nov., 1886, and in the home dept., Aug., 1889; on special duty to report on penal settlt., Port Blair, Dec., 1889; sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Nov., 1890; C.S.I., June, 1893; offg. chief commr. of Assam, July-Oct., 1894; chief commr. of Central Prov., Dec., 1895; K.C.S.I., June, 1897; retd., July, 1898; apptd. sec. judl. and public dept., India Office, Aug., 1898; author of article on 'Hindustani Literature' in Encyclopædia Britannica, 1880; 'Sketch of the Hindustani Language,' 1880; 'Translations of Ancient Arabic Poetry,' 1885; 'Guide to the Transliteration of Hindu and Muhammadan Names in the Bengal Army,' 1885; and has edited (in Arabic) Tibrizi's commentary on ten ancient Arabic poems, 1894.

LYALL, DAVID ROBERT, C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 25th Sept., 1861; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and mag. and collr.; insp.-gen. of police, March, 1883; commr. of Chittagong, Aug., 1887; offd. as pol. officer with the Lushai expedi. force in 1889; C.S.I., Jan., 1891; member of rev. board, May, 1892; member of Bengal legisl. council, June, 1893; on special duty in mil. dept., April-Nov., 1894; retd., April, 1896.

LYALL, FRANK FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 11th Oct., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt. of Bengal, genl. dept., May, 1899; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1899.

LYALL, SIR JAMES BROADWOOD, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury in 1858; arrived, 8th April, 1858, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., settlt. officer, and dep. commr.; settlt. commr., Multan and Derajat, March, 1874; finl. commr., Punjab, Jan., 1879;

rest. in Mysore and chief commr. of Coorg, Dec., 1883; also jud. commr. of Coorg; lieut.-govr. of the Punjab, April, 1887, to Nov., 1892, when he retd. from the service; K.C.S.I., June, 1888; G.C.I.E., May, 1892; member of the royal commn. on opium, 1893-95.

LYLE, DAVID RANKEN, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Acadl. Instn., Coleraine, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 30th Oct., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Oct., 1899.

LYLE, HENRY WARD, LL.B., Indian C.S. (supt., Dehra Dun, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—(Educ. at Winchester, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 24th Nov., 1887, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Oct., 1891; dep. supt., family domains of Maharaja of Benares, April-July, 1892; district and sessions judge, Feb., 1897; supt., Dehra Dun, Nov., 1899.

LYON, ISIDORE BERNADOTTE, C.I.E., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Indian med. service, 31st March, 1865; apptd. profir., Grant med. coll., and chemical analyser to govt., Jan., 1867; was also third physician at the Jamsetji Jijibhoj hosp. 1867-72, and profir. at the Elphinstone coll. from Dec., 1875; on special duty conducting petroleum experiments, 1882; C.I.E., May, 1889; retd., April, 1892.

LYON, PERCY COMYN, Indian C.S. (dir. of land records and agriculture, Bengal).—Educ. at King's Sch., Bruton, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 1st Jan., 1884, and served in Assam as asst. commr. and personal asst. to chief commr.; offd. as private sec. to lieut.-govr. of Bengal, 1888 and 1890; under sec. to govt. of Bengal, jud., &c. depts., June, 1890; offg. under sec. to the govt. of India, rev. and agricl. dept., April, 1891; asst. settlt. officer, Cuttack, Nov., 1891; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1891; employed on N. Behar survey, as asst. supt., Oct., 1892; also settlt. officer, Saran and Champaran, Feb., 1895; mag. and collr., April, 1895; dir. of land records and agric., Nov., 1897.

LYONS, ROBERT WILLIAM STEELE, Major, Indian Med. Service (Professor, Grant Med. Coll., Bombay).—First commn., 1st April, 1882; in mil. employ till Jan., 1897; acted as 1st physician Jamsetji Jeejeebhoy hosp., and as profir. Grant med. coll. in 1893 and 1894; confid. Jan., 1897; prest. plague committee, Jan., 1897.

LYS, WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Bath Coll., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 10th Dec., 1893, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

LYTTELTON, HON. NEVILLE GERALD, C.B., Lieut.-Genl., late Rifle Brigade.—First commn., 17th Jan., 1865; A.D.C. to lord lieut. of Ireland, 1869-73; served in the Jowaki expedi. of 1877 (medal with clasp); private sec. to sec. of state for war, 1880-82; A.D.C. to chief of staff, expedi. force, Egypt, 1882 (medal with clasp, bronze star, 4th class Osmanieh); asst. mil. sec. to govr. of Gibraltar, 1883-85; mil. sec. to govr. of Bombay, March, 1885, to April, 1890; asst. adjutant-gen., head quarters (England), Dec., 1894; asst. mil. sec. (War Office), Oct., 1897; served

as brigadier-gen. in the Soudan campaign of 1898 (capture of Omdurman); maj.-gen. comdg. a divn. at Aldershot, Sept., 1899; comdg. a brigade in S. Africa, Oct., 1899; local lieut.-gen. comdg. a divn., March, 1900.

MARTHUR, ALEXANDER DONALD, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., March, 1869, and employed on irrign. works in Bengal; exec. engr., Sept., 1872; offg. under sec. to govt. of Bengal, public works dept., 1885 and 1887; suptg. engr., July, 1891; chief engr. and sec. to govt. of Bengal, irrign. branch, Jan., 1896 to Jan., 1900.

MACARTHUR, RIGHT REV. JAMES, D.D., Bombay Ecclesl. Establ. (Bishop of Bombay).—Apptd. Bishop of Bombay, 1898; arrived, Nov., 1898.

MACAULIFFE, MICHAEL, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Newcastle Sch., Limerick, Springfield Coll., and Queen's Coll., Galway; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 9th Feb., 1864, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and jud. asst.; dep. commr., Dec., 1882; divl. judge, Nov., 1884; retd., April, 1893; author of 'Life of Baba Nanak,' and of articles on the Sikh religion in the 'Calcutta Review,' 1880-81.

MACAULAND, REDMOND CONYNGHAM SAMUEL, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Jud. Dept., Punjab (cantonment mag., Ferozepore).—Lieut., 13th Nov., 1872; arrived, 9th March, 1873, and served in the mil. dept., and in the Punjab as cantonmt. mag.; 1st class cantonmt. mag., Dec., 1887.

MCBLAINE, FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at private schs. and at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 29th Nov., 1886, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; employed in Burma and Assam as asst. commr., from Jan., 1890; dep. commr., April, 1896; mag. and collr., Mar., 1899; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1899.

MCALLUM, EDWIN, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the Wesleyan Connexional Sch., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 31st Oct., 1870, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; first asst. collr., April, retd., March, 1893.

MCCARTHY, SAMUEL TRANT, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at St. Patrick's Coll., Carlow; apptd. after exam. of 1862; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1882; retd., Jan., 1891.

MCCARTHY, The Ven. WELBORE, late Ecclesl. Dept., Bengal.—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 20th Dec., 1873; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1883; archdeacon of Calcutta, Aug., 1892; retd., Dec., 1898.

MACCARTIE, FREDERICK FITZGERALD, C.I.E., M.B., B.Ch. (Dub.), Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (assay master, Calcutta Mint).—Joined the service, 30th Mar., 1878; arrived 7th Nov., 1878, and served on gen., mil., and jail duty and as dep. sanitary commr.; apptd. health officer of port of Bombay, June, 1884; served as assay prob. in Bombay in 1886 and 1889; reverted to his appt. as health officer, Jan., 1890; acted as dep. assay master, Bombay mint, in 1891, 1892, and 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1898; assay master, Calcutta mint, May, 1900.

MCCLOGHRY, JAMES, F.R.C.S.I., Lt.-Col., Indian Med. Service, Bombay (civil surgn., Karachi).—Entered the service 31st Mar., 1875;

mil. duty till March, 1891; civil surgn., Karachi, March, 1891; med. officer, Kathiawar agency, Nov., 1895; reverted to Karachi, March, 1897.

MCCOLL, HUGH ERNEST, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1891, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

MCCOLLUM, FLORENCE AUGUSTUS (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 31st Dec., 1894.

MCCONAGHEY, JOHN, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (civil surgn., Lucknow).—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; from April, 1876, served as civil surgn. and supt. of jail at various stations; civil surgn. and supt. Lucknow Lunatic Asylum, Jan., 1895.

MCCONAGHY, WILLIAM, M.D., Col., Indian Med. Service, Bombay (principal med. officer, Sind).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1869; supt. of Matheran, Sept., 1877, and Mahabaleshwar, Oct., 1878; civil surgn., Karwar, 1882-83; Dharwar, Feb., 1885; Poona, Oct., 1892; principal med. officer, Sind dist., Sept., 1898.

MCCORKELL, GILMOUR, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Foyle Coll., Londonderry, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 11th Nov., 1872, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge; senr. asst. judge, April, 1890; judge and sessions judge, June, 1894; retd., July, 1897.

MCCUDDEN, EDMUND GERALD JAMES, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., Jan., 1870, and employed on irrign. and mil. works in Rajputana; transfld. to state railways, July, 1875; exec. engr., Nov., 1880; engr.-in-chief, Cuttack-Midnapur-Calcutta railway survey, March, 1894; N. Bengal-Assam connection survey, Nov., 1894; retd., 1896.

MCULLAGH, JAMES ROBERT, Col., R.E., late India Survey Dept.—First commn., 18th Dec., 1861; arrived, 20th Oct., 1865; apptd. to the public works dept., Nov., 1868, and to the great trigonl. survey of India, March, 1869; asst. supt., Jan., 1872; dep. supt., July, 1882; supt. (new scale), Aug., 1892; retd., Jan., 1895.

MCDERMOTT, BENJAMIN KENICOTT SELBY (dep. commr., Burma).—Joined the service, 20th Dec., 1872; asst. supt. of police, Oct., 1880, and district supt., Oct., 1882; apptd. to the commn. as asst. commr., Jan., 1887; dep. commr., Feb., 1891.

MACDONALD, ANGUS RODERICK, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1879, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Sept., 1888.

MACDONALD, DENIS PETER, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (senr. med. officer, Port Blair).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1873; served in the Jowaki and Afghan campaigns; on special duty in Egypt, 1883; served with the field hosp., Lushai expedn., Nov., 1889; senr. med. officer, Port Blair, July, 1890.

MACDONALD, DONALD, Col., I.S.C., late Survey of India Dept.—First commn., Bengal infantry, Oct., 1853; arrived, Dec., 1853; apptd. to the survey dept., rev. branch, Dec., 1860; rev. surveyor, Jan., 1865; dep. supt., April, 1866; in charge of the N.W. frontier survey, 1879-84; asst. surveyor-gen., Sept., 1887; reverted to mil. duty, 1890.

MACDONALD, FREDERICK WESTON PEILE,

Captain, I.S.C. (pol. agent, Zhob)—First commn., Oct., 1881; joined the Staff Corps, June, 1883; entered the pol. dept., April, 1888; actg. pol. agent in Zhob, Dec., 1897; confd., March, 1899.

MACDONALD, ROBERT GRAHAM, late India Public Works Dept.—Served in the subord. accts. establ., Oct., 1863, and subsequently as exam. of railway accts., asst. acctnt.-gen., Calcutta, &c.; dep. acctnt.-gen. and under sec. to govt. of India, Sept., 1888; prest. of public works accts. committee, Oct., 1888; and of the railway accts. committee, Oct., 1890; acctnt.-gen. and dep. sec., July, 1893; ret'd., Dec., 1897; has received the Burma war medal.

MACDONALD, WILLIAM, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.)—Apptd. to the dept., Oct., 1876; asst. engr., June, 1877; exec. engr., Nov., 1880.

MACDONELL, ALEXANDER, M.A. (prof'r., Sibpur engrg. coll., Bengal)—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the dept., 30th Aug., 1879, and served in succession as prof'r. at Patna, Dacca, Presidency, and Hugli colls.; prof'r., Sibpur engrg. coll., Nov., 1890; offg. principal, April, 1897.

MACDONNELL, SIR ANTONY PATRICK, G.C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh)—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Galway; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1865, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., offg. mag. and collr., and offg. acctnt.-gen., Bengal; sec., govt. of Bengal, rev. and gen. depts., July, 1884; member of provl. legisl. council, Nov., 1884; sec., govt. of India, home dept. March, 1887; C.S.I., June, 1888; offg. chief commr., Burma, Sept., 1889; chief commr., Central Prov., Nov., 1890; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1893; acting lieut.-govr. of Bengal, May, 1893; member of govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1893; lieut.-govr. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Nov., 1895; G.C.S.I., June, 1897; author of 'Food Grain Supply and Famine Relief in Behar and Bengal,' 1876.

McDOWALL, ARTHUR CARLAW (2nd class clerk, Store Dept., India Office)—Clerk, 2nd Class, 26th Oct., 1898.

McDOWELL, GEORGE RONALD, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.)—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 26th Sept., 1864, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab, and in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., March, 1874.

MACFARLAN, JOHN, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1857; arrived, 21st Dec., 1857, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; dep. collr. and mag., in Sind, 1860-61; second asst. collr. and mag., 1862-64; first asst., July, 1869; acted as postmr.-gen., Bombay, and was apptd. postmr.-gen., Madras, in 1875; offg. postmr.-gen., Bengal, 1877-78; ret'd., Dec., 1878.

MCGANN, TERENCE JOSEPH, Col., Indian Med. Service, Madras (principal med. officer, Bangalore dist.)—Joined the service, 1st April, 1867; senr. surgn., Bangalore, and sanitary commr., Mysore, Sept., 1870; principal med. officer, Rangoon dist., April, 1895.

MAGGEORGE, HENRY JOHN, late Central Prov. Commn.—Joined the service, 10th June, 1854; dep. collr., Betul, Nov., 1856; asst. commr., Wardha, Dec., 1860; in charge of settlt. duties, Wardha and Chanda, 1862-63; dep. commr., Jan., 1877; ret'd., March, 1890.

MACGREGOR, ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma)—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ. and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 3rd Dec., 1898, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

MACGREGOR, EDWIN GEORGE LISTER LAIRD, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bombay)—Educ. at the Charterhouse and Trin. Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 3rd Dec., 1898, and served in Bombay.

McHUTCHIN, WILLIAM, India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.)—Trained at Madras Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd., 13th Oct., 1874, and posted to Mysore; exec. engr., June, 1887; services lent to Mysore state, 1881-87, and again from June, 1889; asst. chief engr. and asst. sec. to govt., June, 1889; local suptg. engr., Oct., 1894.

McINTOSH, HENRY JAMES, Indian C.S. (dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Bengal)—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad. and Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 25th Nov., 1886, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., finl. and municipal depts., Oct., 1892; employed temporarily in fin. dept., govt. of India, in 1893 and 1894; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1894; mag. and collr., April, 1897; joint sec. to rev. board, April, 1900; dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Sept., 1900.

McIVER, CHARLES (dist. supt., Bombay Police)—Joined the service as asst. supt. of police, Karachi, 10th March, 1867; supt., March, 1873; district supt., Aug., 1875.

MACIVER, STEWART WILLIAM GRAHAM IVER, Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras)—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 20th Dec., 1892, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Dec., 1899.

MACIVOR, CHARLES VERNON, M.A., L.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Dec., 1867, and posted to Central Prov.; transfed. to state railways, Jan., 1870; exec. engr., April, 1875; offg. engr.-in-chief, Rajputana-Malwa railway, May, 1894; ret'd., Aug., 1896.

MACKAY, DUNCAN LACHLAN MACPHERSON, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma)—Educ. at Madras Coll. and Univ., St. Andrew's; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 8th Nov., 1875, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; transfed. to Burma, July, 1876; cantonmt. mag., Rangoon, May, 1888; dep. commr., Aug., 1892; reverted to asst. commr., Sept., 1897.

MACKAY, HENRY FISHER (2nd class clerk, Finl. Dept., India Office)—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen; clerk, 2nd class, 2nd Oct., 1895.

McKAY, HENRY KELLOCK, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (civil surgn., Jubbulpore)—Entered service, Sept., 1873; civ. surgn. and supt. of lunatic asylum, Jubbulpore, Oct., 1894; C.I.E., Dec., 1898.

MACKAY, SIR JAMES LYLE, K.C.I.E. (member of the Council of India)—Chairman of the Bengal chamber of commerce, 1889 to 1893; member of the viceroy's legisl. council, 1891 to 1893; apptd. member of council of the sec. of state, 27th April, 1897.

McKEE, JOHN ALEXANDER, late Forest

Dept., Cent. Prov.—Entered the service, 2nd Oct., 1869; served in the Forest dept. as sub-assst., asst., and dep.-conservator; conservator, Feb., 1894.

MACKENNA, JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Dumfries Acad., Edinburgh Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and asst. settl. officer.

MACKENZIE, SIR ALEXANDER, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Edward VI.'s Gr. Sch., Birmingham, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 11th Dec., 1862, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; from Feb., 1866, served as under sec. and junr. sec. to govt. of Bengal; on famine duty, 1873; offg. sec., board of rev., 1875 and 1877; mag. and collr., Murshidabad, July, 1876; junr. sec., board of rev., May, 1877; sec., govt. of Bengal, finl. dept., Oct., 1877; member of provl. legisl. council, Jan., 1879; sec., govt. of India, home dept., April, 1882; C.S.I., May, 1886; chief commr., Central Prov., March, 1887; chief commr. of Burma, Dec., 1890; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1891; member of govr.-gen.'s council, April, 1895; lieut.-govr. of Bengal, Dec., 1895; retd., April, 1898; author of 'History of the Relations of the Govt. with the Hill Tribes of the N.E. Frontier of Bengal.'

MACKENZIE, ARCHIBALD THOMAS, A.M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1881; exec. engr., Feb., 1894.

MACKENZIE, GORDON THOMSON, Indian C.S. (district and sessions judge, Madras).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 19th Nov., 1869, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., asst. commr., head asst. and acting registrar, high court; acting sec., board of rev., Nov., 1884; prest. of the municipal commn., Jan., 1885; sub-collr. and joint mag., Nov., 1886; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1888; compiled district manual of Kistna.

MACKENZIE, KENNETH JAMES LOCH, C.I.E., Col., I.S.C., late Berar Administration.—Joined Madras artillery from Addiscombe, 11th June, 1858; from April, 1862, served in the Haidarabad assigned districts, as asst. commr., asst. conservator of forests, and dep. commr.; jud. commr., July, 1889; commr., March, 1892; C.I.E., Jan., 1893; offg. rest., Haidarabad, March, 1895; reverted to mil. dept., Oct., 1895.

MACKENZIE, MALCOLM DOUGLAS (dep. commr., Sind).—Entered the service, Aug., 1877; asst. collr., Dec., 1885; dep. commr., Upper Sind Frontier, May, 1898.

MACKENZIE, NICOL FINLAYSON, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1878, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh, irrigation branch; exec. engr., June, 1892; under sec. to govt. of India, Aug., 1897.

MACKENZIE, THOMAS DUNCAN, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; sec., Baroda inquiry commn., Nov., 1873; under sec. to govt. in the rev., &c., depts., Nov., 1877; junr. collr. and mag.,

Broach, May, 1884; collr., mag., and pol. agent, Khandesh, Feb., 1886; acted as chief sec. to govt. of Bombay for various periods; commr., S. divn., May, 1889; chief sec. to govt., rev., finl. and gen. depts., May, 1891; acting commr. of customs, &c., Bombay, March, 1893; retd., May, 1897.

McKERRON, ROBERT GORDON, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Kelso High Sch., Edinburgh Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 15th Dec., 1894, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settl. offr.

MACKIE, ANDREW WALLACE, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Univs. of Edinburgh and St. Andrew's; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 20th Oct., 1873, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine duty, 1874 and 1877; asst. to dir.-gen. of statistics, Feb., 1875; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1884; employed in the Punjab in 1889 as divl. judge; district and sessions judge, Feb., 1890; retd., July, 1898.

MACKINTOSH, JAMES SIMON, M.A., B.L., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Royal Acad., Inverness, Edinburgh Univ., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; called to the bar at the Middle Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. sec. to govt.; private sec. to the lieut.-govr., 1874; offg. sec. to govt., 1875; junr. sec. to govt., July, 1876; sec. to board of rev., Aug., 1877; mag. and collr., Jan., 1886; commr., Lucknow, April, 1895; retd., Oct., 1896.

MACLAGAN, EDWARD DOUGLAS, M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 18th Dec., 1886, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and offg. under sec.; supt. of census operations in the Punjab, 1890; under sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agricl. dept., April, 1893; retransfd. to the Punjab, as asst. commr., March, 1895; dep. commr., Oct., 1896.

MACLARAN, FRANCIS BLANEY, M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (supig. engr.).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1872, as local fund engr., Thana; asst. engr., April, 1875; exec. engr., Oct., 1880; suptg. engr., June, 1890.

McLAUGHLIN, FREDERICK HUBERT, LL.M., B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Shrewsbury Gr. Sch., and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 21st Jan., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; from April, 1876, served in the jud. dept., and was apptd. district and sessions judge, 1st grade, March, 1884; retd., March, 1890.

MACLEAN, CHARLES SMITH, C.B., C.I.E., Maj.-Gen., I.S.C., late Pol. Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 14th Dec., 1853; served in the Indian mutiny, 1857, China war, 1860, Afghan war, 1878-80, and Mashud-Waziri expedn., 1881; consul-gen. at Meshed, March, 1889; agent to govr.-gen., and consul-gen. for Khorassan and Seistan, Nov., 1889; returned to Europe, Dec., 1891.

MACLEAN, SIR FRANCIS WILLIAM, Kt., K.C.I.E., Q.C. (Chief Justice, Calcutta High Court).—Called to the Bar (Inner Temple), 1868; M.P. for Mid-Oxfordshire, 1885-1891; master in lunacy, 1891-1896; appointed Chief Justice,

Calcutta High Court, Nov., 1896; chairman of the Indian committee of the Indian charitable famine relief fund of 1897; K.C.I.E., 1898.

MACLEAN, FREDERICK GURR, M.I.E.E. (dir.-gen. of telegraphs, India Telegraph Dept.).—Apptd. to the dept., 1st Sept., 1868; personal asst. to dir. of telegraphs, April, 1890; in charge, Sind and Baluchistan divn., Feb., 1892; in charge, check office, March, 1893; dir., April, 1895; dep. dir.-gen., May, 1897; dir.-gen., May, 1900.

MCLEAN, JOHN JAMES, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (district and sessions judge, N.W.
