(member of the Council of India).—Educ. at Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. to the Bombay civil service after exam. of 1855; arrived 8th Oct., 1856, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on special duty for inquiry into claims of thakurs of Bhaunagar on account of land and sea customs, May, 1859; specially apptd. to inquire into condition of talukdars, Ahmadabad, Jan., 1860; alienation settlt. officer, Gujarat, April, 1861; asst. to rev. commr., Dec., 1861; under sec. to govt. of Bombay, rev. dept., April, 1862; on special duty for carrying out provisions of bill for relief of the talukdars, Ahmadabad, Dec., 1862; commr. for revising subord. civil establs., April, 1866; registrar-gen. of assurances, July, 1866; dir. of public instrn., April, 1869; acting municipal commr., Bombay, May-Oct., 1872; acting pol. agent, Kathiawar, Jan., 1873; on duty at Delhi in connection with imperial assemblage, Dec., 1876; acting commr. in Sind, June, 1878; member of famine commn., 1878-79; acting sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts., March, 1879; from April, 1879, served as sec. in the rev., finl., and gen. depts., and acted as chief sec.; on special duty on famine commn. in England, 1879-80; on special duty in India, 1880 and 1881; member of council, Bombay, Dec., 1882; acted as govr. and prest. in council in March, 1885; temporary member of govr.-gen.'s council, Oct., 1886; ret'd., Nov., 1887; member of council of India, 12th Nov., 1887; vice-pres., 1894-95; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1888; member of royal commn. on Indian expenditure, May, 1895.

PEILE, SOLOMON CHARLES FREDERICK, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Burma Police (inspr.-gen. police).—First commn., 9th Aug., 1874; served in the Afghan war of 1878-79 (medal), and in the Burmese expdn. of 1886-87 (medal and clasp); apptd. dir. of police supply and clothing, Upper Burma, March, 1887, the office being subsequently extended to the whole of Burma; dep. inspr.-gen., mil. police, June, 1890; inspr.-gen. of police, Feb., 1892; C.I.E., Jan., 1901.

PEMBERTON, ROBERT CHARLES BOILEAU, C.S.I., Major-Gen., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Lieut., Bengal engrs., 1853; joined the dept., May, 1857; served during the Mutiny (medal and two clasps), and subsequently in the road dept., Oudh, as exec. engr.; asst. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., March, 1865; offg. suptg. engr., railway branch, Haidarabad, Sept., 1870; dir. of stores, railway branch, May, 1877; consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Lucknow, 1880; chief engr., 1st class, March, 1886; dir.-gen. of railways and dep. sec. to govt. of India, railway branch, Feb., 1887; sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., April, 1887; reverted to mil. dept., 1890; offg. member of viceroy's council, Feb., 1891, to April, 1892; ret'd. from the army, Nov., 1892; C.S.I., May, 1894.

PEMBERTON, WILLIAM WALKER, Col., I.S.C., late Burma Commn.—First commn., July, 1853; arrived, 25th Sept., 1853; served in the Burma commn. from Dec., 1859, as asst. commr., dep. commr., small cause court judge, and boundary officer; reverted to mil. duty, 1890.

PENNELLY, AUBRAY PERCIVAL, M.A., Indian C.S. (district and sessions judge, Bengal).—

Educ. at private schools and at Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 25th Nov., 1886, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and as comptr. of Indian treasuries; transfed. to Burma, Oct., 1889, as asst. commr.; under sec. to chief commr., Oct., 1890; dep. commr., Jan., 1892; settlt. officer, 1892-93; returned to Bengal as joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1895; mag. and collr., June, 1896; district and sessions judge, June, 1897.

PENNELL, CHARLES STUART, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Edinburgh univ. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 10th Dec., 1897, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

PENNINGTON, HENRY FRANCIS DUMARESQ, Indian C.S. (district and sessions judge, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Westminster; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 21st Oct., 1875, and served in Oudh as asst. commr. and sub-judge; joint mag., April, 1887; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1890; also addl. judge, Moradabad, 1893-94.

PENNINGTON, JAMES BURN, B.L., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Sedbergh Gr. Sch., and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., sub-collr., sub-sec. to the board of rev., and acting collr. of sea customs at Madras; collr. and mag., July, 1875; ret'd., Sept., 1888.

PENNY, EDMUND, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and posted to state railways; transfed. to Central Prov., Aug., 1875; exec. engr., May, 1883; planned and carried out scheme for Nagpur water-works, 1890; acted as asst. sec. to chief commr. in 1891; suptg. engr. and sec. to chief commr., Jan., 1899; C.I.E., May, 1900.

PENNY, JAMES CHAMPION, M.D., Dep.-Surgn.-Gen., late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Arrived, 3rd June, 1857; from Feb., 1863, served in the Punjab at various stations as civil asst. surgn., and civil surgn. and sunt. of jail; ret'd., June, 1887.

PENNYCUICK, JOHN, C.S.I., Col., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—Lieut., Madras engrs., Dec., 1858; arrived, 11th Nov., 1860; served in the dept. from April, 1862; exec. engr., Dec., 1866; served in the Abyssinian expdn., 1867-68 (medal); suptg. engr., Oct., 1881; dep. chief engr., and under sec. to govt., Jan., 1883; supt. of works, tank maintenance scheme, April, 1884; on special duty to England to select machinery for Periyar works project, July, 1888; chief engr. and sec. in the public works dept., July, 1890; member of legisl. council, Nov., 1893; C.S.I., Oct., 1895; ret'd., Jan., 1896; presdt. of Royal Indian Engrg. Coll., Cooper's Hill, Sept., 1896, to Sept., 1899.

PERCIVAL, PHILIP EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. coll. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at the Charterhouse and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 25th Nov., 1895, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

PERKINS, SIR AENEAS, K.C.B., Gen., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 1851; joined the dept., March, 1855, and served in the Punjab; served during the Mutiny, 1857-58 (medal and clasp); asst. principal and prof'r., Calcutta civil engrg. coll., 1861; served with the Bhutan field force, 1864-65; acting suptg. engr.,

N.W. Prov., Oct., 1870; from April, 1872, served in the mil. works branch as suptg. engr.; was also on active service, Oct., 1878, as commanding engr., field force, Cabul, and in Oct., 1879, Kuram divn. (medal and four clasps and bronze star); C.B., 1879; A.D.C. to the Queen, March, 1881; acting dep. inspr.-gen. of mil. works, 1881; chief engr. and sec. to the chief commr., Central Prov., public works dept., Feb., 1883; chief engr. and sec., Punjab, April, 1886; reverted to mil. duty, 1889; K.C.B., June, 1897.

PERKINS, NORMAN CHICHESTER, Major, I.S.C. late Burma commn.—First commn., 13th Aug., 1879; served in Afghan campaign of 1880-81, and in Burma, 1886-89 (medal and clasp); joined the commn. as asst. commr., Aug., 1886; dep. commr., Oct., 1896; ret'd., April, 1900.

PERRAM, GEORGE JAMES, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Apptd. asst. engr., 21st Dec., 1868, and posted to Mysore; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; transfed. to Central Prov., March, 1881; to Coorg, Jan., 1887; and to Burma, Feb., 1893; suptg. engr., Jan., 1896.

PERRIN, CHARLES, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 10th Oct., 1874, and employed on irrign. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., May, 1883; under sec. to govt., Feb., 1892; sanitary engr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, with rank of suptg. engr., July, 1893; supt. of practical training of passed students at Cooper's Hill Coll., 1896-7; ret'd., April, 1899.

PERROTT, FINCH (senr. clerk, acctng.-gen.'s dept., India Office).—Junr. clerk, 24th Dec., 1880; senr. clerk, May, 1900.

PERRY, FRANCIS FREDERIC, F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service, (profr., Mayo hosp., Lahore).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1879; from March, 1882, served in the Punjab as civil surgn., prof'r., Lahore med. coll., and chemical examn. to govt.; prof'r. of surgery, and surgn. to Mayo hosp., Lahore, Dec., 1883; acted as prof'r., Calcutta med. coll., in 1894.

PERT, FREDERICK JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint sec. to rev. board, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 15th Nov., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Feb., 1893; settlt. officer, Nov., 1894; dep. commr., July, 1898; joint sec. to rev. board, March, 1899.

PESTONJI, HORMASJI PATAK, B.A., L.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Entered the dept. as asst. engr., Feb., 1877; exec. engr., Nov., 1891.

PETERS, CHARLES JASPER, M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 26th July, 1867, as proby. asst. engr.; exec. engr., April, 1875; ret'd., Nov., 1894.

PETERS, EDWARD NICOLLS, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the Bombay public works dept. as exec. engr., 11th March, 1879; transfed. to state railways, Feb., 1880; served in the Afghan war, 1880-81 (medal); posted to Burma, Sept., 1881; employed on Sind-Pishin railway, April, 1885; transfed. to Central Prov., March, 1888; in 1891 acted as public works sec.; ret'd., Nov., 1895.

PETERSON, FRANCIS WILLIAM, late Mint Dept., Bombay.—Joined the mint dept., Calcutta; apptd. dep. assay master, Oct., 1881;

dep. assay master, Bombay mint, Oct., 1837; retd., Nov., 1892.

PETERSON, FREDERICK WILLIAM VOYSEY, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Leicester Coll. Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 7th Dec., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and dep. commr.; from May, 1877, served in the jud. dept., and was apptd. district and sessions judge, 1st grade, Sept., 1885; retd., April, 1889.

PETERSON, HERBERT PARSONS, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Loughborough Gr. Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 10th Nov., 1875; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam as district and sessions judge, Sylhet; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1887; district and sessions judge, April, 1893; retd., Feb., 1895.

PETERSON, JOHN CARLOS KENNEDY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 6th Dec., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

PETHERAM, SIR WILLIAM COMER, KT., Q.C., B.L.—Called to the bar (Middle Temple), 1869; chief justice of the high court, N.W. Prov., 1st Nov., 1884; chief justice, Calcutta, 24th March, 1886; retd. Nov., 1896; vice-chancellor of the univ. of Calcutta, 1887-90.

PETLEY, EATON WALLACE, C.I.E., Commander R.N. (dep. conservator, port of Calcutta).—Dep. conservator of the port of Calcutta, and river surveyor, Nov., 1881; port officer and master attendant, April, 1888; hon. A.D.C. to lieut.-govr. of Bengal, and commander, Calcutta naval volunteers; C.I.E., Jan., 1897.

PETRE, FRANCIS LORAINÉ, B.L., late Indian C.S., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Educ. at Oscott Coll., and Univ. of London; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 15th Nov., 1874, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agricl. dept., Aug., 1882; junr. sec., board of rev., N.W. Prov., Nov., 1882; from April, 1885, served as asst. agent to govr.-gen. in Central India; asst. commr., March, 1888; joint mag., Dec., 1888; joint sec., board of rev., Jan., 1891; mag. and collr., July, 1891; retd., March, 1900.

PETTY, ALBERT JAMES (second class clerk, store dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 16th July, 1883.

PHEAR, SIR JOHN BUDD, KT., M.A., B.L., F.G.S.—Educ. at Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; graduated 6th wrangler in 1847, and was elected fellow, and afterwards apptd. mathematical lecturer and asst. tutor of Clare Coll.; called to the bar at the Inner Temple in 1854; judge of the high court, Calcutta, 1864-76; knighted in 1877; chief justice of Ceylon, 1877-79; unsuccessfully contested the Honiton divn. of Devon in 1885, and the Tavistock divn. in 1886; author of 'The Aryan Village in India and Ceylon,' 'International Trade, and other works.

PHELAN, ALFRED BERNARD, B.A., L.C.E., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Apptd., 15th July, 1869, and employed on irrign. work in the Punjab; exec. engr., Oct., 1878; suptg. engr., March, 1898.

PHELPS, EDWIN ASHLEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—

Educ. at Clifton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 7th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.

PHELPS, WILLIAM EDMONDSON (asst. supt., India Store Depot).—Entered the royal artillery, 30th Oct., 1869; from Feb., 1876, served in the Bombay and Bengal ordnance depts., including duty with the Quetta field force, 1878-80; supervisor, 2nd grade, India store depot, 17th June, 1884; asst. supt., 25th Dec., 1898.

PHIBBS, ALFRED (second class clerk, Mil. Dept., India Office).—Educ. at Stourbridge Gr. Sch.; clerk, 2nd class, 16th April, 1894.

PHILLIMORE, JOHN ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 12th Nov., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. officer; joint mag. and collr., Nov., 1896.

PHILLIPS, AUGUSTUS MARSHALL, Col., I.S.C., late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—Ensign, Bombay infantry, June, 1855; arrived, 13th Dec., 1855, and served with the Persian expedit. force, 1856-57 (medal and clasp); from March, 1863, served as asst. pol. supt., Savantvadi, Mahi Kantha, and Kathiawar, and held various acting appts.; pol. supt., Savantvadi, March, 1889; reverted to mil. duty, 1890.

PHILLIPS, FRANCIS ANGELO THEODORE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Winchester and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 11th Nov., 1880, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., and personal asst. to the chief commr.; judge, small cause court, Aug., 1891; commr. of excise, Oct., 1891; dep. commr., March, 1892.

PHILLIPS, WILLIAM WATKIN, Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 25th Nov., 1890, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., July, 1897.

PIGGOTT, THEODORE CARO, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Kingswood Sch., Bath, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 30th Nov., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1893.

PIGOT, JONES QUAIN, B.A., B.L., late Calcutta High Court.—Advocate, high court, Bombay, 1870-82; puisne judge, high court, Calcutta, 11th July, 1882; vice-chancellor, Calcutta univ., 1892-93; retd., March, 1896.

PIKE, HERBERT WATSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Honiton Gr. Sch., Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 17th Nov., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; under sec. to govt., Nov. 1890; joint mag., April, 1893; dep. commr., March, 1896; mag. and collr., Jan., 1900.

PILCHER, JESSE GRIGGS, F.R.C.S., Surgn. Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—First commr., 1860; from March, 1866, served as jail supt. at Allahabad and Agra, offg. sanitary commr. for Bengal, and civil surgn. at various stations; dep. surgn.-gen., Central Prov., March, 1890; inspr.-gen. of civil hosp., Bengal, April, 1893; ditto, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, May, 1894; retd., March, 1895.

PILGRIM, HERBERT WILSON, M.B., Major, Indian Med. Service (supt., presidency gen. hosp., Calcutta).—First commn., Sept., 1886; in mil. employ to June, 1890; then as acting and substantive civil surgn. at various stations; rest. surgn., presidency gen. hosp., Calcutta, April, 1892; surgn.-supt., presidency gen. hosp., June, 1898.

PIM, ALAN WILLIAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. settlt. offr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Leipzig Univ. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 4th Dec., 1895, and served in the N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; pte. sec. to lieut.-govr., May, 1898; asst. settlt. offr., Nov., 1898.

PINHEY, ALEXANDER FLEETWOOD, Capt., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent, Baghelkhand).—First commn., 10th May, 1882; from Feb., 1886, served as asst. pol. agent, Banswara, asst. gen. supt., thagi and dakaiti, and supt. for control of Moghias; pol. agent, Haraoti, March, 1885; acting pol. agent, Baghelkhand, May, 1895; confd., April, 1897.

PINHEY, ARTHUR FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1884, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and in Burma as asst. commr. (1888-90); head asst., June, 1891; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1896.

PINHEY, EDWARD, Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the Roy. Indian Engrg. Coll.; joined the service 1st Oct., 1876, and served as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1886.

PINHEY, HUGH THEODORE, M.I.E.E. (supt., India Telegraph Dept.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., Jan., 1879; in charge of telegraphs in Manipur expedn., April, 1891; supt., June, 1891.

PINKERTON, JOHN, M.D., Surgn.-Major-Gen., late Bombay Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 4th Aug., 1855; served with Indian navy, during the Persian war in 1857; on civil and mil. duty, 1858-61; acting supt. of vaccn., Sind, March, 1863; supt.-gen. of vaccn., Bombay presidency, April, 1868; surgn. in European gen. hosp. and dep. sanitary commr.; dep. surgn.-gen., Sind, Aug., 1882; surgn.-gen. of Bombay, Feb., 1888; member, legisl. council, June, 1892; ret'd., Feb., 1893.

PIPON, PHILIP JAMES GRIFFITHS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

PITCHER, DUNCAN GEORGE, Col., I.S.C., late Oudh Commn.—Arrived, as cornet, Bombay light cavalry, 3rd Sept., 1859, having previously served in the royal marine light infantry and artillery, 1856-59; served from Aug., 1868, in the Oudh commn. as asst. commr., and small cause court judge, and registrar, Lucknow; dep. dir. of agric. and commerce for Oudh, June, 1882, and for N.W. Prov. and Oudh, July, 1886; dep. commr., June, 1887; dir. of land records, Gwalior, Nov., 1890; returned to Europe, Sept., 1897.

PITMAN, CHARLES EDWARD, C.I.E., M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the dept. as asst. supt., 16th Jan., 1868; on special duty in connection with Lushai

expedn., 1871, and with Afghanistan field force, 1878-79; C.I.E., 1879; supt., April, 1880; chief supt., Punjab, April, 1891; ret'd., May, 1900.

PITTAR, CHARLES WILLIAM ERSKINE, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 31st Oct., 1884, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam (1889-95) as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1895; district and sess. judge, Dec., 1896.

PLACE, GEORGE WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B., B.L., late Indian C.S., Bengal.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 29th Nov., 1875, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; from Jan., 1886, served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; district and sessions judge, March, 1893; ret'd., Nov., 1900.

PLANCK, CHARLES, F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Gen.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establt., 4th Aug., 1855; civil asst. surgn., Fatehgarh, July, 1859; supt., central prison, Agra, Feb., 1862; inspr.-gen. of prisons, British Burma, Oct., 1864; sanitary commr., N.W. Prov., April, 1868; also in Oudh, April, 1877; also supt.-gen. of vaccn., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Jan., 1879; ret'd., Aug., 1886.

PLATEL, JOHN JOSEPH, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 14th Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1900.

PLAYFAIR, ARCHIBALD LEWIS, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, Aug., 1856; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasp); and with the Haidarabad contingent, 1862-73; served subsequently in civil employ as cantonmt. mag., small cause court judge, and jail supt.; ret'd., Feb., 1887.

PLOWDEN, CHARLES HENRY, Col., I.S.C., late Central Prov. Commn.—First commn., Feb., 1854; arrived, 28th April, 1854; served during the Mutiny, 1858-59; served from May, 1860, in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., commr. of excise, &c., and dep. commr.; offd. as commr., Jabalpur divn., 1892; reverted to mil. dept., April, 1893.

PLOWDEN, SIR HENRY MEREDYTH, Kt., B.A., B.L., late Jud. Dept., Punjab.—Served in the Punjab from 17th March, 1871, as govt. advocate and offg. judge of the chief court; confd. as judge, chief court, Jan., 1878; knighted, Feb., 1887; ret'd., April, 1894.

PLOWDEN, SIR TREVOR JOHN CHICHELE, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Winchester Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 31st Oct., 1868, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and offg. under-sec.; offg. under-sec., govt. of India, home dept., Feb., 1873; insp.-gen. of police, Assam, Nov., 1876; sec. to the prison conference, Jan., 1877; sec., govt. of India, foreign dept., Dec., 1878; rest., and pol. agent, Turkish Arabia, March, 1880; consul-gen., Baghdad, June, 1880; commr. of Ajmir-Merwara, June, 1885; rest. in Mewar, Nov., 1885; rest. in Kashmir, Nov., 1887; commr., Haidarabad assigned districts, Nov., 1888; rest., Haidarabad, March, 1892; C.S.I., Jan., 1893; K.C.S.I., May, 1898; author of several reports on journeys in Kurdistan, &c.; ret'd., Sept., 1900.

PLOWDEN, WALTER FRANCIS COURTENAY CHICHELE, Lient.-Col., I.S.C., Jud. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh (cantonmt. mag., Ranikhet).—First commn., 19th Oct., 1872; served in the Jowaki expdn., and Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal and clasp); offg. cantonmt. mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Sept., 1888; confd., May, 1890.

PLOWDEN, SIR WILLIAM CHICHELE, K.C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury Coll., 1852; arrived, 20th Sept., 1852; from Nov., 1853, served in the rev. and jud. dept. in the N.W. Prov., Oudh, and Punjab; dep. commr., Aug., 1858, and joint mag. and dep. collr., first grade, March, 1863; sec., board of rev., N.W. Prov., July, 1864; on special duty on the census report, April, 1866, and again, May, 1872; mag. and collr., March, 1870; civil and sessions judge, March, 1877; on special duty at Calcutta as prest. of census committee, Jan., 1878; commr., Meerut divn., Sept., 1879; offg. member, board of rev., Aug., 1880; census commr. for India, March, 1881; addl. member, govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1881; ret'd., July, 1885; M.P. for Wolverhampton (West), 1886-92.

POCOCK, REYNOLDS JOHN MICHEL, late Police Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Served as a volunteer during the Mutiny; joined the dept., 26th Jan., 1861; asst. district supt., Oct., 1866; district supt., Sept., 1872; ret'd., April, 1895.

POLLEN, ARTHUR DANIEL, B.A., LL.D., B.L., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Koyal Sch., Dungannon, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 26th Oct., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., asst. judge and joint sessions judge; special judge under the Deccan agriculturists relief act, Jan., 1880; judge and sessions judge, Jan., 1884; ret'd., March, 1894.

POLLEN, JOHN, B.A., LL.D., Indian C.S. (commr. of customs, Bombay).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1871, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine relief duty, 1874 and 1878; on special settlt. duty, March, 1879; acting under sec. to govt. of Bombay, pol., &c., depts., Dec., 1880; asst. commr. and branch inspr.-gen. of registration and stamps in Sind, April, 1881; also Sindi translr. to govt., Nov., 1881; acting collr. and mag., Haidarabad, May, 1887; junr. collr. and mag., March, 1892; senr. collr., June, 1896; commr. of customs, &c., April, 1900; author of 'Rhymes from the Russian,' &c.

PONTIFEX, SIR CHARLES, K.C.I.E., B.A., late Legal Adviser, India Office.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, May, 1854; judge of the high court, Calcutta, May, 1872; ret'd. in 1882 on appt. as legal adviser to sec. of state for India; legal adviser and solicitor, May, 1887; ret'd., April, 1892; K.C.I.E., May, 1892.

POPE, ALFRED WILLIAM UGLOW, India Public Works Dept. (traffic supt.).—Apptd. to the Public Works Dept., May, 1878, and posted to state railways; asst. traffic supt., July, 1879; traffic supt., 1880.

POPE, JOHN VAN SOMEREN, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instrn., Burma).—Educ. at Trinity Hall, Cambridge (scholar); joined the Bengal educl. service, 17th June, 1876, and served as prof'r. at the Patna Coll. and presidency coll., Calcutta, and as principal,

Dacca coll., Jan. 1878; inspr. of schs., Behar circle, June, 1883; fellow of the Calcutta univ., 1886; apptd. dir. of public instrn. in Burma, May, 1889; presdt. of the Educl. Syndicate, Jan., 1897.

POPE, RICHARD RODNEY, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Oratory Sch., Edgbaston, and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 1st Nov., 1879; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag., and in Assam as asst. commr., and district and sessions judge; district and sessions judge, Jessor, March, 1895; Dinajpore, May, 1896.

POPE, THOMAS ARCHDALE, Survey of India (asst. surv. gent.).—Joined the service, July, 1880; 2nd asst. supt., Sept., 1881; asst. supt., Survey of India, Oct., 1886; dep. supt., Mar., 1891; supt., Oct., 1899.

POPE, THOMAS HENRY, M.D., C.M., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (supt., ophthalmic hosp., Madras).—Joined the service, 30th March, 1878, and served in Afghanistan, 1879-81 (medal); acting residency surgn., Travancore, April, 1885; civil surgn., Negapatam, Oct., 1885; asst. physician, gen. hosp., Jan., 1888; also prof'r. at the med. coll., to Dec., 1888; district surgn. and supt. of jail, S. Arcot, March, 1892; supt., ophthalmic hosp., Madras, April, 1894.

POPERT, EDWIN PHILIP, late Forest Dept., Madras.—Arrived, 16th Dec., 1872, and was employed in Burma as asst. conservator of forests; dep. conservator, July, 1880; conservator, March, 1887; transf. to Madras, Jan., 1892; ret'd., March, 1900.

PORTEOUS, ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Harrow; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 16th Jan., 1879, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; dep. commr., May, 1890; offg. pol. agent and supt., Manipur, Dec., 1892, and again, July, 1897; offg. pol. officer, N. Lushai, July, 1894.

PORTEOUS, CHARLES ARKCOLL, C.I.E., Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Madras.—Arrived, 26th Nov., 1856, and was employed in the suppression of the Mutiny, 1857-59 (medal and two clasps); served in the dept. from May, 1860, as asst. supt., and supt.; in charge of the Madras police at the imperial assemblage, Delhi, Dec., 1876; dep. inspr.-gen., July, 1883; inspr.-gen. of jails, Jan., 1889; inspr.-gen. of police, March, 1891; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1894; C.I.E., May, 1895.

PORTEOUS, WILLIAM, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 26th Nov., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and pol. agent; from Jan., 1874, was employed in Mysore as asst. commr., and in various offg. appts.; from April, 1878, served in Bombay as asst. collr. and acting collr. of salt rev.; junr. collr. and mag. Nov., 1886; senr. collr., April, 1890; acted as commr. in 1894; ret'd., Nov., 1896.

PORTER, ALEXANDER, M.D., F.R.C.S.I., M.R.I.A., Surgn.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn, 31st March, 1865; held the civil surcegency of Akola, 1866-74, and acted as sanitary commr., Berar, 1870-71; from April, 1874, served in Madras, as chemical examr. to govt., surgn., 4th district, and prof'r. in the med. coll.; fellow of Madras univ., 1883; principal

and prof. of medicine, med. coll., Dec., 1886; reverted to mil. duty, July, 1890; ret'd. July, 1895; author of 'Notes on the Pathology of Famine Diseases.'

PORTER, FORTESCUE WILLOUGHBY, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Felsted Gr. Sch., and Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 21st Nov., 1865, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., settl. officer, and joint mag.; dep. commr., Aug., 1885; mag. and collr., Nov., 1886; inspr.-gen. of police, Aug., 1891; ret'd., May, 1894.

PORTER, GEORGE EDWARD, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Felsted Gr. Sch., and Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 11th Dec., 1862, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., inspr. of schs., and dep. commr., wards' estate, Patna; district and sessions judge, April, 1879; jud. commr., Chota Nagpur, Oct., 1885; off. judge, high court, Calcutta, Feb., 1886; ret'd., Sept., 1887.

PORTER, JOHN SINCLAIR, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., settl. officer, joint mag., and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., Shahjahanpur, April, 1879; acted as opium agent, Benares, 1886, and as commr. of Allahabad, 1886; ret'd., July, 1889.

PORTER, LESLIE ALEXANDER SELIM, M.A., LL.D., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Royal Acad. Instn., and Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 13th Nov., 1876, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and settl. officer; offd. as dep. sec. and sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., 1891; sec. to Deccan agriculturists relief commn., Nov., 1891; dep. commr., Sept., 1892; mag. and collr., Feb., 1894; acting commr., June, 1898, and Jan., 1899.

PORTER, LUDOVIC CHARLES, Indian C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Eton, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 8th Jan., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; city mag., Lucknow, Nov., 1895.

PORTER, WILLIAM NINNIS (dep. commr., Burma).—Joined the service as inspr. of police, 11th Oct., 1870; was apptd. asst. district supt., April, 1875, and district supt., Jan., 1880; asst. commr., April, 1886; dep. commr., Nov., 1890.

PORTMAN, AUGUSTUS BERKELEY, Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 11th April, 1860, and was employed on mil. duties till May, 1866; subsequently served in the dept. as asst. supt., district supt., and supt. of railway police; acting inspr.-gen. of registrn. and stamps, 1879, and 1880-83; 1st grade supt. of police, Jan., 1885; ret'd., Dec., 1890.

POSFORD, JOSEPH, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Finchley, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 12th Nov., 1869, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1882; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1888; ret'd., April, 1896.

POSSMANN, JULIUS, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the dept., 11th Aug.,

1863; asst. supt., Sept., 1866; supt., Aug., 1869; engr. and electrician, Karachi, Dec., 1886; offg. dir., Persian Gulf section, May-Oct., 1891; ret'd., Feb., 1897.

POTTINGER, BRABAZON HENRY, Lieut.-Gen., R.A.—Second lieut., 12th Dec., 1857; sec. to govt. of Bombay in the mil., marine and eccles. depts., June, 1887; ret'd., Dec., 1892.

POTTINGER, JOHN CURVEN, M.I.C.E. (sanitary engr., Bombay).—Joined the Bombay public works dept., 23rd July, 1868; exec. engr., May, 1875; sanitary engr. and member of sanitary board, Oct., 1893; suptg. engr., July, 1898.

POULTON, HENRY DUNBAR (junr. clerk, India Audit Office).—Junr. examr., 26th Dec., 1873; designation changed to junr. clerk, Oct., 1892.

POWELL, WALTER JOHN, Commander, R.I.M., late Marine Dept., Madras.—First commn., 19th Dec., 1864; presidency port officer, Madras, 18th Feb., 1893; ret'd., Feb., 1896.

POWER, AMBROSE WILLIAM BUSHE, B.L., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. in Germany, and at Kingstown Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and dep. commr. and pol. agent, Hill Tipperah; mag. and collr., Aug., 1887; commr., Bardwan, Dec., 1891; ret'd., April, 1894.

POWER, GUY FRANCIS THOMAS, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at the Somersetshire Coll., Bath, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1875, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., head asst., and addl. sessions judge; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1890; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1891; collr. and mag., Jan., 1894; again district and sessions judge, March, 1895.

POWLETT, PERCY WILLIAM, C.S.I., Col., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, 58th native infantry, Bengal, April, 1855; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasps); asst. commr., Punjab, 1862; and Berar, 1865; asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Rajputana, 1868; settl. officer, Alwar, Jan., 1871; pol. agent, Kota, Jan., 1877; pol. agent for the W. Rajputana states, 1880; rest., W. Rajputana, Dec., 1881; C.S.I., Jan., 1891; reverted to mil. dept., April, 1892; compiled the gazetteers of Bikanir, Alwar and Karooli.

POYNDER, JOHN LEOPOLD, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgn., Raipur).—Joined the Madras medl. serv., 31st Oct., 1879; on mily. duty till Aug., 1883; since which date he has held various civil surgeoncies in the Cent. Provs.; civil surgn., Raipur, April, 1896.

POYNTING, JOHN WHITFIELD ELFORD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.).—Educ. at Sedbergh School, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 29th Nov., 1897, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.

PRAIN, DAVID, M.A., M.B., Major, Indian Med. Service (dir. of botanical survey).—First commn., Oct., 1884; offg. curator of herbarium, roy. bot. gard., Calcutta, June-Oct., 1885; confd., Jan., 1887; profr. of botany, med. coll., Calcutta, July, 1895; supt. of the roy. bot. garden, Calcutta; govt. quinelogist and dir. of botanical survey of India, March, 1898; is the author of numerous papers on Indian botany.

PRAMODA CHARAN BANERJI, B.A., B.L. (judge, High Court, N.W. Prov.).—Joined the service, 16th Jan., 1872, as munsi; sub-judge, Aug., 1880; after serving as small cause court judge at Agra and Allahabad, was apptd. addl. civil judge, Lucknow, Aug., 1893; and puisne judge, high court, N.W. Prov., Dec., 1893.

PRASANNA KUMAR RAY, D.Sc., Bengal Educ. Dept. (prof., presidency coll., Calcutta).—Joined the service, 11th Jan., 1877, and served as asst. prof., Patna and Dacca colls.; prof., presidency coll., Feb., 1888.

PRATT, EDWARD MILLARD, Indian C.S. (judge and sessions judge, Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 21st Dec., 1886, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., March, 1894; 1st asst., March, 1897; judge and sess. judge, Sept., 1897.

PRATT, FREDERICK GREVILLE, Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 24th Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., March, 1897; acting dist. supt. of police, Sholapur, May-June, 1898.

PRATT, HENRY JAMES FREDERIC, late Bengal Opium Dept.—Entered the dept. Jan., 1861, and served as asst. sub-dep., and sub-deputy opium agent; ret'd., Nov., 1897.

PRATT, HENRY SHELDON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at King's Grammar School, Warwick, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 30th Nov., 1896, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

PRATT, JAMES, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (judge, high court, Calcutta).—Educ. at Aberdeen and London Univs.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 12th Oct., 1869, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Oct., 1880; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1883; offg. supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, April, 1897; judge, high court, May, 1900.

PRATT, WILLIAM DERING, late Police Dept., Bengal.—Joined the service as asst. supt. of police, 6th Sept., 1863; district supt., Aug., 1868; dep. inspr.-gen., May, 1894; ret'd., April, 1899.

PREECE, HENRY CHARLES (second class clerk, store dept., India Office).—Clerk, second class, India store dept., 5th Dec., 1885.

PREM NATH PANDIT, Rai Bahadur, India Public Works Dept. (examr. of accts.).—Apptd. as acct., sub-accts. estab., 4th July, 1867; asst. examr., Mar., 1878; dep.-examr., Jan., 1880; examr., May, 1886.

PRENDERGAST, SIR HARRY NORTH DALRYMPLE, K.C.B., V.C., Gen.—Second lieut., 8th June, 1854; served in the Persian war in 1857 (medal with clasp), with the Malwa field force in 1857, with the Central India field force in 1858 (medal with clasp and V.C.), and in the Abyssinian war 1867-68 (medal); in command of sappers in the Indian expdn. to the Mediterranean, 1878; commanded the expdn. that resulted in the annexation of Upper Burma in 1885-86 (clasp); has received the distinguished service reward; held appts. in the dept. of public works and in the railway dept.; commanded the Madras sappers and miners for 10 years; acted as

quartermaster-gen. of the Madras army and as sec. to govt. in the mil. dept.; offd. as British rest. in Travancore and Cochin in 1887, and as British rest. in Mysore and chief commr. in Coorg in 1887 and 1888; agent to the govr.-gen., Baroda, Jan., 1889; offg. rest. and govr.-gen.'s agent in Baluchistan, March to Oct., 1889; offg. rest., Mysore and chief and jud. commr., Coorg, June, 1891; reverted to mil. dept., April, 1892.

PRESTON, SIDNEY, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Apptd., 10th Aug., 1870, and employed in the Punjab on irrign. works; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; offg. under sec. to the Punjab govt., 1885-86; suptg. engr., April, 1893; sec. to govt., N.W.P. and Oudh, and chief engr., Jan., 1899; transf. to Punjab as chief engr. and sec., irrigation branch, Feb., 1900.

PREVOST, GEORGE FAIRFAX, late Indian Forest Dept.—Apptd. to the dept. as asst. conservator, Dec., 1873; dep. conservator, March, 1884; ret'd., Jan., 1900.

PRICE, GORDON, M.D., Surgn.-Lt.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—Served from 29th July, 1875, as civil surgn. and on jail duty; principal asst. to opium agent, Behar, Nov., 1887, to April, 1889; civil surgn., Murshidabad, April, 1896; ret'd., Jan., 1898.

PRICE, JAMES CATTRALL, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; settlt. officer, Midnapur, Dec., 1871; also supt. of survey, March, 1876; mag. and collr., Aug., 1876; author of 'Notes on the History of Midnapur.'; ret'd., Oct., 1896.

PRICE, JOHN ARTHUR, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the Madras civil engrg. coll.; apptd., 14th May, 1869, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., April, 1882; transf. to Burma, July, 1887.

PRICE, SIR JOHN FREDERICK, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Melbourne Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 25th Oct., 1862; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr.; collr. and mag., S. Arcot, and pol. agent for Pondicherry, April, 1878; collr. and mag., Chengalpatt, Sept., 1878; board of examrs., Jan., 1882; acting dir. of rev. settlt. and agric., Madras, April, 1885; sec. to govt., rev. dept., Oct., 1887; member of legisl. council, Oct., 1888; chief sec. to govt. of Madras, Nov., 1888; C.S.I., Jan., 1893; addl. member of legisl. council, Madras, Jan., 1897; ret'd., Oct., 1897; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1898.

PRICE, WILLIAM, M.D., M.Ch., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Arrived, 7th Nov., 1869; civil surgn., Vellore, April, 1877; med. officer, Ootakamund, March, 1878; zillah surgn., Feb., 1881; senr. physician, gen. hosp., and principal, med. coll., Oct., 1891; ret'd., April, 1896.

PRIDEAUX, WILLIAM FRANCIS, C.S.I., Col., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Bombay infantry, Jan., 1860; asst. pol. rest., Aden, April, 1864; attached to mission to Abyssinia, March, 1865, and was in captivity at Magdala, 1866-68; offd. as pol. agent, Zanzibar, 1873-75; fellow of Bombay univ., 1874; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., June, 1875; offg. pol. rest., Persian Gulf, 1876-77; pol. agent, Bhopal, March, 1879; agent

to the govr.-gen. with the ex-King of Oudh, and supt. of pol. pensions, Calcutta, Nov., 1880; offg. rest., E. Rajputana states, 1882-83; apptd. in 1887 agent to the govr.-gen. for the affairs of the late King of Oudh, and supt. of pol. pensions; rest. at Jaipur, Nov., 1888; acting rest., Kashmir, Dec., 1890; rest. in Mewar, Nov., 1893; acting agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, 1893-94; rest., Jaipur, April, 1894; C.S.I., Jan., 1895; reverted to mil. dept., April, 1895.

PRINGLE, JOHN CHRISTIAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Winchester and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 17th Nov., 1896, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

PRINSEP, SIR HENRY THOBV, Kt., Indian C.S. (judge, Calcutta high court).—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 7th Dec., 1855, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; offg. junr. sec., board of rev., Sept., 1861; registrar of the sadar diwani and nizamat adalat, Feb., 1862, and subsequently of the high court, July, 1862; mag. and collr., 1st grade, May, 1866; served in the jud. dept., from Feb., 1867, as district and sessions judge, and offd. as jud. commr. of Mysore, 1875-76; judge, high court, Calcutta, Aug., 1878; member of Viceroy's legisl. council, Jan., 1897 to July, 1898.

PRITAMDAS PARSUMAL CHANDANANI, L.C.E., Bo. Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Joined the service 20th April, 1875, and served as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Nov., 1886.

PRITCHARD, ALFRED BASSETT, Major, Indian Staff Corps (dep. commr., Burma).—First commn., 11th Nov., 1878; apptd. to Burma as asst. commr., June, 1887; dep. commr., March, 1899.

PRITCHARD, SIR CHARLES BRADLEY, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1857; arrived, 24th Jan., 1858, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; collr. of salt rev., Bombay, Feb., 1873; on special duty in Madras as prest. of the salt commn., 1876; on special duty to submit a scheme for the working of the abkari system, 1877-78; commr. of customs, &c., April, 1881; commr. of salt rev., Oct., 1882; C.S.I., March, 1886; acting commr. in Sind, April, 1887; confd., Aug., 1888; temporary member of council of govr. of Bombay, May, 1889; confd., Nov., 1890; K.C.I.E., May, 1891; member of viceroy's council, Nov., 1892; ret'd., Feb., 1896.

PRITCHARD, CHARLES HAMERTON, Capt., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent, Bundelkhand).—Educ. at Clifton Coll.; first commn., 10th May, 1882; from March, 1888, acted as asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Baluchistan; engaged in boundary settlt. work in Mewar, 1889-90; asst. to rest., Haidarabad, and to the gen. supt. thagi and dakaite, April, 1890; asst. commr., Merwara, July, 1891; offg. pol. agent, E. states of Rajputana, April, 1893; settlt. officer, Tonk, Feb., 1894; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Nov., 1896; asst. commr., Ajmere, April, 1897; pol. agent in Bundelkhand, April, 1899.

PROBY, DAVID GRANVILLE, late Police Dept., Central Prov.—Entered the service as offg. asst. dist. supt. of police, July, 1867; confd. Jan., 1869; dist. supt., April, 1874; ret'd., April, 1900.

PROPERT, WILLIAM HENRY, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1856; arrived, 6th Nov., 1856, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and pol. agent; from Feb., 1876, served as collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Khandesh; senr. collr., Sept., 1877; commr., April, 1886; ret'd., April, 1890.

PROTHERO, MICHAEL ERNLEE DU SAUTOY, M.A. (inspr. of schools, Bengal).—Joined the service as profr., presy. coll., Calcutta, 17th April, 1881; inspr. of schools, April, 1897.

PROTUL CHANDAR CHATTERJI, Rai Bahadur, M.A., B.L. (judge, Punjab Chief Court).—Joined the service as offg. judge, 14th April, 1894; addl. judge, July, 1894.

PUCKLE, RICHARD KAYE, C.I.E., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch., and Haileybury Coll.; entered the Madras civil service in 1851, and was apptd. dep. dir. of rev. settlt. in 1859; collr. and mag., Tinnevelli, 1866; dir. of rev. settlt., 1874, and member of board of rev., 1879; was engaged for 22 years in preparing data for the re-assessment of the lands, and for the revision of govt. estabts. in the ryotwari villages of Madras; employed in famine districts in 1877; C.I.E., 1878; ret'd. in 1881; publ. a hand-book on rev. matters and the duties of village officers.

PUGHE, ROBERTSON FRANCIS HOME, Bengal Police Dept. (dist. supt.).—Joined the service, Jan., 1864, and served as asst. supt. and dist. supt. of police.

PULFORD, RUSSELL RICHARD, C.I.E., Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Joined the dept., 23th Feb., 1870, as asst. principal, Thomason coll.; asst. engr., June, 1873; exec. engr., March, 1874; served through the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); suptg. engr., March, 1892; offg. chief engr. and sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, July-Oct., 1894; transfld. to Rajputana and Central India, May, 1896; C.I.E., June, 1897.

PURSER, WILLIAM EDWARD, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Enniskillen Royal Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 21st Nov., 1865, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; senr. settlt. officer and dep. commr. of settlt., Jalandhar, Sept., 1880; dep. commr., Dec., 1885; ret'd., Aug., 1890; author of reports on the settlt. of the Montgomery district, and of the Muktsar and Mamdot parganas.

PURVES, HENRY BLACK, Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—First commn., 1865; from 12th March, 1867, served as civil asst. surgn., and civil surgn., in various stations in Bengal; ret'd., Dec., 1895.

PUTNAM, JAMES (confidential clerk on Viceroy's staff).—Clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., India Office, 26th Feb., 1892; transfld. to pol. dept., Dec., 1895; went to India as confidential clerk on the staff of the Viceroy, Nov., 1897.

PUTNAM, PHILIP (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 28th July, 1881.

QUAYLE, WILLIAM ADAIR, M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgn., Cent. Prov.).—Joined the Madras medl. serv., 30th Mar., 1878; on mil. duty till Aug., 1885; civil surgn., Narsingpur, Sept., 1885; ditto, Nimar, Oct., 1889.

QUERIPEL, ALFRED ERNEST, Veterinary Lieut.-Col. (inspr.-gen., India Civil Veterinary

Dept.).—Joined the (home) service, 27th Aug., 1870; arrived in India, 3rd Feb., 1877; served in Afghan war, 1879-80, and with Nile expedit. force, 1884-85; asst. supt., horse breeding operations, Babugarh, 1880-83; reverted to home establ., April, 1884; apptd. to charge of chief station veterinary hosp., Ambala, and instructor, army veterinary sch., Simla, March, 1891; inspecting veterinary officer, E. circle, Dec., 1893; inspr.-gen., civil veterinary dept., May, 1894; author of an essay on stable management and dietetic disease in India, and of a manual of materia medica for veterinary students.

QUICKE, WILLIAM HENRY, Major, Indian Med. Service, Bombay (prof. of Grant med. coll.).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1883; acted as Bombay port health officer in 1888-89, and as supt. of vaccn. in the latter year; confd. as civil surgn., Kaira, Sept., 1890; 2nd surgn., Jamsetji Jijibhoj hosp., and prof. of anatomy and curator of museum, Grant med. coll., April, 1893.

QUIN, HENRY OSBORNE, Indian C.S. (1st asst. Bombay).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 13th Nov., 1885, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., March, 1892; administrator, Porbandhar State, Aug., 1896; 1st asst., Nov., 1897.

QUIN, THOMAS JAMES, Major-Gen., late Oudh Commn.—First commn., ensign, 13th April, 1859; from May, 1869, served under the govt. of the N.W. Prov. as offg. asst. commr. and cantonmt. mag.; dep. commr., Nov., 1889; served during the Bhutan campaign of 1865 (medal); returned to Europe, March, 1897.

QUINLAN, FRANCIS PETER, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the accts. branch, Oct., 1870, and was employed on the accts. of various railways in the grades of acctnt., asst. examr., asst. controller, and dep. examr.; examr., Sept., 1881; ret'd. March, 1895.

QUINN, CHARLES CAMPBELL, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Dunganon, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 8th Jan., 1864; served in the rev. and jud. depts., Bengal, and was apptd. mag. and collr., June, 1882; commr., Bhagalpur divn., Oct., 1891; ret'd. March, 1895.

QUINN, JOHN, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. commr., Oudh, April, 1877, and mag. and collr., Ballia, Dec., 1881; commr., Lucknow divn., March, 1884; offd. as jud. commr., Oudh, in 1884; ret'd. March, 1888.

RADICE, CHARLES ALBERT, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Bedford Sch., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlt. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., June, 1897; mag. and collr., Aug., 1900.

RADICE, EVASIO HAMPDEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Bedford Sch., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1887, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. supt., Tarai, asst. commr., and joint mag.; city mag. and wasika

officer Lucknow, Aug., 1893; offg. postmr.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Feb., 1895; joint mag., Nov., 1895; dep. commr., March, 1897; mag. and collr., Sept., 1899.

RAGHUNATH SHIVRAM TIPNIS, B.A., LL.B., Statutory C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay).—Served as subord. judge from July, 1878, and was apptd. an asst. collr. in Bombay, under the native civil service rules, 1st April, 1887; on depn. to the Junagad state, 1893-94; 2nd asst., June, 1894; 1st asst., May, 1897; judge and sess. judge, Dec., 1897.

RAIKES, FREDERICK DUNCAN, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C. (commr., Burma).—Arrived, as ensign, 66th regt., 1st April, 1870; acting asst. supt. of police, Panch Mahals, March, 1873; from May, 1873, served in the Burma commn. as asst. commr., and acted as asst. town mag., Rangoon, 1874-75; transfd. to Upper Burma, Feb., 1886; dep. commr., Aug., 1886; served in the Burmese campaign, 1885-87 (medal and clasp); on special duty with the Chin field force, Dec., 1888; commr., 1892-93 and 1894-95; confd., Oct. 1896.

RAINY, GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. wag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Edinburgh Academy, and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

RAJAGOPALA CHARIYAR, PERUNGAVUR, M.A., B.L., Statutory C.S. (Dewan to the Raja of Cochin).—Educ. at the Madras Univ.; served in the jud. dept. from 13th May, 1886; dep. collr., Dec., 1887; apptd. prob. for the civil service, 10th Jan., 1888; served as asst. collr. and mag., and confd., 2nd May, 1890; Dewan to the Raja of Cochin, Dec., 1896.

RAJANINATH RAY, M.A., Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (dept. acctnt.-gen., Bengal).—Joined the dept., Nov., 1872, and served in the Central Prov., and afterwards in Bombay; asst. compt.-gen., Nov., 1882; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Aug.-Nov., 1885; dep. compt.-gen., April-June, 1887; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, April, 1893.

RAJARATHNA MUDALIYAR, PAKAM, C.I.E., Dewan Bahadur (inspr.-gen. of registrn., Madras).—Served in the salt dept. from 26th Oct., 1860; head sarishtadar, board of rev., Aug., 1882; sec. to the commr. of rev. settlt. and dir. of the dept. of land records and agric., Nov., 1887; inspr.-gen. registrn., July, 1896; member of Madras legisl. council, Nov., 1896; C.I.E., May, 1900.

RAJENDRA KUMAR BOSE, B.L., Bengal Judicial Dept. (subordinate judge).—Entered the service as munisif, March, 1869; subordinate judge, Nov., 1883.

RALEIGH, THOMAS, D.C.L., Government of India (member of govr.-gen.'s council).—Educ. at Edinburgh, Tübingen and Oxford Univs., Fellow of All Souls' Coll., and Reader in English Law; regr. of the Privy Council; apptd. legal member of the Governor-General's Council; assumed charge, 3rd April, 1899; vice-chancellor of Calcutta Univ., Aug., 1900; author of 'Elementary Politics' 1886, and 'Outlines of the Law of Property,' 1889.

RAM AUTAR PANDE, Pandit, B.A., Statutory C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Joined the service as offg. dep. collr., 13th March, 1884; apptd. asst. mag. and collr. under the statutory service rules, 12th Feb.,

1887; joint mag., Nov., 1891; asst. commr., Nov., 1895; addl. judge, July, 1897; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1899.

RAMACHANDRA AIYAR, CUDDALORE, late Madras Jud. Dept.—Joined the dept., 19th Jan., 1860; 4th presidency mag., Madras, March, 1885; apptd. asst. collr. and mag., Feb., 1887; sub-collr. and joint mag., April, 1889; district and sessions judge, June, 1891; retd., March, 1894.

RAMACHANDRA RAO, RAGHUNATHA RAO, B.A., late Madras Rev. Dept.—Apptd. probir. under the native civil service rules, 13th June, 1890, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; dep. collr., July, 1892.

RAMA SHANKAR MISRA, Pandit, M.A., Statutory C.S. (dep. commr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Offg. profir. of mathematics, Benares coll., Feb., 1877; resigned, Jan., 1878; rejoined as profir., Agra coll., 23rd Aug., 1879; apptd. under the native civil service rules asst. mag. and collr., 29th Dec., 1887; joint mag., May, 1892; asst. settl. officer, Nov., 1893; asst. commr., April, 1896; dep. commr., June, 1899; author of a 'Treatise on Mensuration' in Hindi, and a 'Manual of Rent and Revenue Law.'

RAMPINI, ROBERT FULTON, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (judge, high court, Calcutta).—Educ. at the Acad., Instn., and Univ., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 28th Nov., 1864, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; joint sessions judge, Sept., 1875; district and sessions judge, July, 1880; judge of high court, Calcutta, April, 1893; edited Alexander's 'Indian Case Law on Torts,' Field's 'Law of Evidence,' and (with Mr. Finucane) 'The Bengal Tenancy Act.'

RAMSAY, HENRY LUSHINGTON, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Feb., 1874; acted as asst. to agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, 1877-78; junr. attaché, foreign dept., March, 1878; and semi., June, 1880; pol. officer on staff of Sir F. S. Roberts with the Kuram field force, Sept., 1879 (medal and clasp); asst. to agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, Oct., 1880; mag. at Abu, and asst. gen. supt., thagi and dakaiti dept., Nov., 1880; asst. agent, Baluchistan, June, 1882; consul at Bassora, May, 1883; cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, Nov., 1883; British joint commr. of Ladakh, Oct., 1885; pol. agent, Bhopawar, March, 1891; pol. agent, S. Baluchistan, Nov., 1893; pol. officer with Sardar Ayub Khan, Oct., 1894; author of 'Western Thibet.'

RAMSAY, HERBERT MAYNARD, Major-Genl., late Police Dept., Bengal.—First commn., 1860; from and July, 1863, served in the dept. as asst. supt., district supt., and personal asst. to the inspr.-gen.; on depn. to report on chaukidari system, 1890-91.

RAMSAY, JAMES, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service 8th Oct., 1868; exec. engr., March, 1871; engr.-in-chief, Indus valley state railway, Nov., 1880, and subsequently of the Sind-Sagar and Muskhaf-Bolan lines; suptg. engr., Feb., 1890; chief engr., Feb., 1893; retd., July, 1894.

RAMSAY, JOHN, C.I.E., Capt., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol.-agent, Quetta-Pishin).—First commn., 9th Sept., 1882; from July, 1886, served at Ajmer and Indore as acting asst. agent; confd. in latter post, Jan., 1889; pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk, Dec., 1891; 1st asst., Indore, May, 1892; in addn., asst. gen. supt.,

thagi and dakaiti, Nov., 1892; pol. agent in Bundelkhand, April, 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1898; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1898.

RANGACHARI, SIVARAM, B.A., Madras Rev. Dept. (dep. commr.).—Joined the service, 6th April, 1865; asst. commr. of rev. settlt., April, 1887; dep. commr. of rev. settlt. Jan., 1889.

RANKIN, JAMES THOMSON, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Glasgow and Edinburgh Univs.; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1892, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and sub-divl. officer; asst. mag. and collr., March, 1898; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1900.

RANKIN, GEORGE SPIERS ALEXANDER, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (sec. to board of examrs.).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1875; apptd. sec. to board of examrs., June, 1894; author of a guide to Hindustani for mil. med. officers, a work on Arabic and Persian prosody, &c.

RANSOM, HENRY EDWARD, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Bedford Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 12th Dec., 1885, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam as asst. commr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., June, 1894; mag. and collr., Nov., 1896; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1897.

RASTAMJI MERWANJI PATEL, Khan Bahadur, M.A., LL.B. (judge, Bombay small cause court).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1894, as 2nd judge, Bombay small cause court.

RATTON, JAMES JOSEPH LOUIS, M.D., M.R.C.S., Surgn.-Major, late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 30th Sept., 1867; served in the Abyssinian expdn. (medal); civil surgn., Central Prov., 1869-70; on special duty in connection with salt manufacture, 1872-76; on famine relief works, 1876; zillah surgn. and supt. of jail, Chittur, April, 1877; surgn., gen. hosp., Madras, and profir. in the med. coll., Feb., 1885; fellow of Madras univ., 1886; retd., Jan., 1888; has publd. a 'Handbook on Common Salt,' a 'Treatise on Tetanus,' and a 'Syllabus of Surgery.'

RAVENS CROFT, EDWARD W., C.S.I., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at King Henry VIII.'s Gr. Sch., Otterey St. Mary, and Haileybury Coll.; arrived, 25th May, 1851, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; inspr.-in-chief under the cotton frauds act, Bombay, Feb., 1864; prest. of the municipal commn., Bombay, June, 1864; chief sec. to govt. of Bombay, in the rev., &c., depts., 1877; collr. of Bombay, and supt. of stamps and stationery, March, 1868; member of legisl. council, Bombay, 1871 and 1873; collr. of salt rev., Oct., 1871; senr. collr. and mag., Dec., 1871; member of the Baroda commn. to investigate the charges brought against the Gaekwar; member of govr.'s council, Bombay, April, 1879; retd., March, 1884.

RAVENSHAW, CHARLES WITHERS, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (resdt., Baroda).—First commn., 8th May, 1872; from Dec., 1875, offd. as asst. cantonmt. mag. at various places; from Feb., 1877, served under the foreign dept. as asst. pol. agent; on special boundary duty in 1879-80; served in Afghan war (medal); supt. of Rajputana railway police, Sept., 1880; asst. commr., Merwara, Dec., 1885; 1st asst. pol. rest., Persian Gulf, July, 1887; cantonmt. mag. and judge, small

cause court, Secunderabad, April, 1890; asst. to the rest. in Mysore, and sec. to the chief commr. of Coorg, April, 1891; offg. rest., Gwailor, April, 1895; offg. rest., Mewar, 1897; commr., Ajmere-Merwara, June, 1899; resdt., Baroda, April, 1900.

RAW, WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Roy. Gr. Sch., Newcastle and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 7th Dec., 1896; and served in the N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

RAWLINS, THOMAS WILLIAM, late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 11th Jan., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; small cause court judge, Allahabad, Nov., 1872; served under the govt. of India, fin. dept., from March, 1877; acctnt.-gen., Punjab, and dep. commr. of paper currency, Aug., 1880; acctnt.-gen. and commr. of paper currency, Madras, Oct., 1888; ret'd., Feb., 1896.

RAWSON, COURTNEY CLARKE, Col., R.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (supfg. engr.).—Joined the dept., 27th June, 1871; exec. engr., March, 1878; served in Afghan war, 1878-79 (medal); acted as dep. chief engr. and under sec. to govt. in 1881 and 1882; supfg. engr., Jan., 1896.

RAYE, DANIEL O'CONNELL, M.D., Col., late Indian Med. Service.—First commn., 31st March, 1866; from 1st Sept., 1875, served as rest. surgn., presidency gen. hosp.; profir., med. coll., and surgn. to the coll. hosp., May, 1881; principal med. officer, Calcutta, Jan., 1895; inspr.-gen. of civil hosp., Punjab, March, 1895; ret'd. April, 1899.

RAYMENT, GEORGE JOSEPH ROBERT, Veterinary Lieut.-Col., late Indian Civil Veterinary Dept.—First commn., 24th July, 1872; apptd. supt., civil veterinary dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, 1st April, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., June, 1894.

REAY (BARON), RIGHT HON. DONALD JAMES MACKAY, G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., LL.D.—Took his seat as govr. of Bombay, 30th March, 1885; G.C.I.E., June, 1887; G.C.S.I., March, 1890; returned to Europe, April, 1890; was lord rector of the univ. of St. Andrew's, 1884; parly. under sec. of state for India, March, 1894, to July, 1895; chairman, London School Board, Dec., 1897; is the prest. of the Royal Asiatic Society, London.

REAY, ROBERT WILLIAM (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 23rd Oct., 1894.

REBSCH, SAMUEL, A.M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1875, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., April, 1884.

REDFERN, THOMAS ROBERT, B.A., B.L., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at New Coll., Oxford; called to the bar at the Inner Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 2nd Oct., 1870, and served in Oudh as asst. commr. and offg. settlt. officer; joint mag., May, 1885; district and sessions judge, Sept., 1887; ret'd., Oct., 1895.

REED, JOHN HENRY FREDERICK, B.A. (junior clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dep., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872; B.A., London univ., 1881; junr. clerk, June, 1887.

REES, FREDERICK WILLIAM JOSEPH, B.A.,

late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Shrewsbury, and St John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 11th Jan., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; mag. and collr., April, 1878; from Dec., 1880, served in the jud. dept. as district and sessions judge; ret'd., Dec., 1887.

REES, JOHN CHARLES, B.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd., 8th Aug., 1873, and posted to Burma; exec. engr., Feb., 1882; employed on Burma state railway, 1884-85; received Burma medal and clasp; in charge, Pegu divn., May, 1894.

REES, JOHN DAVID, C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., London, and Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 10th Nov., 1875, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; from April, 1882, served in the secretariat as asst sec. and under sec., and in Mysore (1884-85) as acting civil and sessions judge; private sec. to the govr. of Madras, Dec., 1886; fellow of Madras univ., 1887; on special duty in connection with visit of Prince Albert Victor, 1839-90; C.I.E., May, 1890; apptd. collr. and mag. and sessions judge, Nilgiris, Aug., 1890, continuing to act as private sec. until Dec., 1891; acting rest., Travancore, April, 1895; member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, Oct., 1895 to Oct., 1897; collr. of customs and protector of emigrants, Madras, May, 1897; resdt., Travancore, and Cochin, Nov., 1897; member of legisl. council of the govr.-genl., Nov., 1898; author of 'Notes of a Journey from Kasween to Hamadan in N.W. Persia,' 'Tours in India by Lord Connemara,' 'The Duke of Clarence in S. India,' and 'Epochs of Indian History: The Muhammadans;' and compiler of 'The Administration of Lord Connemara,' and 'Speeches of Lord Connemara'; ret'd., Sept., 1900.

REEVES, FRANCIS CASEMENT, Lieut.-Col., Indian Medl. Service (surgn., 1st dist., Madras, and medl. inspr. of emigrants).—First commn., 31st Oct., 1879; mily. duty till 1885; civil surgn. at Betul, Cocanada and Tellicherry; dist. surgn., Malabar, Mar., 1893; dist. medl. officer, Madura, July, 1894; surgn., 1st dist., Madras, and medl. inspr. of emigrants, May, 1900.

REID, ADAM SCOTT, Col., I.M.S. (chief med. officer, Cent. Prov.).—Apptd. administrative medl. offr. and sanitary commr. of the Central Provs., May, 1899.

REID, ARTHUR HAY STEWART, M.A., B.L. (judge, chief court, Punjab).—Educ. at Harrow, and Trin. Hall, Cambridge; profir. of Law, Muir Coll., Allahabad, Jan., 1883; offd. as public prosecutor and standing counsel, high court, N.W.P., March, 1895-Jan., 1896; and as judge, chief court, Punjab, April, 1896-Sept., 1896; judge, chief court, Punjab, Sept., 1896; offd. as chief judge, May-Nov., 1899.

REID, CHARLES LESTOCK ONSLOW, Captain, I.S.C. (asst. commr., Burma).—First Commn., Aug. 29, 1885.—Joined the Burma commn. as asst. commr., April, 1891.

REID, GEORGE BOILEAU, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Harrow, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 26th Nov., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 1st asst. collr., July, 1878; settlt. officer at Baroda, in 1879 and 1882; junr. collr. and mag., Oct., 1886; collr.

and mag., Nov., 1886; senr. collr., May, 1888; commr., N. divn., Nov., 1892; ret'd. July, 1897.

REID, JAMES ROBERT, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 13th Dec., 1860, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and joint mag., and offg. dep. commr. in Oudh; from Aug., 1868, served as asst. settlt. officer, and settlt. officer, Azamgarh; mag. and collr., Jan., 1878; sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov., Dec., 1883; chief sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, May, 1884; commr., Kumaon divn., Nov., 1888; member of board of rev., Feb., 1890; C.I.E., May, 1890; member of legisl. council, March, 1891; ret'd., April, 1894.

REID, JOHN, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Blackrock Coll., Dublin, Queen's Coll., Galway, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 21st Nov., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

REID, JOHN WILLIAM, B.L., late Madras C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1855; served from July, 1856, in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., sub-judge, and sub-collr. and joint mag.; from June, 1869, served in Malabar as civil and sessions judge; district and sessions judge, Coimbatore, Oct., 1884; ret'd., 1886.

REID, WILLIAM JAMES, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Glasgow High Sch. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 25th Nov., 1891, and served in Assam as asst. commr. and sub. divl. officer; personal asst. to chief commr., Nov., 1898.

REILLY, HENRY D'ARCY CORNELIUS (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Nov., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

REILY, HENRY MAXWELL, late Police Dept., Bengal.—From May, 1858, served as dep. mag. and dep. collr.; from July, 1863, served in the police dept., and was apptd. district supt., 1st grade, Dec., 1882; on special duty, Garo Hills expedn., Nov., 1872; ret'd., 1890.

REINOLD, EDWARD KONRAD, late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1868, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1879; ret'd., Jan., 1895.

RENDALL, HUGH DAVY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Rugby and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 4th Dec., 1896, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

RENDEL, SIR ALEXANDER MEADOWS, K.C.I.E. (consulting engr. to sec. of state for India).—Educ. at King's Sch. Canterbury, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge (scholar and wrangler); studied as a civil engr., and in 1856 became engr. to the London dock co., the Leith harbour and dock commrs., the East Indian railway, and other companies; visited India in 1857-58, and on various occasions; built the Shadwell new basin, the royal Albert dock, and other works on the Thames, the Albert and Edinburgh docks at Leith, the Workington dock and harbour, and other works; member of the commn. apptd. by the sec. of state for India in 1870, to determine the narrow gauge for Indian railways; designed the Sukkur bridge over the Indus (opened

1839); is consulting engr. to the sec. of state for India, and to various Indian railway cos.

RENNICK, ALBERT DE CLANEY, B.L., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—First commn., 6th July, 1864; arrived, 28th Nov., 1866; from Oct., 1870, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; cantonmt. mag., Dec., 1880; dep. commr., May, 1880; reverted to mil. duty, April, 1890.

RENNIE, FRANCIS PEPYS, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Winchester, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 23rd December, 1895, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

RENNIE, JOHN GEORGE MACKWORTH, Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at Winchester, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 19th Dec., 1881, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; district judge, Dec., 1892; divl. judge, July, 1896.

RENNY, ROBERT HOME, late Bengal commn.—Served in the exec. dept. from 12th Nov., 1864, as dep. mag. and dep. collr. and extra asst. commr.; joined the commn., March, 1877; dep. commr., April, 1890; ret'd., Jan., 1899.

RENNY - TAILYOUR, THOMAS FRANCIS BRUCE, Major, R.E. (supt., Survey of India).—Joined the dept., April, 1888; apptd. dep. supt. with Burma-China boundary commn., Jan., 1896; supt., Nov., 1899.

RENOUF, WINTER CHARLES, Indian C.S. (dist. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 14th Dec., 1889, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dist. judge, April, 1899.

REYNOLDS, CHARLES HENRY, C.I.E., M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the dept. as asst. supt., 7th Jan., 1868; personal asst. to dir.-gen., 1870, and 1872; in charge of famine divn., 1874; supt., April, 1886; delegate for India at the Berlin telegraph conference, 1885; dir., construction branch, July, 1889; dir., traffic branch, Dec., 1892; dep. dir.-gen., June, 1894; dir.-gen., April, 1895; C.I.E., June, 1897; ret'd., 1898.

REYNOLDS, GEORGE BERNARD, M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., M.I.S.I., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to state railways; employed in superior rev. establ., locomotive dept., 1879-91; exec. engr. and manager, Warora colliery, April, 1891; ret'd., Aug., 1897.

REYNOLDS, HERBERT JOHN, C.S.I., B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1855; arrived, 24th Sept., 1856; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., supt. of survey, and mag. and collr.; between 1874 and 1880 served as sec. to the govt. of Bengal, gen. and rev. depts., and as sec. to the board of rev.; member, govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, 1886, and again in 1882 and 1884; member, Bengal legisl. council, 1881, 1883, 1885, and 1887; board of rev., July, 1884; chairman of port commrs., Calcutta, Oct., 1887; ret'd., April, 1889.

REYNOLDS, HERBERT WILLIAM WARD, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Cheltenham; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1877, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. sec. to govt., and junr.

sec., board of rev.; joint mag., Aug., 1890; dep. commr., Dec., 1893; mag. and collr., Jan., 1895.

REYNOLDS, LEONARD WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Bradfield and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 6th Dec., 1898, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

REYNOLDS, PLAYFORD, M.I.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1868, and was employed in Khadesh and Gujarat on irrign. works; exec. engr., May, 1879; supt. engr., June, 1896; services lent to Baroda state in 1883-85.

REYNOLDS, WILLIAM HENRY, Survey of India (supt. of Forest Surveys).—Joined the service, 14th March, 1861, and was engaged on survey duty on the N.W. frontier until 1871, including service with the Ambela expdn., 1863; from Jan., 1871, served in the forest dept. as asst. conservator, dep. conservator, and dep. supt. of forest surveys; rejoined the survey dept. as dep. supt., Oct., 1890; supt. of forest surveys, March, 1891.

RHIND, ROBERT HUNTER, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. probr. by the sec. of state, June, 1862; arrived, 7th Dec., 1862, and served in Madras as asst. engr., and exec. engr.; transf. to Bengal, Aug., 1872; supt. engr., Nov., 1886; ret'd., May, 1893.

RHODES, FRANCIS WILLIAM, D.S.O., Col., late 1st Royal Dragoons.—First commn., lieut., 23rd April, 1875; served in the Soudan expdn. in 1884 (medal with clasp and Khedive's star); also in Nile expdn. of 1884-85, and in the operations near Suakin, 1888 (3rd class Medjidie); mil. sec. to Lord Harris, govr. of Bombay, 1890-93; ret'd., Sept., 1896; re-instated in the army, Sept., 1898.

RIBBENTROP, BERTHOLD, C.I.E., late inspr.-gen. of forests, Govt. of India.—Apptd. by the sec. of state an asst. conservator of forests, Dec., 1866; conservator, Burma, Oct., 1876; transf. to the Punjab, 1883; inspr.-gen. of forests, Jan., 1889; C.I.E., Jan., 1890; author of 'Hints on Arboriculture'; ret'd., Nov., 1900.

RICE, PERCIVAL STANLEY PITCAIRN, Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 8th Dec., 1890, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., July, 1897.

RICE, WALTER FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 16th Dec., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

RICE, WILLIAM GEORGE LEWIS, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Oscott Coll., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 10th Nov., 1882, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; transf. to Burma, Feb., 1886, as asst. commr.; resigned, Oct., 1890.

RICE, WILLIAM ROCHE, M.D., C.S.I., Surgn.-Major-Gen.—Joined the Bengal med. service Nov., 1856; served during the Mutiny (medal); civil asst. surgn., Central Prov., June, 1859; civil surgn., Jabalpur, May, 1871; acting asst. to sanitary commr., Central Prov., July, 1872; supt., central jail, Jabalpur, April, 1873; civil surgn., July, 1879; inspr.-gen. of civil hosp., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Oct., 1887; sanitary commr.

and surgn.-gen. to govt. of India, March, 1890; ret'd., March, 1895.

RICHARD, HENRY JOSEPH, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, July, 1858; asst. engr., Rangoon, 28th Oct., 1868; exec. engr., April, 1875; served with the Burma expdn. force, 1885-86 (medal with clasps); supt. of works, Upper Burma, Jan., 1886; supt. engr., Sept., 1889; chief engr. and sec. to chief commr., Burma, public works dept., Aug., 1895; sec. to govt., Jan., 1898; ret'd. March, 1900.

RICHARDS, FREDERICK JOHN, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 5th Dec., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

RICHARDS, JAMES DAY (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Dept., India Office).—Apptd. supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893; 2nd class clerk, Nov., 1896.

RICHARDSON, EDWARD, late judge, Civil Court, Mandalay.—After service in various subord. posts, was apptd. extra asst. commr., 2nd Feb., 1865; first judge, civil court, Mandalay, Sept., 1887.

RICHARDSON, JOHN, M.B., M.A., Surgn.-Col., late Med. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. estabt., 27th July, 1859; served with the Bhutan field force (medal and clasp); from April, 1866, was employed under the govt. of the N.W. Prov. as civil asst. surgn., supt. of vaccn., and sanitary commr.; dep. surgn.-gen., Central Prov., May, 1889; inspr.-general of civil hosp., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, March, 1890; in 1892 acted as surgn.-gen. and sanitary commr. with the govt. of India; ret'd., May, 1894; was a member of the Venice Sanitary Conference, 1897; a member of the Indian Famine Commission, 1897; and has been a member of the Army Sanitary Commn. since July, 1896.

RICHARDSON, THOMAS WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Brighton Coll., Cheltenham Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 8th Dec., 1886, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., gen., rev., and statistical depts., March, 1893; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1895; registrar, high court, appellate side, Dec., 1895; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1899.

RICHARDSON, WILLIAM PELHAM, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 8th Oct., 1868, and employed on irrigation works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Sept., 1872; ret'd., May, 1896.

RICHEY, SIR JAMES BELLETT, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1856; arrived, 7th Dec., 1856, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., educ. inspr., asst. rev. commr., offg. under sec. to govt., talukdari settlt. officer, and asst. to the agent and special commr. at Baroda; unr. collr. and mag., Jan., 1879; senr. collr., May, 1880; on special duty at Kharaghola, 1883 and 1884, and at Baroda, 1884; acting commr. of customs, March, 1884; acting member of council, Bombay, March, 1885, and again, Oct., 1886; chief sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts., May, 1885; confd. as member of council, Bombay, Aug., 1887; ret'd., March, 1890; K.C.I.E., May, 1890.

RICKETTS, GEORGE H. M., C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Winchester, and Haileybury; apptd., 1847; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and as asst. to the govr.-gen.'s agent, S.W. frontier; from 1853, served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and dept. commr., and from 1858 in the N.W. Prov. as mag. and collr., and civil and sessions judge; C.B. for service in the Punjab in 1857, and medal for service with the troops; commr., Allahabad divn., 1873; junr. member, board of rev., 1878; ret'd., 1879.

RICKETTS, MONTAGUE POYNTZ, Col., I.S.C., late Central Prov. Commn.—Arrived 22nd March, 1855; served in the Central Prov. from Sept., 1859, as asst. commr.; small cause court judge, Jabalpur, Aug., 1862; dep. commr., May, 1867; addl. commr., Nov., 1883; ret'd., April, 1890.

RIDDELL, ROBERT VANSITTART, Col., R.E.—Joined Addiscombe, Feb., 1858; lieut., royal engrs., 10th June, 1859; arrived, March, 1861; from Aug., 1862, served in the topogr. survey of India as mil. asst.; dep. supt., Oct., 1867; asst. surveyor-gen. Oct., 1876; apptd. mint master, Calcutta, Jan., 1884; mint master and mint engr., Bombay, Aug., 1889; ret'd., March, 1895.

RIDGEWAY, THE RIGHT HON. SIR JOSEPH WEST, G.C.M.G., K.C.B., K.C.S.I., Col. (gouv. of Ceylon).—First commn., Bengal infantry, Dec., 1860; in civil employ from May, 1868, in the Central India and Rajputana agencies; attaché, foreign dept., 1873; asst. agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, 1875; pol. agent, E. states, Rajputana, Aug., 1876; on special pol. duty with the field force in Afghanistan, under Gen. Sir F.
