WEBB, WILLIAM CHARLES, B.L. (presy. mag., Bombay).—Appt. prof. of law, Govt. Law School, Bombay, Nov., 1874; presy. mag., Mar., 1892.

WEBB, WILLIAM HAROLD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).

—Educ. at Worcester Cathedral Sch. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

WEBB, WILLIAM TREGO, M.A., late Educl. Service, Bengal.—Joined the service, 6th April, 1875; prof., Dacca coll., May, 1875; prof., Presidency coll., from Jan., 1878; has publ. 'Hints on the Study of English,' 'Indian Lyrics,' and transls.; ret. d., March, 1892.

WEBSTER, EDMUND FORSTER, C.I.E., B.L., late Madras C.S.—Appt. from Haileybury, 1857, and served in rev. and jud. depts., Madras; small cause court judge, May, 1867; district and sessions judge, June, 1873; collr. and mag. and agent, Tanjore, April, 1878; on special duty in connection with the famine commn., Oct., 1878, and again, Aug., 1881, as sec. to the famine code committee; sec. to govt., rev. dept., June, 1882; member of legisl. council, 1883, 1885, and 1886; chief sec. to govt., Jan., 1884; fellow of Madras univ., 1884; ret. d., Oct., 1887; C.I.E., May, 1888.

WEBSTER, HENRY BINNY, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Appt. from Haileybury, 1854; arrived, 17th June, 1854; served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and asst. settl. officer; mag. and collr., Saharanpur, Nov., 1867, and subsequently at Gorakhpur and Meerut; commr., Jhansi divn., Jan., 1878; inspr.-gen. of police, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, April, 1880; ret. d., 1886.

WEBSTER, JOHN EDWARD, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at the Charterhouse and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; appt. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 26th Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and collr., April, 1897; settl. offr., Dec., 1899; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1900.

WEDDERBURN, HARRY GEORGE, B.L. (admir.-genl., Madras).—Reporter for the Indian law reports (Madras series), June, 1881; law reporter, March, 1888; administrator-gen., March, 1889; acting advocate-gen., 1891-92; member of legisl. council of Fort St. George, 1893-94.

WEDDERBURN, SIR WILLIAM, Bart., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; appt. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 25th Nov., 1860, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., asst. judge, acting jud. asst. to the commr. in Sind, and small cause court judge; acting sec. to govt. in the jud., &c., depts., Dec., 1866, and again in 1870 and 1874; under sec. to govt., jud. and pol. depts., May, 1867; acting registrar, high court, Jan., 1869; acting jud. commr. in Sind and judge of the sadar court, 1874-76; judge and sessions judge, June, 1875; acting judge, high court, Bombay, Jan., 1885; acting chief sec. to govt., Oct., 1886; ret. d., May, 1887; M.P. for Banffshire from 1893 to 1900; member of royal commn. on Indian expenditure, May, 1895.

WEEKES, ARTHUR, M.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Exeter Coll., Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 2nd Feb., 1862; served in the Bengal rev. and jud. depts., and was appt. mag. and collr., 1st grade, Aug., 1885; on famine relief duty at Madras, 1877; ret. d., March, 1888.

WEIDEMANN, GEORGE LEVER, late Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1870, and served in Burma

as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; addl. sessions judge, Oct.-Dec., 1890; acted as commr., 1893-95; addl. sess. judge, Jan., 1896; retd., April, 1897.

WEIGHTMAN, WALTER JAMES, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1881, and posted to State railways; exec. engr., Aug., 1893.

WEIR, CHARLES JAMES, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Royal Acad. Instru., Belfast, and Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 17th Nov., 1879, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. sessions judge, and in Coorg as offg. first asst. commr. and district mag.; acting under sec. to govt. of Madras, rev. dept., Oct., 1886; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., May, 1887; offg. dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, April, 1888; head asst. to collr. and mag., S. Arcot, Aug., 1888; sub-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1891; collr. and mag., Dec., 1894.

WEIR, JOHN WILLIAM ALLEN, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Godolphin Sch., Hammersmith, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 12th Nov., 1884, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and administrator of Sachin state; 1st asst., Aug., 1895.

WEIR, PATRICK ALEXANDER, M.B., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (agency surgn., Baghelkhand).—Joined the service in 1875; offg. 2nd rest. surgn., presidency gen. hosp., Aug., 1876; supt. of vaccn., March, 1882; principal asst. to opium agent, Benares, May, 1882; offg. residency surgn., Nepal, May, 1891; offg. agency surgn. and administrative med. officer, Baluchistan, June, 1894; agency surgn., Baghelkhand, Dec., 1897.

WEIR, THOMAS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 8th Dec., 1867, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; dep. registrar, high court, 1870-72; Tamil transl. to govt., Jan., 1879; head asst., Godavari, March, 1880; registrar, high court, April, 1882; from July, 1883, acted as district and sessions judge, and was placed on special duty in connection with the reorganization of the subord. jud. service, Nov., 1883; sub-sec., board of rev., Feb., 1886; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1887; offg. judge of high court, March, 1890, to Jan., 1891; retd., Oct., 1896; author of 'Law of Offences and Criminal Procedure, as expounded by the High Court of Madras,' and of 'Rules of Practice (Civil) of the Madras High Court.'

WEIR, THOMAS STEPHENSON, Col., Indian Med. Service (princ. medl. offr., Quetta dist.).—First commn., Indian med. dept., 1st April, 1870; health officer, Bombay, from 1874; princ. medl. offr., Quetta dist., April, 1900.

WELD, MATTHEW RICHARD, B.A., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Dublin Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 21st Nov., 1868, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; acted as collr. and mag., Kistna, from April, 1885; district and sessions judge, Ganjam, Sept., 1888; Karnul, May, 1889; retd., Aug., 1893.

WELDON, THOMAS, C.I.E., Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Madras.—Joined Addiscombe, Jan., 1852; ensign, Madras infantry, Dec., 1853; from July, 1860, served as asst.

supt. and supt. of police; senr. mag. of police for town of Madras, March, 1867; chief mag. of police, Madras, April, 1877; chief presidency mag., Dec., 1880; board of examers, Jan., 1882; commr. of police for town of Madras, Dec., 1885; C.I.E., May, 1888; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1891.

WELLDON, Most Rev. JAMES EDWARD COWELL, D.D., Bengal Ecclesi. Dept. (bishop of Calcutta).—Educ. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; head master of Harrow, 1885-1898; apptd. Bishop of Calcutta and Metropolitan of India, Jan., 1899.

WELLS, WALTER FOX WILLIAMSON, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Cheltenham; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 16th Nov., 1875, and served in Oudh as asst. commr.; city mag., Lucknow, Oct., 1885; joint mag., July, 1889; dep. commr., Nov., 1891; district and sessions judge, May, 1894.

WELSH, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Haileybury; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 20th Nov., 1876, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., asst. sessions judge, &c.; head asst. to collr. and mag., Jan., 1885; senr. asst., Dec., 1887; sub-collr. and joint mag., Aug., 1889; collr. and mag., Nov., 1891; district and sessions judge, Cuddapah, Jan., 1894; collr. and mag., S. Kanara, Nov., 1894; dist. and sess. judge, S. Malabar, Aug., 1898.

WENLOCK (BARON), RIGHT HON. BEILBY LAWLEY, G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E.—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; M.P. for Chester, April to July, 1880; was vice-chairman of county council, East Riding; govr. of Madras, 1891 to 1896; G.C.S.I., Jan., 1896.

WEST, SIR RAYMOND, K.C.I.E., M.A., B.L., LL.D., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Galway; apptd. after exam. of 1855; arrived, 18th Sept., 1856, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., asst. judge, and offg. under sec.; registrar, high court, Bombay, June, 1863; jud. commr. and judge of the sadar court in Sind, June, 1872; judge of the high court, Bombay, Sept., 1873; member of the law commission, Calcutta, 1879; vice-chancellor of the univ. of Bombay, Oct., 1886; member of council of govr. of Bombay, 12th Nov., 1887; K.C.I.E., June, 1888; retd., April, 1892.

WESTERN, JAMES HALLIFAX, C.M.G., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., Bengal engrs., 1860; arrived, Oct., 1862; asst. engr., public works dept., N.W. Prov., Dec., 1863; exec. engr., Ganges canal, June, 1868; transf. to Punjab, May, 1882; supt. engr., Sept., 1882; offg. chief engr. and joint sec. for irrign., Punjab, Nov., 1884; services lent to Egyptian govt., retd., Jan., 1887.

WESTLAND, SIR JAMES, K.C.S.I. (member of the Council of India).—Educ. at Marischal Coll., Aberdeen, and Wimbledon Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 10th Oct., 1862; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; offg. junr. sec., govt. of Bengal, July, 1869; offg. supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, June, 1870; under sec., govt. of India, finl. dept., Dec., 1870; acctnt.-gen., Central Prov., Dec., 1873; inspr. of local offices of accts., Nov., 1877; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Oct., 1878; comptr.-gen. and head commr. of state paper currency, Sept., 1881;

in England on special duty, 1884; member of fin. committee, March, 1886; offg. member, govr.-gen.'s council, Aug., 1887; temporary member, Nov., 1887; C.S.I., June, 1888; sec. to govt. of India, dept. of fin. and commerce, Nov., 1888; chief commr. of Assam, July, 1889; retd., Oct., 1889; finl. member of govr.-gen.'s council, 27th Nov., 1893; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1895; member of Council of India, Aug., 1899.

WESTMACOTT, EDWARD VESEY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Rugby, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 8th Jan., 1863; served in the Bengal rev. and jud. depts., and was on famine relief duty, 1874; offg. inspr.-gen. of jails, March, 1884; commr. of excise, Bengal, April, 1889; commr., presidency divn., March, 1893; acting member of rev. board, April-Nov., 1894; retd., Jan., 1898.

WESTON, DONALD, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Stratford-on-Avon, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 26th Dec., 1894, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

WESTROPP, ALEXANDER SLIGO ANDERSON, Indian C.S. (asst. pol. agent, Savanur).—Educ. at Dover Coll., and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 5th Nov., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. pol. agent, Savanur, June, 1898.

WESTROPP, GEORGE RALPH COLLIER, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Bombay infantry, Dec., 1850; arrived, 27th Jan., 1851; was employed under the mil. dept., including service in Khandesh against the rebel leader Tantia Topi, 1858; adjutant, Sawantwari local corps, Feb., 1859; acting asst. mag. in Ratnagiri, May, 1860; asst. to pol. supt., Sawantwari, Nov., 1862; asst. pol. agent, Kolhapur, 1865-69; in charge, S. Mahratia jaghir states, 1869-77; pol. supt., Sawantwari, and commandant of the local corps, Aug., 1877; on special duty in the Savanur state in 1880; retd. from civil employment, 1888.

WHALLEY, PAUL, M.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., offg. junr. sec., and under sec. to govt.; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, dept. of rev., agric., and commerce, Dec., 1871, to Feb., 1872; joint mag. and dep. collr., 1st grade, May, 1877; dep. commr. in Oudh, Dec., 1877; mag. and collr., Pilibhit, Nov., 1879; retd., Feb., 1890; author of 'A Collection of the Laws and Rules of the Non-Regulation Provinces.'

WHEELER, FRANK (supplemental clerk, store dept., India office).—Apptd. supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893.

WHEELER, HENRY, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 14th Dec., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1898; under sec., judl., &c., depts., July, 1898; and ditto gen. rev. and stat. depts., July, 1898.

WHEELER, PERCY COTTERILL, B.L., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 25th Dec., 1877, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and

collr.; asst. settlt. officer, Jaunpur, Nov., 1882; asst. commr., April, 1887; joint mag., Nov., 1888; retd., 1892.

WHISH, CHARLES WILLIAM, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch., and Blackheath Proprietary Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 1st Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and asst. commr.; dep. commr., Feb., 1890; mag. and collr., July, 1890; retd., April, 1898; author of 'The District Officer in India.'

WHITE, SIR CHARLES ARNOLD, Kt., B.L. (chief justice, Madras).—Apptd. advocate-gen., Madras, and addl. member of legisl. council, March, 1898; chief justice, Madras high court, Sept., 1899; Kt., May, 1900.

WHITE, CHARLES ARTHUR, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1881, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., May, 1893.

WHITE, EDMUND, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; dep. supt. of provl. census operations, 1880; also under sec. to govt. in the census dept.; dir. of public instrn., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, June, 1885; member of lieut.-govr.'s legisl. council, Feb., 1889; retd., Aug., 1892; author of 'Report on the Census, N.W. Prov. and Oudh,' 1881.

WHITE, GEORGE GILBERT, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Apptd. from Cooper's Hill Coll.; arrived, 29th Oct., 1878; served in Central Prov. as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Nov., 1886.

WHITE, SIR GEORGE STEWART, G.C.B., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., G.C.V.O., V.C., Gen., late Commander-in-Chief of the Forces in the East Indies (gouv. of Gibraltar).—First commn., 4th Nov., 1853; served in the Indian Mutiny, 1857-59 (medal), and Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal and star, V.C., and C.B.); mil. sec. to the viceroy, 1880-81; took part in the Soudan expdn., 1884-85 (medal and star), and was asst. adjutant and quartermaster-gen. in Egypt, 1885; brigadier-gen., Madras, Sept., 1885, and commanded the brigade detailed for service in Burma; from 1886 to 1889, commanded the Upper Burma field force (K.C.B.); commanded Zohob field force, 1890; commander-in-chief in India, 8th April, 1893-March, 1898; extraordinary member of viceroy's council, 12th April, 1893; G.C.B., June, 1897; quartermaster-gen., War Office, Oct., 1898; lieut. genl. comdg. in Natal, Oct., 1899 to June, 1900; govr. and commr.-in-chief of Gibraltar, July, 1900.

WHITE, HENRY FREDERICK, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; joined the service, 19th Dec., 1859; 1st civil divl. engr., April, 1865; exec., engr., Oct., 1866; in charge of Mhow mil. works divn., 1872, 1878, and 1883; offg. chief engr. and sec. to chief commr., Burma, July, 1885; offg. suptg. engr. and sec. to rest., Haidarabad, Sept., 1887; suptg. engr., May, 1888; suptg. engr. and sec. to agents for Rajputana and Central India, public works dept., June, 1892; retd., June, 1895.

WHITE, HENRY RICHARD, B.A. (2nd class

clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 4th Dec., 1872; B.A., London univ., 1882.

WHITE, HERBERT THIRKELL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (judl. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 2nd Jan., 1878, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and personal asst. to the chief commr.; cantonmt. mag. and small cause court judge, Thayetmyo, April-Sept., 1882, and again in 1883; sec. to the chief commr. for Upper Burma, March, 1886; sec. to the chief commr., April, 1887; chief sec., July, 1890; commr., Dec., 1890; C.I.E., Jan., 1892; offg. jud. commr., Upper Burma, May, 1894; offg. chief sec. to chief commr., July, 1894; jud. commr., Aug., 1898.

WHITE, JOHN CLAUDE, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; joined the service, 17th Nov., 1876, as asst. engr., and was posted to Bengal; temporarily employed on Behar-Assam railway, 1884; exec. engr., Aug., 1887; pol. officer, Sikkim, June, 1889; exec. engr., Sikkim, in addition to other duties, April, 1890.

WHITE, JOHN HUBBARD, Major-Gen., R.E., late Mint-Master, Bombay.—Second lieut., Bombay engrs., Dec., 1853; arrived, Nov., 1855; from June, 1856, served in the Bombay public works dept. as asst. engr. and exec. engr. (for irrign.) from May, 1865, served as dep. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay; acting chief engr., and under sec. to govt. of Bombay, railway branch, June-Dec., 1874; acting mint-master, Bombay, June, 1876; rejoined the railway dept., Feb., 1878; from March, 1879, served as mint-master and mint-engr., Bombay; senr. mint-master from Jan., 1884; ret'd., March, 1890.

WHITE, WALTER HANBURY, Lt.-Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Apptd. asst. engr., Jan., 1874, and posted to state railways; asst. dir., north-eastern system, and asst. sec. to govt. of Bengal, Oct., 1878; served in Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal); exec. engr., Jan., 1880; services lent to S. Indian railway co., Jan., 1891; employed on E. Coast line, 1891; offg. consulting engr. to govt. of India for railways, central divn., April, 1893; and at Calcutta, April, 1894; suptg. engr., March, 1895; chief engr., Jan., 1900.

WHITE, WILLIAM HENRY, Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Joined the service, 23rd June, 1873, as local fund engr., Surat; asst. engr., April, 1875; exec. engr., March, 1882.

WHITEHEAD, JOHN EDWARDS (supplemental clerk, registry and record dept., India Office).—Apptd. supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893.

WHITEHEAD, Right Rev. HENRY, D.D., Madras Eccles. Dept.—Apptd. bishop of Madras, June, 1899.

WHITELEY, JOHN JOSEPH, A.M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1881; exec. engr., Feb., 1894.

WHITEWAY, RICHARD STEPHEN, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Univ. of London and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 10th Dec., 1868, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settl. officer, and joint mag.; settl. officer, Aligarh, Nov., 1881; settl. officer, Ajmir

and Merwara, Jan., 1883; dep. commr., March, 1888; mag. and collr., April, 1888; ret'd., Aug., 1893.

WHITLOCK, CHARLES JAMES TOLLER, Col., I.S.C., late Madras Police Dept.—First commn., 20th Feb., 1858; served in the suppression of the Mutiny (medal); joined the dept., April, 1867, as proby. asst. supt.; confd., Nov., 1868; resumed mil. duty, Aug., 1871; rejoined the dept., April, 1874; supt., Nov., 1881; reverted to mil. duty, April, 1895.

WHITMORE, JOHN, B.L., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 31st Dec., 1868; served in rev. and jud. depts., Bengal, and was apptd. joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1878; offg. jud. commr., Chota Nagpur, Dec., 1881; district and sessions judge, Sept., 1885; ret'd., March, 1895.

WHITTEN, RICHARD JOHN, late N. India Salt Rev. Dept.—Joined the service in Feb., 1862, as asst. patrol; dep. inspr., internal branch, Jan., 1867; personal asst. to commr. of inland customs, Oct., 1869; collr. of customs, Sept., 1871; employed temporarily under govt. of Bombay in salt inquiries in Cutch and Kathiawar; dep. commr. of inland customs, Dec., 1879; at various periods has offd. as commr. of N. India salt rev.; ret'd., Aug., 1898.

WHITTLE, GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 28th Nov., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; joint mag., Dec., 1895.

WHITTY, JOHN TARLTON, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., New Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 29th Dec., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

WHITWORTH, GEORGE CLIFFORD, Indian C.S. (judl. commr., Sind).—Educ. at Sandicroft Sch., Northwich, and Queen's Coll., Liverpool; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 5th Dec., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; acting under sec. to govt. of Bombay in the pol., &c., depts., Jan., 1876; under sec. to govt. in the public works dept. for relief purposes, 1877-78; second asst. collr., Feb., 1881; jud. asst. to the pol. agent, Kathiawar, Nov., 1886; judge and sessions judge, Nov., 1887; acting sec., pol., &c., depts., 1893-94; acting jud. commr., Sind, April, 1897; confd. July, 1898; author of 'The Theory of Relevancy for the purpose of Judicial Evidence,' an 'Anglo-Indian Dictionary,' 1885, the 'Rajkumar Law Lectures,' 1888; 'A Letter to Magistrates,' 1896, and 'Presumptions and Inferences,' 1899.

WICKHAM, WILLIAM ALLEN (supervisor, store dept., India Office).—Apptd. 2nd grade supervisor, India store depot, 23rd Sept., 1889.

WIGHT, JOHN KNOX, B.A., late Indian C.S., Bengal.—Educ. at Queen's Coll. Sch., Trinidad, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 4th Dec., 1872, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine duty, 1874; transfld. to Assam, Dec., 1874, and served as asst. commr. and asst. inspr. of immigrants; dep. commr., March, 1884; returned to duty in Bengal, May, 1890; district and sessions judge, June, 1890; ret'd., July, 1900.

WIGNER, JOHN MURCH, B.A., B.Sc. (senr. clerk, Store Dept., India Office).—Educ. at King's Lynn Gr. Sch., and London Univ.; temporary clerk, 17th July, 1862; junr. clerk, Jan., 1867; senr. clerk, July, 1885.

WILBERFORCE, SAMUEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 17th Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

WILCOX, FREDERICK, late Bengal Police Dept.—Joined the service, 25th May, 1862, as asst. supt. of police; district supt., June, 1873; retd., March, 1895.

WILD, ALBERT EDWARD (conservator, India Forest Dept.).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1869, as asst. conservator, and served in Burma and the Punjab; dep. conservator, Oct., 1876; conservator, Jan., 1892; in charge, central circle, N.W. Prov., April, 1892; dir. of forest sch., Nov., 1893; conservator of forests, Bengal, April, 1894.

WILD, ARTHUR CLEMENT, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Highgate Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; arrived, 24th Nov., 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr.

WILD, CHARLES EDMUND, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at City of London Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 17th Dec., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., July, 1898.

WILKIE, DAVID, M.B., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (statistical offr. to govt. of India, sanitary and med. depts.).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1873, and was employed as civil surgn. and jail supt. at various stations in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; statistical officer to govt. of India in sanitary and med. depts., Nov., 1886.

WILKINS, CECIL ANSELL, late Indian C.S., Bengal.—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 26th Nov., 1868; served in rev. and jud. depts., Bengal; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1884; registrar, high court, March, 1885; district and sessions judge, April, 1886; member of Bengal legisl. council, March, 1894; supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, Dec., 1894; judge, high court, Calcutta, Sept., 1899; compiled revised edns. of the high court's rules and orders, in 1881 and 1883; retd., April, 1900.

WILKINS, THOMAS JAMES HACKETT, Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (dist. surgn. Malabar).—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1873; arrived, 29th April, 1874, and was employed in Madras under the mil. dept. (including service in the Burma campaign) and as acting civil surgn.; district surgn., Anantapur, Sept., 1887; Ganjam, Nov., 1894; Malabar, May, 1895; and superintendent of lunatic asylum, Calicut.

WILKINS, WILLIAM HENRY, Col., I.S.C., late Survey of India.—Joined the Bengal artillery, Dec., 1857; arrived, Jan., 1858, and served with the Cawnpore field force; studied at Thomason Coll., 1861-62; served in the rev. survey from April, 1863, as asst. surveyor; dep. supt., Oct., 1878; supt. (newscale), Aug., 1892; asst. surveyor-gen., Sept., 1892; offg. dep. surveyor-gen. and offg. dir. of Bengal surveys, April, 1895; retd. from the survey dept., Dec.,

1895; placed on unemployed supy. list, Oct., 1898.

WILKINSON, CHARLES ROBERT, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 30th Nov., 1896, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

WILKINSON, FRANCIS HENRY, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Milton Abbas Sch., Blackheath Proprietary Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 10th Nov., 1863; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and acting under sec. to govt.; on special duty as arbitrator of boundary disputes, 1870; sub-collr. and joint mag., Salem, April, 1878; district and sessions judge, S. Malabar, June, 1882; judge of the high court, Madras, Nov., 1887; retd., Feb., 1893.

WILKINSON, THOMAS ORMEROD, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Chudleigh Gr. Sch., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1865, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and offg. registrar of the chief court; jud. asst., Dec., 1883; district judge, 1st grade, Nov., 1884; divl. judge, Jan., 1887; retd., Sept., 1890.

WILKINSON, WALTER HUGH JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Winchester and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 29th Nov., 1897, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

WILLOCOCKS, ALEXANDER JOHN, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (civil surgn., Naina Tal).—Joined the service as asst. surgn., 30th Sept., 1873; served on mil., civil, and jail duty, and was apptd. civil surgn., April, 1877; offg. profir., Calcutta med. coll., 1892; hon. surgn. to viceroy, Jan., 1894; depd. to Dholpur state in 1894; civil surgeon, Agra, Nov., 1894; Naini Tal, March, 1897.

WILLOCOCKS, JOHN, India Public Works Dept. (sec. to govt., Bombay).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the service, Nov., 1879, and posted to state railways; asst. engr., Feb., 1880; exec. engr., Nov., 1891; under sec. to govt., Bombay, Oct., 1897; sec. to govt., July, 1899.

WILLIAM, JAMES HENRY, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept., Nov., 1874, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; asst. engr., March, 1875; exec. engr., July, 1884.

WILLIAMS, ALBERT, LL.M., Indian C.S. (pol. agent, Quetta).—Educ. at Uppingham, Clifton, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 8th Jan., 1886, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and under sec. to govt.; under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., May, 1894; pol. agent, Haidarabad, Nov., 1895; pol. agent, Quetta, May, 1897.

WILLIAMS, SIR EDWARD CHARLES SPARSHOTT, K.C.I.E., Gen., R.E.—Educ. at the Royal Naval Sch., New Cross, and Addiscombe; first commn., 1848; adjutant of engrs., Fort William, and studying machinery in Calcutta mint, 1850; extra asst. sec., mil. board, 1851; supt., iron bridge yard, Calcutta, 1851-52; served with the Pegu expedn., 1852-53 (medal and clasp); supt., Pegu topogr. survey, 1854-56; principal, civil engrg. coll., Calcutta, 1856-60; member of senate, Calcutta univ.; acted

as under sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., during the Mutiny; principal, Thomason Coll., 1861-62; under sec., govt. of India, public works dept., 1863-69; member of Bengal sanitary commn.; in charge of office of inspr.-gen. of forests for one year; acting mint-master and commr. of paper currency, Bombay, 1867; dep. sec., govt. of India, public works dept. (railway branch), 1869-74, and 1877-78; dir. of state railways, 1874-77; C.I.E., Jan., 1878; dep. govt. dir. of Indian railway companies, India office, March, 1880; govt. dir., March, 1892 to Aug., 1897; K.C.I.E., June, 1893.

WILLIAMS, GEORGE ROBERT CARLISLE, B.A., late Indian C.S., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 21st Nov., 1865, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. and joint mag. and dep. collr.; dep. commr., Jhansi, March, 1883; mag. and d. collr., Dec., 1886; commr., Feb., 1894; author of 'An Historical and Statistical Memoir of Dehra Dun,' and 'Gazetteer of Dehra Dun' (part); ret'd., Aug., 1900.

WILLIAMS, HENRY CLISSOLD, Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal).—Educ. at Haileybury; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 25th Oct., 1871, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., on famine duty in Bengal, 1874; from Nov., 1878, served in Assam as asst. commr.; inspr.-gen. of police and jails, &c., Assam, Sept., 1885; returned to Bengal as mag. and collr., Nov., 1891; acting commr., April, 1894; prest. of mining commn., May, 1895; prest. of labour enquiry commn., Dec., 1895; chairman of Calcutta Corporation, April, 1896; commr., Jan., 1898.

WILLIAMS, RALPH KING, late India Public Works Dept.—From Nov., 1869, served in the office of the consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, and was apptd. asst. examr. of guaranteed railway accts. in 1872; served in Madras from Feb., 1873, and was apptd. dep. examr., May, 1876; examr., Indus valley state railway accts., Aug., 1880; transf'd. to Nagpur, March, 1885; examr., guaranteed railway accts., Lahore, April, 1885; examr., N.W. railway accts., Jan., 1886; ret'd., June, 1898.

WILLIAMS, WILLIAM, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the dept. as asst. supt., 16th Jan., 1868; arrived, 29th Feb., 1868; supt., April, 1880; held charge of various divns., including Indore, Bombay, and Nagpur; ret'd., 1890; author of 'Manual of Telegraphy.'

WILLIAMS-WYNN, ARTHUR W., B.A., B.L.—Educ. at Westminster Sch. and Christ Church, Oxford; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1882; apptd. asst. private sec. (unpaid) to the sec. of state (Viscount Cross), Aug., 1886; asst. private sec., Dec., 1889; private sec., June to Aug., 1892.

WILLIAMSON, FREDERICK LENNOX JERVIS, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 5th Jan., 1895, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; J.P., Sept., 1896; transf'd. to Mandalay, Feb., 1899.

WILLIS, JOHN (clerk, funds dept., India office).—Writer, 3rd Nov., 1862; junr. book-keeper, Nov., 1868; asst. book-keeper, Dec., 1881; clerk, Oct., 1892.

WILLOCK, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Trinity Coll.

Dublin, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 12th March, 1870, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sec. to the salt commn., Jan., 1876; from March, 1879, served in the salt dept. as dep. commr., and was also employed in suppressing the disturbances in the Gudum Hills, 1879; collr. and mag., Dec., 1888; collr. and agent, Vizagapatam, July, 1890; acting member of rev. board, July, 1895; collr., Vizianagram Zemindari, Nov., 1897; ret'd., 1898.

WILLOUGHBY, MICHAEL WEEKES, C.S.I., Lieut.-Gen., I.S.C., late Bombay Secretariat.—First commn., Bombay army, June, 1849; acted as sec. to govt. of Bombay in the mil., marine, and ecclesi. depts., from May, 1880, and was confd., Feb., 1881; returned to Europe, 1888.

WILLSON, JAMES, M.A., late Indian Educ. Dept.—Educ. at Ballinasloe Coll., Ranelagh Inst., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; profr., cathedral mission coll., Calcutta, Jan., 1870; asst., geological survey of India, Sept., 1871; profr., Hugli coll., March, 1873; Dacca coll., Jan., 1874; Patna coll., May, 1875; from Jan., 1879, served in Assam as inspr. of schools, and was apptd. dir. of public instrn., Assam, Jan., 1889; inspr. of Sch., Patna, Mar., 1899; has edited Wilson's 'Dynamics'; ret'd., Feb., 1900.

WILMER, JOHN RANDAL, Col., I.S.C., late Survey of India.—Joined the service as lieut., royal artillery 22nd June, 1859; arrived, 27th Oct., 1860; served in the mil. dept., and in Aug., 1869, was apptd. asst. supt., topogr. survey; dep. supt., July, 1882; in charge of Baluchistan survey party, Oct., 1885, to Oct., 1886; supt. (new scale), Aug., 1892; reverted to mil. dept., Jan., 1896.

WILSON, SIR ARTHUR, K.C.I.E. (legal adviser, India office).—Called to the bar (Inner Temple), 11th June, 1862; puisne judge, high court, Calcutta, 1878; vice-chancellor, Calcutta univ., 1880-88; prest. of the Crawford enquiry commn. at Poona, 1888-89; legal adviser and solicitor, India office, 27th April, 1892; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1898.

WILSON, CHARLES ROBERT, M.A. (principal, Patna coll.).—Entered the service, April, 1887, apptd. profr., Dacca coll., May, 1887; apptd. profr. presidency coll., Calcutta, April, 1895; principal, Patna coll., Jan., 1900.

WILSON, DAVID, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Hutcheson's Gr. Sch., Glasgow, Univ. of Glasgow, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 4th Feb., 1886, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Oct., 1894.

WILSON, FREDERICK ALEXANDER, Col., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., as lieut., Bengal artillery, 8th June, 1860; extra A.D.C. to viceroy, Dec., 1869; from Nov., 1870, served under the foreign dept. as guardian and tutor to the Nawab of Jaora, and as pol. asst. in Jaora, in Central India, and at Tehri; tutor to the Maharaja of Mysore, June, 1876; 1st asst. rest., Haidarabad, Oct., 1879; pol. agent, Oct., 1881; agent with the king of Oudh, and supt. of pol. pensions, March, 1882; boundary settl. officer and asst. to pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Jan., 1883; offd. as rest., Nepal, and pol. agent, Bhartpur and Karauli; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Oct., 1886; commr.,

Ajmir, Feb., 1894; rest., Persian Gulf, May, 1894; served in the Bhutan campaign of 1864-65 (medal and clasp), the Hazara campaign of 1868, and the Black Mountain expedn. (clasp); returned to Europe, 1897.

WILSON, FREDERICK JAMES, A.M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Entd. the dept., Sept., 1879, and served as asst. engr. and exec. engr., under sec. to govt., irrigation branch.

WILSON, GEORGE, Commander, R.I.M., Marine Dept. (Port officer, Bombay).—Commander, Indian marine, Aug., 1878; port officer, Bassein, April, 1882; acting asst. dir. of Royal Indian marine, May, 1889; port officer, Rangoon, Sept., 1890; port officer, Bombay and offg. dep. dir. of Royal Indian marine, Aug., 1896.

WILSON, GEORGE FREDERICK, Major, R.E., India Public Works Dept. (manager, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway).—Apptd., May, 1875, and posted to state railways; asst. dir., N.E. system, 1877 and 1879; asst. sec., railway branch of public works dept., Bengal, 1878; exec. engr., May, 1883; served in Soudan expedn. (medal), and was employed on Soudan railway, 1884-85; asst. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., April, 1888; dep. manager, N.W. railway, April, 1891; on special duty in Ceylon, Oct.-Dec., 1895; manager, Oudh and Rohilkhand Railway, Dec., 1898.

WILSON, JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (settl. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Perth Acad. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam., of 1873; arrived, 14th Nov., 1875, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settl. officer; offg. under sec. to govt., 1881 and 1884; offg. senr. sec. to finl. commr. in 1885 and 1886; dep. commr., Sept., 1890; settl. commr., Nov., 1899; author of works on tribal customs in the Gurgaon and Sirsa districts, and of the Sirsa settl. report.

WILSON, JOHN HATTON, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Dept., Bengal.—Proby. asst. engr., 5th March, 1856; served in the public works dept. in the Central Prov., and temporarily in Oudh (1865-67), as asst. engr. and exec. engr., also in mil. works branch, 1879; suptg. engr., and transf. to Bengal, Dec., 1881; ret'd., 1890.

WILSON, ROBERT HENRY, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 2nd Feb., 1862; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and mag. and collr.; offg. under sec. to the govt. of Bengal, Dec., 1870; inspr. of registrn. offices, July, 1872; offg. sec., board of rev., 1877 and 1879; offg. commr., Bardwan divn., Aug., 1884; employed under the public works dept. on the commn. in connection with the Orissa canals, 1884; offg. sec., govt. of Bengal, finl. dept., Feb., 1886; ret'd., Sept., 1887.

WILSON, THOMAS CORBY, Indian C.S. (settl. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 26th Nov., 1883, and served as asst. commr., Central Prov.; asst. settl. officer, June, 1886; settl. officer, May, 1888; transf. to Burma as dep. commr., Feb., 1891; dir. of land records and agric., Aug., 1892; settl. commr., Jan., 1900.

WILSON, WILLIAM, M.A., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Marischal College, Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1859; served in the Madras rev.

and jud. dept., and was apptd. district and sessions judge, Kistna, Feb., 1874, and collr. and mag., June, 1877; dir. of rev. settl. and agric., Nov., 1882; addl. member, board of rev., April, 1883; fellow of Madras univ., 1885; acting sec. to govt. of Madras in the rev. dept., April, 1885; member of legisl. council, Sept., 1885; acting chief sec. to govt. of Madras, Oct., 1885; member of board of rev., April, 1887; 1st member and commr. of land rev. settl., &c., Jan., 1889; ret'd., Oct., 1889.

WILSON, WILLIAM HENRY, Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 22nd Jan., 1857, and was employed on mil. duties till April, 1864, when he was apptd. acting asst. pol. agent, Sadra; from Nov., 1864, served as asst. supt. of police, subord. mag., supt. of railway police, and district supt.; commr. of police, Bombay, July, 1888; reverted to mil. duty, Sept., 1894.

WILSON, WILLIAM HERBERT, Ph.D. (Heidelberg) (profr., presy. coll., Madras).—Served from 4th Feb., 1875, as profr. of physical science, presidency coll.; fellow of Madras univ., 1880; in addn., registrar of Madras univ., March, 1886; profr. of chemistry, presidency coll., Nov., 1892.

WILSON-JONSTON, JOSEPH, B.A., B.L. Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 6th Dec., 1899, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

WILTSHIRE, CHARLES PALMER BLUETT, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the Proprietary Sch., Islington, City of London Sch., King's Coll., London Univ., and Middle Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 8th Nov., 1869, and served as asst. collr. and mag., acting port officer at Karwar, and supt., timber depôt, Kanara; boundary commr. between Kanara and Mysore, May, 1873; on famine duty, Dharwar, in 1876, 1877, and 1878; in charge of Huzur treasury in 1880 and 1881; 1st asst. collr., Sept., 1886; also forest settl. officer, Poona, June, 1891; ret'd., Aug., 1894; transld. the preliminary chapter of Mill's 'Political Economy' into Marathi.

WIMBERLEY, REGINALD JUSTUS, B.L., Col., late I.S.C.—Ensign, 3rd Bengal European regt., 1859; served in the Punjab police force, 1861-67; commissariat dept., March-Aug., 1867; from Aug., 1867, served in Bengal as district supt. of police in command of police force to quell insurrection in the tributary mehals, May, 1868; 1st asst. supt., Port Blair and Nicobars, April, 1872; dep. supt., Dec., 1884; ret'd., Dec., 1887.

WINCHESTER, CHARLES BLAKE, B.L., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1873, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine duty, 1877; 2nd asst. collr., Nov., 1886; 1st asst., March, 1890; junr. collr. and mag., May, 1893; senr. collr., Oct., 1896; ret'd., Dec., 1898.

WINCHESTER, ERNEST CHARLES (2nd class clerk, registry and record dept., India Office).—Writer, India office, 16th Dec., 1872; clerk, 2nd class, Nov., 1878.

WINGATE, SIR ANDREW, K.C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commr., Bombay).—Educ. at Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, Kensington Gr. Sch., Univ. of London, and Univ. of Heidelberg; apptd. after

exam. of 1867; arrived, 8th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; in pol. charge of the Jath state, 1876; addl. sec. in the famine dept., Mysore, Aug., 1877; asst. sec. to famine commn., Aug., 1878; C.I.E., June, 1879; settl. officer in Udaipur state, Aug., 1879; offg. rest., Mewar, and settl. officer, Kashmir state, Feb., 1887; junr. collr. and mag., Kanara, July, 1890; acting collr. of salt rev., Bombay, Oct., 1890; also acting commr. of customs, &c., April, 1891; settl. officer, Udaipur state, 1891-93; senr. collr., Sept., 1893; addl. mem. of legisl. council, July, 1896, and again, Feb., 1898; offd. as commr. in Sind, Dec., 1896-June, 1897; K.C.I.E., Dec., 1898; sec. to govt., plague dept., and plague commr., April, 1900; commr., Aug., 1900; addl. member of legisl. council, Oct., 1900.

WINGATE, JOHN BRUCE, Indian C.S. (sec. to govt., Burma).—Educ. at Old Aberdeen Gymnasium, Aberdeen Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 28th Nov., 1890, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under sec. to chief commr., Sept., 1895; under sec. to govt. of Burma, May, 1897; sec. to govt., Jan., 1900.

WINGFIELD, COMMANDER G. T., R.N. (supt., India store depôt).—Apptd. supt. of the India store depôt, 10th Sept., 1898.

WINMILL, GEORGE, India Public Works Dept. (locomotive supt., Oudh and Rohilkhand railway).—Joined the service as second sub-engr., Indus valley railway, 17th Nov., 1875; transf. to N.W. railway, May, 1888; and to Oudh and Rohilkhand line, Oct., 1888; locomotive supt., Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, Jan., 1889.

WINNY, THOMAS HERBERT, A.R.I.B.A. (surveyor, India Office).—Apptd. asst. to the surveyor and clerk of the works, India office, 17th Aug., 1885; surveyor, 26th April, 1897.

WINTER, EDGAR FRANCIS LATIMER, Indian C.S. (sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Harrow, Trinity Hall, Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 20th Nov., 1884, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., Nov., 1889; dep. supt., family domains of Maharaja of Benares, April, 1893; supt., April, 1894; dep. commr., April, 1896; mag. and collr., Dec., 1896; sec. to govt., Oct., 1898.

WINTERBOTHAM, HENRY MARTIN, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (member of council, Madras).—Educ. at Portland Gr. Sch., and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived 19th Nov., 1869, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., principal asst., and sub-collr. and joint mag., collr. and mag., Malabar, Aug., 1888; Tanjore, Nov., 1893; member of rev. board, Sept., 1895; member of council, Madras, Dec., 1898; C.S.I., May, 1900.

WODEHOUSE, THE HON. ARMINE, C.B.—Asst. private sec. (unpaid) to sec. of state for colonies (Earl of Kimberley), 1880; private sec. (unpaid) to sec. of state for India (Earl of Kimberley), Dec., 1882, to 1885; private sec., Feb. to Aug., 1886, and again, from Aug., 1892; private sec. to sec. of state for foreign affairs (Earl of Kimberley), March, 1894, to July, 1895; C.B., July, 1895.

WOLFE-MURRAY, FRANCIS D'ARCY OSBORNE, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge,

Madras).—Educ. at Harrow, and St. Andrew's Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 19th Dec., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. sessions judge and forest settl. officer, sub-collr. and joint mag., March, 1890; collr. and mag., March, 1893; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1897.

WOLLASTON, ARTHUR NAYLOR, C.I.E. (registrar and supt. of records, India Office).—Educ. at Stockwell Gr. Sch.; entered the India office by open competition as writer, old establ., 3rd March, 1859; junr. clerk, pol. and secret dept., 1860; senr. clerk, Jan., 1873; asst. sec., rev., statistics, and commerce dept., Jan., 1884; in charge of the dept., May to Oct., 1886, and April, 1887, to March, 1888; registrar and supt. of records, July, 1898; in 1877 publ. a transln. of the 'Anvar-i-Suhaili, or Fables of Bidpai'; in 1879 edited Sir Lewis Pelly's miracle play of 'Hasan and Husain'; succeeded Sir F. J. Goldsmid as examr. in Persian at the royal staff coll., Sandhurst, 1880; selected in 1881 by the science and art dept. to superintend the transln. of the oriental inscriptions in the S. Kensington Museum; in 1882 publ. an English-Persian dictionary, and in 1889 a larger work of the same kind; C.I.E., Jan., 1886.

WOLLEY-DOD, F., A.M.I.C.E., F.C.H., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to state railways; services lent to Kashmir state, 1888-89; exec. engr., Oct., 1889; dep. consulting engr. to govt. of India for state railways, Nov., 1892; on special duty with govt. of India, May, 1897; retd., Oct., 1899.

WOMACK, ARTHUR SHAW, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 5th Dec., 1882, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Central Prov., July, 1885, and served as asst. commr., and registrar of the jud. commr.'s court; judge, small cause court, March, 1892; commr., of excise, Feb., 1893; dep. commr., April, 1893; also pol. agent, Chhattisgarh feudatories, May-Nov., 1893.

WOOD, ARTHUR LIDBETTER MARTIN, Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 13th Nov., 1892, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; chief inspr. factories, Feb., 1897; 2nd asst., July, 1897; offg. pol. agent, Jawhar, July, 1899.

WOOD, EDWARD SEPTIMUS, Capt., late Forest Dept., Oudh.—Arrived as lieut., 93rd Sutherland highlanders, 20th Sept., 1857; from Dec., 1859, served in the public works dept. on irrign. works, N.W. Prov.; offd. as conservator of forests, 1864-66; reverted to his regt., Feb., 1866; apptd. exec. engr., March, 1867; from April, 1867, served in the forest dept., Oudh and was apptd. conservator, Oct., 1868; retd., May, 1891.

WOOD, GEORGE PINCKNEY (2nd class clerk, registry and record dept., India Office).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Addiscombe; writer, India Office, 25th Jan., 1869; clerk, 2nd class, Aug., 1878.

WOOD, JOHN BARRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 11th Dec., 1894, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

WOOD, JULIUS JOHN, M.B., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st May, 1868, and served on gen. and jail duty, and as supt. of vaccn. ; on famine relief duty in 1874 ; dep. sanitary commr., Ranchi circle, Sept., 1888 ; ret'd., Feb., 1892.

WOOD, RICHARD BOARDMAN, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Bromsgrove Sch., and Hertford Coll., Oxford ; apptd. after exam. of 1894 ; arrived, 6th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

WOOD, SAMUEL GEORGE, India Public Works Dept. (examinr. of accts.).—Apptd. to the accounts estab't. of the public works dept. as accountant, Sept., 1865, and posted to Central Provinces ; dep. examr., Jan., 1882 ; examr., May, 1887.

WOODBURN, ARCHIBALD FRANCIS, B.L., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Ayr Acad., and Edinburgh Univ. ; apptd. after exam. of 1870 ; arrived, 11th Nov., 1872, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. ; second asst. collr., June, 1885 ; 1st asst., Sept., 1888 ; junr. collr. and mag., Haidarabad (Sind), Feb., 1893 ; collr. and mag. and dist. registrar, March, 1897 ; acting commr., Jan., 1898 ; acting chief sec., plague dept., April, 1899 ; ret'd., Dec., 1900.

WOODBURN, SIR JOHN, K.C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (lieut.-govr. of Bengal).—Educ. at Ayr Acad., Glasgow Univ., and Edinburgh Univ. ; apptd. after exam. of 1862 ; arrived, 12th Dec., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. ; transf'd. to Oudh, Feb., 1865, and served as asst. commr., asst. settlt. officer, and offg. sec. to chief commr. ; dep. commr., Faizabad, Aug., 1879 ; sec. to govt. of the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, in the Oudh rev. dept. and forest branch, Jan., 1882 ; also in the Oudh pol. dept., March, 1882 ; sec. to govt. of the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, jud. dept., May, 1884 ; chief sec., Nov., 1888 ; member of provl. legisl. council, Dec., 1886 ; member of viceroy's legisl. council, Feb., 1891 ; resumed his post as chief sec., April, 1891 ; again member of viceroy's legisl. council, 1891-92, and 1893 ; C.S.I., Jan., 1892 ; chief commr. of the Central Prov., Dec., 1893 ; member of govr.-gen.'s exec. council, Dec., 1895 ; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1897 ; lieut.-govr. of Bengal, April, 1898.

WOODCOCK, ELBOROUGH MARTIN, Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., N.W. Prov.—Arrived as ensign, 9th March, 1856 ; served with the Persian expedit. force, 1856-57 (medal and clasp) ; apptd. to Oudh mil. police, 27th Nov., 1858 ; from March, 1860, served in the dept. as district supt. ; reverted to mil. dept., Jan., 1892.

WOODMAN, HENRY CHARLES, B.A., India C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Durham Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford ; apptd. after exam. of 1896 ; arrived, 18th Jan., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

WOODROFFE, JAMES TISDALL, B.L.—Apptd. advocate-gen., Calcutta, Dec., 1899.

WOODS, ALBERT EDWARD, Capt., I.S.C. (dep. commr., Assam).—Educ. at the Royal Naval Sch., New Cross, and Sandhurst ; entered the army, 22nd Oct., 1881 ; joined the Assam commn., 12th Aug., 1889, as asst. commr. ; dep. commr., Nov., 1893.

WOODWARD, HILLERSDEN, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Oakham Gr. Sch., and

Birmingham Gr. Sch. ; apptd. after exam. of 1869 ; arrived, 28th Oct., 1871, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. ; forest settlt. officer, Sholapur, Nov., 1883 ; 2nd asst. collr., May, 1885 ; 1st asst., July, 1888 ; junr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1892 ; senr. collr., July, 1896 ; ret'd., July, 1898.

WOODWARD, WILLOUGHBY, M.A., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Oakham Gr. Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford ; apptd. after exam. of 1862 ; arrived, 25th Nov., 1863, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. ; junr. collr. and mag. and district registrar, Panch Mahals, and pol. agent, Rewa Kantha, Aug., 1881 ; senr. collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Satara, March, 1886 ; ret'd., March, 1890.

WOOTTON, JOHN JOSEPH (2nd class clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 20th Feb., 1872.

WORDSWORTH, WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon.), late Educl. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the educl. service, May, 1861 ; arrived, 10th July, 1861, and served as head-master of the Elphinstone instn., Bombay, acting prof'r. in the Elphinstone coll., and asst. inspr. of schs. ; from May, 1862, served as principal of the Deccan coll., Poona ; principal and prof'r., Elphinstone coll., Nov., 1874 ; also sheriff of Bombay, Dec., 1885 ; C.I.E., Feb., 1887 ; ret'd., 1890.

WORGAN, JOHN BERNEY, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. ; apptd. after exam. of 1856 ; arrived, 16th Dec., 1856 ; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and mag. and collr. ; from May, 1876, served in the jud. dept., and was apptd. district and sessions judge, 1st grade, July, 1879 ; ret'd., Dec., 1891.

WRAY, JOHN WILLOUGHBY, Lieut.-Colonel, Indian Staff Corps (pol. agent, Cutch).—First commn., 12th Nov., 1870 ; entered pol. employ as asst. pol. agent, Cutch, May 1877 ; acting asst. pol. agent, Kolhapur, Jan., 1885 ; pol. agent, Kolhapur, and southern Mahratta country, April, 1897 ; pol. agent, Cutch, June, 1900.

WRIGHT, FREDERICK CHARLES BUCKLEY (senr. clerk, store dept., India Office).—Junr. clerk, 4th July, 1867 ; senr. clerk, June, 1891.

WRIGHT, HENRY NELSON, Indian C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Eton and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford ; apptd. after exam. of 1888 ; arrived, 29th Oct., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. ; joint mag., Nov., 1895 ; under sec. to govt., April, 1897 to Jan., 1899.

WRIGHT, JAMES MCCALLUM, M.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Fordyce Sch., Univ. of Aberdeen, and Univ. Coll., London ; apptd. after exam. of 1883 ; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. ; joint mag., Nov. 1890 ; asst. commr., April-Oct., 1894 ; dep. commr., Sept., 1896 ; mag. and collr., April, 1898.

WRIGHT, JAMES WALKER, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept. in July, 1859 ; served in the Punjab as asst. and exec. engr. ; transf'd. to the Punjab provl. list, Nov., 1879 ; suptg. engr., July, 1886 ; offg. chief engr., and sec. to chief commr. of Burma, April, 1890 ; offg. chief engr., buildings and roads branch, and joint sec. to govt. of Punjab, Sept., 1890 ; sec. to chief commr. of Assam, June, 1891 ; ret'd., Jan., 1893.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM CHARLES, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. proby. asst. engr., Oudh, 23rd Sept., 1864; confd., 1st April, 1865; exec. engr., March, 1871; lent to Cawnpore municipality, Sept., 1884; services lent to Rampur state, Jan., 1890; suptg. engr., May, 1891; ret'd. 1899.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., late Educ. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Educ. at Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd., 7th Aug., 1867, and served as prof'r., Benares and Muir colls.; principal, Bareilly coll., Dec., 1873; prof'r., Muir coll., Jan., 1877; principal, Benares coll., Oct., 1888; ret'd. July, 1897; has publ'd a handbook of the educn. dept., an account of the Muir coll., and various educ. works.

WROUGHTON, ROBERT CHARLES (conservator, Bombay Forest Dept.).—Joined the service, 18th Oct., 1871; served as asst., and dep. conservator of forests; conservator, Dec., 1895.

WYBROW, GEORGE DIGBY, A.M.I.C.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—Joined the service, 26th July, 1867, and served in Madras as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., Jan., 1892.

WYER, FREDERICK, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Dec., 1861; served in the Bengal rev. and jud. depts., and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1st grade, May, 1884; offd. as commr. in 1883 and 1885; ret'd., May, 1887.

WYER, THOMAS ROWLAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Univ. of Glasgow, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 20th Oct., 1873, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. officer; asst. commr., Nov., 1884; joint mag., March, 1888; settlt. officer in addn., March, 1890; dep. commr., Nov., 1890; and district and sessions judge, Dec., 1891; mag. and collr., May, 1894.

WYLIE, HENRY, C.S.I., Major-Gen., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, Oct., 1861; arrived, 15th Nov., 1861; and took part in the N.W. frontier campaign, 1863, the 2nd Yusufzai expdn. (medal and clasp), the Bhutan campaign, 1865 (clasp), the Abyssinian expdn., 1868 (medal), and the Hazara campaign, 1868 (clasp); on special duty in Baluchistan, with Major R. Sandeman, 1875-77; asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Baluchistan, 1877; served in the Afghan campaign, 1878-80 (medal); C.S.I., Feb., 1881; asst. to the rest. in Mysore, and sec. to chief commr., Coorg, 1882; pol. agent, Bhopawar, Feb., 1885; Jhalawar, April, 1885; Bhopal, March, 1887; rest., Nepal, Oct., 1893; offg. agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan, Feb., 1898; ret'd., April, 1900.

WYLLIE, WILLIAM HUTT CURZON, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. aide-de-camp to the sec. of state for India).—Ensign 106th Foot, Oct., 1866; arrived, Feb., 1867; served in Oudh, 1870-79, as asst. commr.; acted as inspr.-gen. of registrn. in 1875, and as junr. sec. to govt., 1876-77; from Jan., 1879, served under the foreign dept., as cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, asst. commr. in Merwara and Ajmir, and as asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Baluchistan; served in Madras, 1880-81, as mil. sec. to the govr., and acted as private sec.; C.I.E., May, 1881; pol. agent, in charge of ex-Gackwar

of Baroda, Mulhar Rao, 1881; asst. rest., Haidarabad, 1882; asst. commr., Ajmir, Nov., 1883; 1st asst. agent, Rajputana, 1884; addl. pol. agent, Kotah, April, 1885; boundary settlt. officer, Mewar-Marwar border, Nov., 1886; pol. agent, Kotah, Jan., 1889; offg. commr., Ajmir, July, 1891; offg. pol. agent, Jhalawar, in 1891 and 1892; offg. rest., states of Rajputana, May, 1893; rest., Mewar, March, 1894; rest., West Rajputana states, June, 1898; pol. aide-de-camp to the sec. of state, March, 1901.

WYNCH, LIONEL MALING, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 7th Dec., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sec. to land rev. commrs., Feb., 1894; sec. to commrs. of salt, Abkari, and sep. rev., Oct., 1896; sub. collr. and joint mag., Nov., 1897; collr. and mag., Dec., 1899.

WYNDHAM, PERCY, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Giggleswick Gr. Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 13th Dec., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr.; joint mag., Aug., 1893; dep. supt. of family domains of Maharaja of Benares, April, 1894; dep. commr., Sept., 1899; mag. and collr., March, 1900.

WYNNE, SYDENHAM HENRY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Royal Naval Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 21st Oct., 1873, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst. to the collr. and mag., S. Arcot, Oct., 1882; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1887; collr. and mag., Jan., 1890; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1898; ret'd. Aug., 1898.

YATE, CHARLES EDWARD, C.S.I., C.M.G., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (resdt., Mewar).—First commn., 9th Nov., 1867; arrived, 27th Feb., 1868; from Aug., 1871, served under the foreign dept.; on special duty, Sind border, 1872 and 1873; boundary settlt. officer, Mewar-Marwar border, Sept., 1874; and Mewar-Tonk border, Dec., 1875; offd. for various periods as pol. asst., asst. to supt. for suppression of thagi and dakaiti, &c.; supt. of operations for control of Moghias, Sept., 1879; served in Afghanistan, 1880-81 (medal and clasp, and bronze star); subsequently served in the pol. dept. in Bhopal, Central India, and Bhopawar; attaché with Afghan boundary commn., July, 1884; on duty at Herat in connection with the fortifications, 1885; employed on various special services in connection with the Afghan boundary commn.; pol. agent, Thal Chotiali, March, 1890; offg. rev. commr., Baluchistan, Oct., 1892; pol. agent, Zhob, March, 1893; on special duty on Russo-Afghan frontier, April, 1893; acting govr.-gen.'s agent and consul-gen., Khorassan and Seistan, Sept., 1893; confd. 1897; rest., Mewar, June, 1898; offg. resdt., Western States, Rajputana, April, 1899; author of gazetteers of Mewar, Banswara and Partabgarh, 'Northern Afghanistan,' &c.

YELD, HORACE PARR, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—Joined the Indian med. dept., 1874; was employed in the mil. dept. up to Aug., 1884, when he was apptd. prob'r. in the assay dept., Bombay mint; transf'd. to Calcutta mint, Sept., 1884; dep.

assay-master, Bombay mint, May, 1886, and Calcutta mint, Jan., 1888; dep. assay-master, Bombay mint, May, 1892; retd., Nov., 1894.

YEWALL, FRANK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Leys Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 10th Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

YOUNG, FRANK POPHAM, Capt., C.I.E., I.S.C. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Entered the service, Aug., 1884; in mil. employ till Dec., 1888; apptd. to the Punjab commn. as asst. commr., Dec., 1888; dep. commr., April, 1898; C.I.E., June, 1899.

YOUNG, GEORGE EDGAR (2nd class clerk, registry and record dept., India Office).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 22nd May, 1888.

YOUNG, GEORGE GORDON, Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 23rd Oct., 1856; from June, 1858, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and asst. settl. officer; dep. commr., Jan., 1873; commr. and supt., Jan., 1881; offg. 2nd finl. commr., 1888 and 1890; reverted to mil. dept., 1892.

YOUNG, HARRY GAVIN (dep. inspr.-gen., Madras Police).—Entered the service, April, 1874, and served as inspr., asst. supt., and supt. of police; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1895.

YOUNG, JAMES, late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1868, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., July, 1879; retd., 1899.

YOUNG, MATTHEW, Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 25th Nov., 1892, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., May, 1900.

YOUNG, SIR WILLIAM MACKWORTH, K.C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (lieut.-govr. of the Punjab).—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 12th Dec., 1863, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., asst. settl. officer, and offg. sec. to finl. commr.; offg. under sec. to govt., Oct., 1873; settl. sec. to finl. commr., March, 1875; dep. commr., Jan., 1878; offg. supt., Kapurthala state, Feb., 1878; sec. to govt., Punjab, Jan., 1880; commr. and supt., May, 1887; offg. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Jan., 1888; commr., Jalandhar divn., April, 1888; 2nd finl. commr. of the Punjab, April, 1889; 1st finl. commr., Feb., 1893; C.S.I., Jan., 1890; member of viceroy's legisl.

council, June-Nov., 1893; prest. of the Indian hemp drugs commn., Oct., 1893; rest., Mysore, and chief commr. of Coorg, Sept., 1895; lieut.-govr. of the Punjab, March, 1897; K.C.S.I., June, 1897.

YOUNGHUSBAND, ARTHUR DELAVAL, Indian C.S. (commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Rugby, and Clifton Coll.; apptd. to the Bombay civil service after exam. of 1875; arrived, 9th Dec., 1877, and served as asst. collr. and mag., talukdari settl. officer, and asst. pol. agent; acted as private sec. to govr. in 1890; and as administrator, Rajpipla state, in 1888 and 1890-91; transfld. to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Nov., 1891; dep. commr., Jan., 1892; also pol. agent, Chhattisgarh feudatories, Nov., 1893; commr., April, 1897; and acting pol. agent, April, 1897.

YOUNGHUSBAND, FRANCIS EDWARD, C.I.E., Captain, I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk).—First commn., 10th May, 1882; apptd. to the foreign dept., July, 1889; asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, April, 1892; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Oct., 1892; asst. to gov. genl.'s agent, Rajputana, Nov., 1897; pol. agent in Haraoti and Tonk, Sept., 1898.

YOUNGHUSBAND, ROMER EDWARD, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (judl. and gen. sec. to govt., Punjab).—Educ. at Rugby, Clifton Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 14th Dec., 1879, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settl. officer; junr. sec. to finl. commrs., March, 1886; under sec. to govt., Jan., 1888, to April, 1890; offg. junr. sec., May, 1893; dep. commr., Sept., 1894; chief pol. offr., Tochi Field Force, July, 1897; judl. and gen. sec. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1899.

YUSUF, MOHAMMAD, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at the Patna Coll. Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 12th Nov., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. mag. and collr., Jan., 1896; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1899.

ZORAB, JOHN MANOOK, M.B., C.M., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (civil surgn., Cuttack).—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; served in the mil. dept., and as civil surgn., Balasor, March, 1887, Cuttack, March, 1891, Bahaglpur, April, 1896; Cuttack, June, 1897.

CASUALTIES.

DEATHS.

Name. Appointment. Date.
Adams, Lieut.-Col. A., M.D. ... Indian Medical Service, serving under Foreign Department, Government of India 20 May, 1900.
Allen, W. G. ... .. India Public Works Department ... .. 15 Feb., "
Anley, G. A. D'A. ... .. late Public Works Department, Bengal ... 16 Feb., "
Argyll, Duke of, K.G., K.T., &c. ... Secretary of State for India, Dec. 1868 to Feb. 1874 24 April, ;
Arnot, Lieut. W. P. ... .. late Indian Navy ... .. 23 Jan., 1899.
Baden-Powell, B. H. ... .. Bengal Annuitant ... .. 2 Jan., 1901.
Balfour, G. G. ... .. Bengal Annuitant ... .. 2 Feb., 1901
Barrett, Maj. A. L., D.S.O. ... Indian Staff Corps ... .. 7 March, 1900.
Beresford, Colonel Lord W. L. de la P., V.C., K.C.I.E. ... Military Secretary to Viceroy, Dec. 1881 to Dec. 1893 29 Dec., 1900.
Biggs, T. H. ... .. Financial Department, Government of India ... 7 Dec., "
Blunt, Maj.-Gen. C. H., C.B. ... Royal (late Bengal) Artillery, retired ... 15 Aug., "

Brazier, Lieut. E. J. R. B. ... late Indian Navy ... .. 14 March, "
Brett, A. C. ... .. Bengal Annuitant ... .. 3 July, "
Browne, Sir S. J., V.C., G.C.B., K.C.S.I. ... Indian Staff Corps ... .. 14 March, 1901.

Carmichael, C. P., C.S.I. ... .. Bengal Annuitant ... .. 29 Jan., 1900.
Carnegy, Gen. A., C.B. ... .. Bombay Staff Corps ... .. 25 Oct., "
Carter-Campbell, Col. T. T., R.E. ... late Survey of India ... .. 14 Jan., "
Center, Brigade Surgeon Lieut.-Col. W., M.B. ... late Medical Department, Punjab ... .. 16 May, "
Chamberlain, Rev. F. W. ... .. Bengal Ecclesiastical Establishment... .. 28 Sept., "
Chamberlain, W. J. ... .. late India Public Works Department ... 27 Oct., "
Chase, T. A. N. ... .. Madras Annuitant ... .. 4 June, "
Chester, C. ... .. Bengal Annuitant ... .. 3 Jan., "
Cordery, J. G., C.S.I. ... .. Bengal Annuitant ... .. 8 April, "
Davidson, A. ... .. Public Works Department, Bombay... .. 8 Aug., "
Davidson, Major Sir D., K.C.B. ... late Bombay Infantry ... .. 18 May, "
Davis, R. M. ... .. Indian Civil Service (Bengal) Establishment ... 11 April, "
Dickens, Lieut.-Gen. C. H., C.S.I., R.A. ... late India Public Works Department ... .. 21 July, "
Douglass, R. ... .. India Public Works Department ... .. 5 July, "
Duncan, Maj.-Gen. H. T., C.S.I. ... late Madras Staff Corps ... .. 6 Jan., "
Foord, E. B. ... .. Madras Annuitant ... .. 26 March, 1900.
Gaitskill, Maj.-Gen. F.C.B. ... Royal (late Bengal) Artillery, retired ... 9 Feb., 1901.
Grant, A. J. ... .. Indian Civil Service, Bengal Establishment ... 17 July, "
Greene, G. P. ... .. late Madras Revenue Survey Department ... 31 July, 1900.
Grimley, W. H. ... .. Bengal Annuitant ... .. 10 Feb., 1901.
Gunter, A. H. ... .. Indian Civil Service (Bengal) Establishment ... 25 Mar., 1900.
Hardinge, T. von D. ... .. Madras Annuitant ... .. 25 Dec., "
Hawkes, Lieut.-Gen. H. P., C.B. ... Madras Staff Corps ... .. 9 Oct., "
Hayes, A. ... .. late India Public Works Department ... .. 10 Sept., "
CASUALTIES.DEATHS—continued.
Name. Appointment. Date.
Hellard, Commander S.B. ... late Indian Navy... 25 March, 1899.
Higgins, Col. A., C.I.E. ... ... ...
Hoggan, Maj.-Gen. J. W., C.B. ... late Bengal Staff Corps... 23 Nov., 1900.
Hope, Maj.-Gen. H. R., C.B. ... late Madras Cavalry ... 11 May, "
Howard, Rev. A. ... Bengal Ecclesiastical Establishment... 3 May, "
Hunter, Sir W. W., K.C.S.I., C.I.E. ... Bengal Annuitant ... 7 Feb., "
Inverarity, G. ... Bombay Annuitant ... 5 Dec., "
Johnston, W. ... Bengal Annuitant ... 7 Feb., 1901.
Keelan, Surg.-Maj. B. C. ... late Bombay Medical Department ... 28 Dec., 1900.
Keess, Deputy Surg.-Gen. J., M.D. ... late Madras Medical Department ... 29 Dec., "
Keith, T. W. ... late Accountant General, India Office ... 12 Aug. "
Kelly, Engineer G. ... late Royal Indian Marine ... 23 Aug., "
Kernan, J. I. ... late Judicial Department, Madras ... 31 Oct., "

K.C.S.I. ...		 Bengal Infantry, Commander-in-Chief in
India ...		 18 March, "
Long, J. S. L. ... Bengal Public Works Department ... 16 Dec., "
Lushington, F. ... Madras Annuitant ... 7 Feb., 1901.
Lyde, Lieut.-Col. M. T. ... Bombay Political Department... 11 July, 1900.
MacCartie, C. F., C.I.E. ... Madras Annuitant ... 10 March, "
Macdonald, Gen. J. A. M., C.B. ... Bombay Staff Corps ... 26 April, "
MacEwen, R. S. T. ... late Bengal Judicial Department ... 10 June, "
Money, Sir A., K.C.M.G., C.B. ... Bengal Annuitant ... 8 April, "
Moriarty, A. S. ... Indian Civil Service, Bombay Establishment ... 19 April, "
Morris, G. L. ... Madras Annuitant ... 4 Feb., "
Newton, H. F. ... Bombay Annuitant ... 20 Mar., "
Nugent, J., C.S.I. ... Indian Civil Service, Bombay Establishment ... 5 Aug., "
Orr, J. W. ... late Bombay Judicial Department ... 25 Dec., 1899.
Ozanne, Rev. J. D. ... Bombay Ecclesiastical Establishment ... 26 June, 1900.
Painter, E. D. ... India Audit Office ... 20 Feb., "
Parker, Sir G. A. ... Madras Annuitant ... 6 June, "
Passannah, H. G. ... India Public Works Department ... 19 Aug., "
Perkins, Rev. H. E. ... Bengal Annuitant ... 5 Sept., "
Phillips, Col. A. N. ... late Judicial Department, N.W. Provs. and
Oudh ...		 22 June, "
Plowden, Maj.-Gen. G. W. C. ... late Judicial Department, N.W. Provs. and
Oudh ...		 12 Feb., "
Power, J. ... Bengal Annuitant ... 3 Oct., "
Prinsep, E. A. ... Bengal Annuitant ... 30 Mar., "
Richardson, Maj.-Gen. J. F., C.B. ... ... 17 May, "
Sargent, Sir C. ... late Bombay Judicial Department ... 21 June, "
Sconce, A. ... Bengal Annuitant ... 3 Oct., "
Sinclair, W. F. ... Bombay Annuitant ... 15 May, "
Skipwith, Col. G. T., R.E. ... late India Public Works Department ... 12 Aug., "
Smith, G. L. ... Bengal Annuitant ... 18 Jan., 1901.
Smith, Maj.-Gen. Sir R. M.,
K.C.M.G., R.E. ...		 late Indo-European Telegraph Department ... 3 July, 1900.
Snell, R. H. ... Bengal Annuitant ... 7 Dec., 1899.
Somers-Eve, J. F. ... Madras Public Works Department ... 15 Aug., 1900.
Stent, W. K. ... late India Public Works Department ... 7 May, "
Stewart, Field-Marshal Sir D. M.,
Bart., G.C.B., C.S.I., C.I.E., S.C. ...		 Member of Council of Secretary of State ... 26 Mar., "
DEATHS—continued.
Name. Appointment. Date.
Symes, Sir E. S., K.C.I.E. ... Indian Civil Service, Bengal Establishment 10 Jan., 1901.
Thompson, A. ... Indian Civil Service, Madras Establishment 1 Sept., 1900.
Thornhill, M.B. ... Bengal Annuitant ... 14 Mar., "
Thyne, Commander W. K. ... late Royal Indian Marine ... 24 July, "
Townsend, Surgn.-Gen. S. C., C.B. late Bengal Medical Service ... 9 Feb., 1901.
Tucker, W. T. ... Bengal Annuitant ... 30 Jan., "
Tulloh, Maj.-Gen. J. S. ... Royal (late Bengal) Artillery, retired 15 Feb., "
Turner, Maj.-Gen. S. C., R.E. ... Director-General of Military Works in India 30 Dec., 1900.
Warden, Lt.-Col. C. J. H., M.D.,
I.M.S.		 Examiner of Medical Stores, India Store
Depôt		 18 July, "
Waterfield, Maj.-Gen. H. G., C.B. Indian Staff Corps ... 7 Jan., 1901.
Whiteside, W. S. ... Madras Annuitant ... 12 Oct., "
Wilson, W. J. ... late Indian Public Works Department ... 13 Aug., "
Wright, F. N. ... Bengal Annuitant ... 25 July, "
RETIREMENTS.
Adams, J. ... Bombay Public Works Department ... 17 Sept., 1900.
Agnew, Sir W. F. ... Burma Judicial Department ... 16 April, "
Anderson, J. D. ... Indian Civil Service, Bengal Establishment 17 Aug., "
Bayly, J. D. ... Burma Judicial Department ... 20 April, "
Belcham, G. P. ... Survey of India ... 15 Feb., "
Beresford, J. S., C.I.E. ... India Public Works Department ... 11 Feb., "
Bewley, A. ... India Public Works Department ... 1 May, "
Blennerhasset, W. ... Indian Civil Service, Bengal Establishment 1 Nov., "
Boyd, J. E. E. A. ... Madras Police Department ... 5 Nov., "
Burke, C. T. ... Bombay Public Works Department ... 27 Sept., "
Campbell, Sir J. McN., K.C.I.E. ... Indian Civil Service, Bombay Establishment 28 April, "
Cherry, J. W. ... Madras Forest Department ... 27 July, "
Chichele-Plowden, Sir T. J., K.C.S.I. Indian Civil Service, Bengal Establishment 1 Sept., "
Christie, A. D. L. ... Survey of India Department ... 29 Sept., "
Connan, W. ... India Public Works Department ... 14 Feb., 1901.
Crozier, R. G. ... Store Department, India Office ... 24 June, 1900.
Cunningham, Sir W. J., K.C.S.I. ... Indian Civil Service, Bombay Establishment 3 Jan., 1901.
Deas, J. ... Indian Civil Service, Bengal Establishment 10 March, 1900
Dease, P. P. ... India Public Works Department ... 25 Dec., 1899.
Douglas, C. G. ... Madras Forest Department ... 4 May, 1900.
Duncan, P. ... India Public Works Department ... 8 Oct., "
Ebden, E. J. ... Indian Civil Service, Bombay Establishment 18 Feb., "
Edgell, W. ... India Store Depôt ... 4 Aug., "
Feret, C. J. ... Store Department, India Office ... 3 Oct., "
Forrest, G. W., C.I.E. ... Home Department, Government of India ... 14 Jan., 1901.
Fraser, E. G. ... India Public Works Department ... 14 Jan., 1901.
Gatherer, A. B. ... India Public Works Department ... 9 Aug., 1900.
Gillman, A. J. ... Bengal Pilot Service ... 17 Aug., "
Gray, Lieut.-Col. M. A., S.C. Assam Commission ... 6 April, 1901.
Grèy, Lieut.-Col. W. F. H., S.C. Burma Commission ... 24 March, 1901.
Handley, F. F., C.I.E. ... Indian Civil Service, Bengal Establishment 14 April, 1900.
Hanlon, J. W. ... Bengal Jail Department ... 22 May, "
Hannan, J. ... Madras Public Works Department ... 16 Dec., "
